Reference your memo No. 2 (1)-H (SOII)/Sp1-67
dated 5th January, 1968 on the subject noted above.
In the middle of December, 1967 it was rumoured that a group of the National Awami Party of Peshawar District, hereinafter to be referred to as the Splinter Group, headed by Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkal intended to revive the old "KHUDAI KHIDMATGAR MOVEMENT. On the 24th December, 1967 this group met in the shop of Hakim Mohammed Aslam Sanjari in Mohallah Dhaki Nalbandi Peshawar City. The prominent workers present in this meeting were Hakim Mohammed Aslam Sanjari, Kiramat Shah Faulad, Amir Mohammed, Ashiq Hussain Kakezai, Abdul Majid Kabari, Mian Abdul Rauf and Younas Qureshi, all of Peshawar City. In this meeting the office of "Khudai Khidmatgar Movement" was formly inaugurated. Addressing the meeting Hakim Mohammed Aslam Sanjari recounted the services rendered by the "Khudai Khidmatgar Movement' in the struggle for achievement of independence. He said that it was because of this movement that the Britishers were ousted from the country. He appealed to the old "Khudai Khidmatgars" to join them in their struggle for ameliorating the lot of the common man.
"1
The opposite group on the other hand in a meeting held at Green Hotel Peshawar Saddar on the 25th December, 1967 under the Presidentship of Haji Abdullah Vice Fresident Peshawar City National Awami Farty termed the action of the splinter group as a step to create rift in the party which according to them, was being done at the instance of the Speci!! Government. Mohammed Saddiq Bazaz of Peshawar City addressing HOME PRIMET the meeting charged the splinter group for weakening tack the National Awami Party by forming a new party and he e therefore exhorted the workers not to be misled by them and remain united. Mohanded Ajmal of Akora speaking in the meeting charged the splinter group of maligning Abdul Ghaffar Khan by forming a new party within the National Awani Farty under the label of "Khudai Khidmatgar". He opposed such a move by the disruption- ists and exhorted the workers to sink all differences. He also demanded the expulsion of such elementa from the party. Wali Khan son of Abdul Ghaffar Khan, also urged the workers not Abdul to side with the disruptioniste. Mohammed Afzal General

1968_p015.text:
*****
eating.
Becretary Barked Rational AwN] Forty alan participated in the Similar meetings of the Mei-*] Red Forty ware kel in Nowshers, Kardan, Fashower City and Cans in which the udience were requested not to participate in the forthcoming meating of the Splinter Group which has been scheduled for the 12th January, 1968 in Peshawar City.
The splinter group again 1-4 on tas 2nd January, 1968 in their party office in Peshawar City to exe Kid Greetings. In the course of discussion they rebutted the allegations of National Awami Party Canesan Group) that the "Rutger Kovement had been revived at the instance of Government. They leballed Mohammed Afsal Bangash as communist and person least interested in the welfare of the poor.
The splinter group however held its meeting on the 12th of January, 1968 from 1100 to 1145 hours at the old Municipal Ball Peshawar City. Addressing the meeting Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Teakal Bala threw light on the "hudai Kaidastear Movement" and said that the Movement was originally started by Abdul Ghaffar Khan and Haji Sahib of Turangzei at the time when He said there was no other political organisation in the Illage. that it was because of that struggle that Britishers had left the country. He added that the "Kaudai Khidmatgar Movement was being revived mainly with the object to restore old 3.W.P.P. and strengthen friendly relations with Afghanistan. He remarked that Abdul Ghaffar Khan would return to the mother land as soon as the Pathans were restored their rights. He disclosed that they would organise the Movement on the old footing and would spread it even to the tribal areas. Continuing he said that though the Britishers had gone; yet the Pathans had not been liberated. He decried One Unit in the usual way and said that even "CHAPLIES" for the police force and Mats for the schools were being purchased from Lahore although better stuff was available locally. He opposed Communism and said that because of communistic policies of the National Awami Party they had decided to quit it.
In this meeting resolutions demanding the disintez- ration of One Unit, the release of political prisoners including Abdul Samad Khan Achakzai, G.M. Syed and Shahzada Abdul Karim and repeal of repressive laws like Ghoonda Ordinance, Defence of Pakistan Rules, Pakistan Security Act and other so called black laws, end of the state of emergency and restoration of fundamental rights including direct system of election based on adult franchise were also adopted. Slogans of One Unit Barbad, Abdul Ghaffar Khan Zindabad were raised at the meeting.
It appears that the rift between the two groups has come to stay. The splinter group led by Aslam Sanjari has permanently severed its identity from the National Awami Party and has been successful in giving a good start to the new Movement under the old familiar name of "Khudai Midnatgar". The splinter group has been lucky in winning over the services of an ardent

1968_p016.text:
worker like Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkal who is also a close friend and confident of Abdul Ghaffar Khan, The ramification of the split has already started reaching the other parts of the N.W.F.F. and it appears that quite a few of the older generation of the redshirts would cross the floor to the side of Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan. The Afzal Bangash faction is getting active support from Abdul Wali Khan but they have so far been unsuccessful in stemming the first tide of the emergence of "Khudai Khidmatgar Party." However they are cease- lessly trying to tarnish the image of the new party by dubbing it as the tool of foreign agents etc.
It is perhaps true that the emergence of the new party was a culmination of private disputes and political rivalary between the individuals such as Afzal Bangash and Mir Mehdi Shah on the one side and Aslam Sanjari etc on the other; but it cannot be denied that the new party will have a greater pro-Fukhtoonistan and anti leftist stance. Their party manifesto will have pronounced parochial objectives and their propensity towards Afghanistan Movement is bound to be un- equivocal. The new Khudai Khidmatgar Movement is therefore being watched more carefully.
(Sher Afzal Khan) SQA, CSP, Commissioner, Peshawar Division, Peshawar.
No.
/AP
Copy forwarded to Military Secretary to the
Governor of West Pakistan, Lahore for information.

1968_p020.text:
D. O 756 PB(A)
3/548/68
23-1-48
SECRET.
OFFICE OF THE
SUPERINTENDENT OF POLICE
SPECIAL BRANCH
Dated Peshawar, the
NORTHERN REGION
7561115 ott: 18.1/68
Dear
Agha Jabile,
21. Cand
E
19/1
19.1.68
The Khudai Khidmatgar movement was started
(1928/29) by Abdul Ghaffar Khan ostensively as a Salvation Army for religious, moral and social uplift of the Pathans. According 12 to a note written by Mr.Matcalph, Deputy Commissioner, Peshawar, on 30.5.1930, " It is true that this is the outward cloak under which the activities of the society are 11sguised and colour has been given to this theory by the name 'Khuda-1-Kh11matgaran' and by the insistence upon non-violence as the creed of the true volunteer. It is also admitted that a large number of recruits to the movement are simple rustics, who believe that the movement is in the main religious and that the Government are attempting to prevent the Pathan from working out his own salvation. There is, however, ample evidence to show that the organizers of the movement have had from the very start different intentions as to the ultimate objects of the Association and it is in my opinion most dangerous to assume that the real leaders have any intention of making any permanent peace with Government. Their aim is to overwhelm and eradicate the present Government altogether within the next few weeks and to establish an entirely independent Pathan State possibly under the suzerainty of the Amir of Afghanistan. If they fail in that, they will only make terms on such conditions as will enable them to renew the struggle at the earliest possible moment. Any negotiations therefore, with those leaders can only result in giving them further time and opportunities for pushing forward their revolutionary plans".
The assessment contained in the above remarks of an Englishman was by and large correct then as it is now. A.G.Khan's association with Mr. Gandhi at the later stage caused a drift in his outward political policy and for a while he

1968_p021.text:
forgot about the establishment of an entirely Independent Pathan State possibly under the suzerainty of the Amir of Afghanistan. However the inception of Pakistan and the consequent frustration of Abdul Ghaffar Khan who 11ke his Hindu friends could not reconcile to a divided India caused a relapse of his views and he once again raised the slogan of an Independent Pathan State. The following instructions which he gave to his followers at the time of Indepentence are worth noting:--
"Khudai Khidmatgars of the Province are asking me as to what they should do on 15th of August, and whether they should participate in the celebrations. The fact is that to us this day is of great rejoicing, as it represents the result of our sacrifices during the last 18 years on behalf of India. But the nation should know that all our efforts were directed for the freedom of the country and Pathans. To-day we see that whole of the country has achieved freedom, but the freedom of Pathans is still in danger. Therefore I advise all Khudai Khidmatgars not to take part in the celebrations, but at the same time, not to interfere with the rejoicing of others.
Every Khudai Khidmatgar should work patiently and determinedly and bear in mind that no power in the world could rule a nation against their will. That which we say will take place".
This was followed in due course by the open hostility of Afghanistan (as demonstrated at the United Nations) to Pakistan and their unqualified support to A.G.Khan's Pakhtoonistan slogan. However it proved a misfortune in disguise for A.G.Khan and his luck continued running out so much so that his frustration compelled him to leave the country four years ago on a sojourn abroad on the plea of seeking medical advice. But soon after 1.e. on 12.12.1964 his itinerary brought him to Afghanistan where he has since been lodged in a self-imposed exile. He has all this time kept close liaison with his old political comrades and the slogan for an Independent Pathan State by A.G.Khan and his party though at times purposely ambiguous was never lacking in
vehemence.
The practical support of the Afghan Government to

1968_p022.text:
3whose ultimate political goal the slogans of A.4.Khan and his party were best suited was never wanting. Similarly the Indian Government who have always looked upon A.G.Khan as their best friend never lost interest in his political manoeuvres. The fate of the Presidential candidate of the C.0.P. (of which the Sarhad N.A.P. were a part) plunged the opposition of the country into utter confusion and thus the frustration of the local N.A.P. was further intensified. The self imposed exile of A.G.Khan followed by petty squables amongst the party workers started a chain of misadventures for the Sarhad N.A.P. culminating in an unbridgable gulf. As a result, a splinter group hitherto led by a dubious person like Aslam Sanjari came into existence, which ultimately severed its identity under the familiar and appealing name of Khudai Khidmagar.
It is a strange coincidence that Abdul Ghaffar Khan himself should start a similar movement under the same magic word Krin
across the Durand where only a year ago he initiated some Pakhtoonistani Maliks into the movement and with his usual stance for the dramatic the first person to be formally converted was a woman by the name of Jahan Khaista. Repeating his old tactics A.G.Khan gave out that the object of his Khudai Khidmagar movement (in the Afghanistan) was the religious, moral and social uplift of the Pathans. But at the same time he never tired of pleading the cause of the Pakhtoons for an independent state. Here, likewise, the splinter group (reviving the old Khudai Khidmagar movement) published a poster in which the services of the old Khudai Khidmatgars were eulogised and emphasis was made on the unity and social and moral uplift of the pathans and "the emancipation of the Pathans from the tyranny of the One Unit". Some of the protagonists of the new Khudai Khidmatgar movement particularly Aslam Sanjri are known to have been paid Afghan agents. It is also worth noting that restoration of provincial autonomy to the former N.W.F.P. and closer friendship with Afghanistan have been offered as the un-concealed political objectives of the new Khudai Khidmagar movement. There is no doubt that the Sarhad N.A.P. was always primarily the old redshirt organisation predominently composed of old associate of A.G.Khan.

1968_p023.text:
and their merger with the N.A.P. was only a matter of political expediency so that if they could finda way of escape from the orders banning the redshirt organisation, they would immeiiately turn their backs on it and come to the old focal point of Khuda! Khidmatgar. In this, the splinter group who seem to be under the Impression that the Khulai Khidmatgars were never banned have stolen march over others and secured themselves the enviable position of pioneers in reviving a once extremely popular move- ment. Whatever the re-action of A.G.Khan himself to the split in his party, the Afghan Government is apt to welcome the advent of a party which should be dedicated exclusively to the political future of the Pathans and would not form a small part of a bigger whole (such as Redshirts in the All Pakistan N.A.P.). Similarly the Americans who were always chagrined over the leftist tendency of the N.A.P. would not hesitate to contribute to the re-emergence of the Pathan Khudai Khidmatgar movement which they could use as a double edged weapon both to woo Afghanistan and black mail Pakistan as and when necessary. The Americans are said to have dropped hints now and then that if the Sarhad N.A.P. could be weaned away from leftist hands they might consider active help to the Pakhtoonistan movement. (Perhaps by dropping these hints they may also be wanting to make amends for their refusal to give a visa to A.G.Khan to visit the U.S. which fact was generally used against the Americans by leftist elements in the higher echelon of the Sarhad N.A.P.) In view of the imminent interest both of the Americans and the Afghans not to speak of the old man of the moutains
the revival of the Khudai
Khidmatgar movement would need careful watching.
Abdul Wali Khan informs me that the splinter group is presently misusing the name of his father and the Khudai Khidmat- gar movement. He told me categorically that his father had given no signal for the revival of the movement and alleged that the splinter group consists of Foreign Agents (except Arbab ghafoor who, he said, is a fanatic, mad man)who are taking cues from the foreign masters. He also hinted that they suspected Government hands pulling the wires from behind the scene in order to under mine the N.A.P., otherwise how could a shady character like Sanjari revive a party which is banned. And soon he made a

1968_p024.text:
-516
forthright confession that if it is proved to them by the conduct of the renegades that the Khudai Khidmatgar is not an 11legal party they would jump in enbloc and elbow out the Sanjari's etc. and seize the party command. And nothing he said wou'd be nearer their heart. In the meantime he said Afzal Bangash will be relieved of his higher responsibilities to assuage the fears of the anti leftist members and he was confident that the splinter group will be out manoeuvred and most of their members would return to the N.A.P. Whether his forecast has a chance to come true is doubtful and it is also unlikely that the splinter group will disintegrate inspite of a fanatic worker like Abdul Ghafoor. But the remark of Wali Khan that they would join the Khudai Khidmatgar enbloc and seize its reins is pregnant with meaning. If the party is revived with its highly parochial but popular manifesto the Government may have qualms about its delayed action. Perhaps it will be necessary to decide soon whether the Khudai Khidmatgar is not an illegal political party.
With kindest regards.
Agha Mohammad Ali Khan,
SK., QPM.,PPm., PSP.,
Yours sincerely,
Sen mene
(ARBAB HIDAYATULLAH) PSP
Deputy Inspector-General of Police,
Special Branch, West Pakistan,
LAHORE.
Enclosures:-
1. Civil Secretariat Old NWFP Notification No.37044 dated 15.9.1948.
2. Two big posters.
3. Note on Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan. 4. Note on Santri.

In the middle of December, 1967 it was rumoured group of the National Awal Party of Peshawar District, hereinafter to be referred to as the Splinter Group, headed by Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkel intended to revive the old *KRUDAI KHIDMATGAR MOVEMENT. On the 24th December, 1967 this group net in the shop of Hakim Mohammed Aslam Sanjari in
he-m. 326-9718-66 Mohallah Dhoki Halbandi Peshawar City. The prominent workers present in this meeting were Makin Mohammed Aslan Sanjari, Firmast Shah Paulsd, Amir Mohammed, Ashiq Hussain Kakezai, Abdul Wajid Kabari, Mian Abdul Rauf and Younas Qureshi, all of Peshawar City. In this meeting the office of "Khudai Khidmatgar Movement was formly inaugurated. Addressing the meating Hakim Mohammed Aslan Sanjari recounted the services rendered by the "Ehudai Khidmatger Movement" in the struggle for achievement of independence. He said that it was because of this movement that the Britishers were ousted from the country. He appealed to the old "Khudai Khidmstgers" to join them in their struggle for ameliorating the lot of the common man.
The opposite group on the other hand in a meeting held at Green Hotel Feshawar Sadder on the 25th December, 1967 under the Presidentship of Haji Abdullah Vice President Peshawar City Netionel Awami Party termed the action of the splinter group as a step to creste rift in the party which according to thes, was being done at the instance of the
3111/68 Government. Mohammed Saddiq Bazaz of Feshawar City addressing
the meeting charged the splinter group for weakening
the National Awesi Party by forming a new party and he therefor exhorted the workers not to be misled by them and reusin united. Mohammed Ajmal of Akors speaking in the meeting charged the splinter group of maligning Abdul Ghaffar Khan by forming a new party within the National Awasi Party under the label of "Eaudai Kailaster". He opposed such a move by the disruption- ists and exhorted the workers to sink all differences. He also demanded the expulsion of such elements from the party. Abdul @sli Kasn son of Abdul Ghaffar Khan also urged the workers not to side with the disruptionists. Mohammed Afzal General

Addressing the meting, Arbab Abdul Ghafoor of Tehkal regretted disunity among the ranks of Pakhtoons and recounted the sacrifices offered by Abdul Ghaffar Khan in the struggle for independence. In this connection, he also spoke on the reformation campaign launched by Abdul Ghaffar Khan during the Martial Laws a result of which he was sent behind the bars. Explaining the alleged disadvantages of One-Unit, he called upon the Pathans to unanimously raise their voice for its disintegration. He remarked that Abdul Ghaffar Khan would return to Pakistan soon after the disintegration of One-Unit and the relations between Pakistan and Afghanistan would improve. 1968_p041.text:
The Sarbad National Avant Party, which has already been suffering from lack of cohesion, has ultimately given way to the after-effects of mumerous splite at the centre. As a result, it now stands divided into three splinter groups and one main group.
The main group which commande majority of following and enjoys the support of the old guards of the red shirt move- ment is led by Abdul Wali Khan who guides the destiny of the party without accepting any office. The chairs are left to Arbab Sikandar (President) and Ajmal Khattak (Secretary), the latter taking the brunt of sweat and toil of party organisation but Abdul Wali Khan whose stage appearances are few and far between remains the de facto leader. This group also enjoya the good wishes of Abdul Ghaffar Khan and can safely be re- garded as the only true successor of the Red-shirt, Khudai hidmatgar and N.A.P. one after the other. This also is the NAP group towards which the P.D.. should ultimately be looking for support from the Northern Region. However, they have so far parried the attempts of the P.D.M. for an affiliation which largely is due to fundamental differences over the ature of the 'ONS-UNIT. Right now the leadership of this group is toying with the remote idea of uniting the Pathans under Abdul Wali Khan or Abdus Samad Achakzai and claining for him the leadership of the parallel N.A.P. of Pakistan. The group is anti-Bhashani, but remains undecided about its affiliation either with Mahmoodul Haq Usmani or Mahmood Ali Qasoori who are both anti-Bhashani. They feel that while Mahmoodul Haq Uscani is under the influence of pro-Russian elements of Sind, Mr. Qasoori is rabidly against "Bengaliat" and Pakhtoonistan and strongly pre-disposed to the P.D.M. The choice, therefore, for the Wali Khan group is difficult to make. However, if their efforts to claim the leadership of the anti-Bhashani N.A.P. for Wali Khan himself or Achakzai meet with failure, the chances are that this group will ge to Mr. Qasoori. One of the main reasons for chosing Qasoori would be the suspected secret paet between Prof.Muzaffar and Usmani umder which the former will be declared as the leader of the rival M.A.P.
JORDAL KUNATOAR SHURA
This splinter came into existence more as a result of clash of personalities than ideological differences. The party was formed by a person no less than Arbab Ghafoor whe has been a close friend of Abdul Ghaffar Khan, the name of the party has prestigeous value and the declared objectives of the party; namely, revival of the old N.W.P.P. as an autonomous province and friendly relations with Afghanistan

1968_p042.text:
ete, carry mass appeal and therefore wali Khan and his friends apprehended solid chunks to walk over to the Khudai Khidmatgar. They were well-advised to lese no time and to resort to all possible ways and means to neutralise the party. Delegations were sent to Arbab chafoor on "Namati", admonishing letters were obtained from Abdul Ghaffar Khan and internal changes in the party high command were made to please Arbab Ghafoor and Aslam Sanjari. Now, it appears that Abdul Ghafoor Khan has given an undertaking that the Khudai Khidmatgar Shura will remain inactive to leave the field open to Wali Khan group. This group has nothing to do with pro or anti-Bhashani polities and the dis-integration of the One-Unit as a pre- condition of A.G. Khan's return remains its sole political objective.
The third and fourth are both pro-Bhashani groups and while one is inclined to the Ishaq faction, the other is dis- posed to the Aslam faction. Afzal Bangash who was the General- Secretary of the N.A.P. till recently was suddenly declared persona non grata and thrown out of office and party. He has been a tireless worker of the NAP, but was, at the same time, responsible for internal dissensions. As a marked pro-lae left wing adventurist he was not quite acceptable to the conservative old guards of the Ned-shirt and his predominent interest in the promotion of the Kissan Committee from within the NAP higher echelon where he was occupying the chair of the Zonal Secretary was considered dangerous. He was, therefore, dropped from office during the annual session of the party and subsequently thrown out of the party. He has, therefore, declared the formation of the Mazdoor Kissan Committee designed to work against imperialism, feudalism and colonialism". Afzal Bangash is a pro-Bhashani Maoist belonging to the sub-section of Major Ishan.
Mian Shahinshah who happens to be the eldest son of Mian Jafar Shah (ex-inister) has thrown himself into the arena with a dramatic surprise. He declared the formation of the parallel Sarhad N.A.P. at a meeting of the Kissan Committee held at Charsadda on 23.3.1968. The formation of his N.A.P. is an unexpected corollary of the momentous decision of the Wali Khan group to close its doors completely to the Kissan Committee banning membership of the Kissan Committee for the NAP followers. This was a decision which not only piqued Bangash but also gave Mian Shahinshah a fillip to form a par of his own as well as to try for winning over the Kissan Committee. This strategy was perhaps being followed under the guidance of C.. Aslan with whom he is affiliated and w happened to be present at the meeting of the Kissan Committ held in Charsadda on 23.3.1968 in which the parallel NAP wa launched. Bhashand himself is reported to be vascillating
T
the ted take

1968_p043.text:
-34
y5
between Ishaq and Aelam and the moment he makes his final decision to discard one, the left over will expootedly rush to join the parent N.A.P. 1.e. the Wali han group.
The Wali Khan group is, in fact, the predominant group and it is thie group which is expected to play a major role in the next elections. It is rabidly against the One-Unit but in has a soft corner for President Ayub. Abdul Ghaffar Khan remains their unchallenged bero but are deeply chagrined over his pro-indu stance. They are trying very hard to win over complete and unreserved leadership of Pathans but have repeatedly failed to achieve this objective owing to the inflexible and ill-defined policies of Abdul Ghaffar Khan. Where A.G. Khan is a source of prestige to them he is also a cause of extreme embarrassment. Where the Serhad N.A.P. decide to define its political objectives as dis-integration of One-Unit and democracy, Abdul Ghaffar Khan would counter it with his own declared objective of Pakhtoonistan, the Utopia for which he is loath to define boundries. However, the complexion of the Sarhad NAF (Wali Khan group) is becoming more and more regional and they feel that if they would unite the Pathane under their banner they would dietate their terms to the constituents of the Opposition. So far they have declined to join hands either with the P.D.M. or with the other regional factions of the N.A.P. They also do not think Buch of Mr. Butto. Their main objective is the undoing of One-Unit and they will join hands with any party, as a necessary evil, that could guarantee the achievement of this objective.

NORMANCE ON AL

Reference Information Report No. 7491-95(X)A...
The councillors session of the B.A.P. (anti-Bhashand group) held in Peshawar recently will remain a land mark in the history of the Sarhad H.A.P. who were able to steer the session to their advantage from the beginning to the end. As we had forecast Abdul Wali Khan has been elected unanimously as party chief and most of the members of his coterie have been dished out offices of distinction both in the provincial and the central tiers of the party. The now party is by and large dominated by smaller units of West Pakistan and therefore the breaking up of One Unit has become its main political objective. In fact, this is the only political party in the country which has provided a platform for anti-One Unit activities.

The Sarhad N.A.P. achieved their first triumph by deciding upon Peshawar as the venue of the conference and their next major achievement was the unanimous election of Abdul Walá Khan as the party chief. But their real triumph lies, in their show of strength with an impressive procession followed by a well attended public meeting on 1.7.1968 at which all the delegates flaunted anti One Unit badges and openly endorsed the demand of Sarhad H.A.P. for an autonomous province under the name of Pukhtoonistan. The conference which was attended by 76 delegates from East Fakistan and over 100 more from different parts of West Pakistan gave the appearance of monolithia integrity for a while but behind this facade was simmering Serious internal dissension. The East Pakistani delegation had from the very start joined hands with the smaller units of West Pakistan to the exclusion of liz. Qasuri and his delegates from the former Punjab. The East Pakistanis were frequently heard raising slogans of 'Pakhtoon Bengali Bhai Bhai', 'Sindhi Bengali Bhai Bhai' and 'Baloch Bengali Bhai Bhai but never once, even for a change, Punjabi Bengali Bhai Bhai'. Mr. Qasuri who sensed this conspiracy against him reacted very sharply and submitted
177 Spec
DEPARIN
1857

1968_p048.text:
his resignation in writing. He had already spoken at length before the session on the objectives of the party and had stressed that as the very name of the party indicated it was necessary to be loyal to the solidarity of Pakistan, to keep the party on national footing and let the masses activate it. The Sarhad H.A.P, who were determined to make the session a success tried to prevail upon lir. Qasuri to withdrew hin resignation. Mr. Qasuri replied that what the Bengali delegaten apparently wanted was nothing different from the G-point programme of lujib-ur-Rahman and what the Sarhad N.A.2. apparently aimed at was an Azad Pukhtoonistan. This was the stage when the party was threatened with complete disaster night at its birth. However, the Sarhad H.A.P. nanured Mr. Qasuri that they were prepared to provide every possible proof that what they actually wanted was provincial autonomy according to the constitution of the Pakistan N.A.P. and that if there were reasons to believe that Professor Muzaffar and his delegates were aiming at secession, they would join hands with Mr. Qasuri with the object of eliminating all such members suspected to be working against the interest of the country and the party. It was after such assurances that Mr. Qasuri accepted the chairmanship of West Pakistan N.A.P. and it is in pursuance of this pledge that Mr. Ajmal Khattak and Abdul Wali Khan will be leaving for Wast Pakistan to study at first hand if their friends from Bast Pakistan were really sharing the views of secessionists. Also they intend to find out about the sources of income of the majority of the East Pakistani delegates who are reported to be poor people who could hardly afford two way faze from Dacea to Peshawar. These delegates from last Pakistan are reported to be what they call Worker-Leaders who have the capacity of putting life into the party put with no ostensible financial means.
The differences between Mr. Qasuri and last Pakistani delegates go deeper tx than a scramble for offices of distinction. In some measure, the differences may be in the natural tug of war between the rightists and the leftists but more

1968_p049.text:
oninous are the differences over the interpretation of article 3(A) of the Constitution of the Pakistan National Ament Party. This article states that both the wings of Pakistan will be given complete provincial autonomy and that except the Ministries of Defence, Foreign Affairs and Currency, all the other portfolion will be delegated to the Provinces of last and West Pakistan. But when it comes to the interpretation of the ambit of each portefolio mentioned above there are vast differences of opinion between Mr. Qasuri and Professo Muzaffar and it was for they wer that the taction of Rast Paldstand delegation from the very start aimed at the
exclusion of Mix. Quuzi za of responsibility. However Abdul Wali Khan could not afford to lose the support of the Punjab altogether. Also he had much in common with Mr. Qasuri, as both of them belong to the rightist group, both are moderate in their political views and both of them are inclined towards the P.D.M.; though Wali Khan is finding it extremely hard to ignore his bigoted supporters over the One Unit issue when it comes to joining the P.D.M. It was, therefore, through the efforts of Abdul Wali Khan that Mr. Qasuri accepted the presidentship of West Pakistan S.A.P. and splintering of the party at its very birth was averted. As already reported the N.A.P, passed various resolutions which gives an indication of its future course of action. The most important shift of emphasis evident from these resolutions is to the necessity of launching a strong lass movement to realise their political objectives. A resolution, demanding direct elections, parliamentry form of Government, fundamental rights and aivil liberties atated that these objectives could not be realised through the present systen of election and therefore the only alternative left was to launch a strong mans movement. To this mass movement they have also invited the opposition parties and democratic fozees of the country. So far no definition of a strong mass movement has been given. It is yet premature to say whether the ... would join hands with the P.D.M. because the later in ret darur of the basing of Che-Unit.

As far as record of this office oes, there is no indication if any such ban was imposed md, therefore, action will have to be taken afresh in this direction.
Further, if a ban is to be imposed now, it will have to be done under the Political Parties Act, 1962. Section 3 of this act lays down prohibitim for formation of a certain political party as under :-
3(1) No Political Party shall be formed with the object of propagating any opinion, or acting in a mann er prejudicial to the Islamic Ideology, or the integrity or security of rakistan.

The "Zamindar", Lahore, dated the 30th October, 1942,
The formation of the "Afghan Ittihad Party".
In the course of a short leaderette the paper writes:-
party known as the "Afghan Attihad Party" has been
to counteract the hooliganism started by the Congress
C. 1.and the Khudai Khidmatgars, in the N.W. F. P. at the present
moment. It has been announced that as the actvities of the Congress and Khudai-Khidmatgars are detrimental to the interests of the Musalmans, this party will oppose them. Prior to this Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan had formed an organisation, called the 'Pushtun Jirga" which also aimed at opposing the Congress, but now when a new party has been established in view of the same object, he has dissolved the former and joined the latter.
At present we lack details about the constitution of this new party and therefore cannot offer detailed comments. Any how the report regarding its formation gives at least this much sati sfaction that the compaign launched by the congress to make a Muslim Province, against its traditions, abide by the principle of non-violence, will successfully be counteracted."
No. 15109-10
ID,
OFFICE OF THE SECRETARY TO GOVERNMENT, NWFP., PUBLIC INFORMATION DEPARTMENT.
Dated Peshawar, the 30th October, 1942, 375t

On 16.2.43 Arbab Abdul hafoor Khan, M.L.A.,of Tehkel, came to hie office of the Afghan Jirga at about 1130 hours and remained their till 1730 hours. He also supervise

afghan_jirga_p101.text:

ANHAN JIRIA.
The Afghan Jinma brought into being in Utmanzai in August 1942, and included prominent ex-Redshirt lenders like Amir N-awa Jalia and Sarfaraz Khan of Utmanzai. The Jinga'a office was opened in Utanzai. Its flag was the old Kulai dimtar fl with a crescent and star added to it while the uniform of volunteers was red. The aims and objects- as declared by the members were to revive the Kudai Khidmar movement in its original form and strike for the social and econordic uplift of the Pathans. The following were the office bearers:-
1. Fazal Raziq Khan of Utmanzai.
2. Cul Izar
-do3. Pir Lal Badshah
-do4. Amir Nawaz Jalia
-do5. Sarfaraz Khan
-do6. Bahadur Sher
-do7. Mohd Aslam
-doPresident. Vice-President. Treasurer. Secretary. Nazim
Salar-i-hla. General.
On breaking away from the Congress, Arbab Abdul Ghaffur, ex-Parliamentary Secretary in the Congress Ministry, joined the Jiren and became its President and became ite nost ardent worker. Mian Abdullah Shah of Mazara and Mohd Ramzan Khan, Pleader, of D.I.Khan also followed the Arbab and proved of great assistance in promoting the cause of the Jirga. Tater on organisation called the Afghan Ittehad Party ace-amalgamating the Afghan Jirge was formed in Ghaziabad, T.T. at the instance of Badshah Gul I, and the following were its office bearers:-
1. Badshah Cal I.
2. Rahim Shah Badshah of Hoti.
3. Abdul Ghaffur Khan of Tehkal.
4. Sarfaraz Khan of Utmanzai.
President. Member for Mardan. Member for Khali Mohmands.
do Charsadde
The new party, according to its declared aims and objecta, was to oppose the Congress and Khudai Khidmatgars as "they did not serve the best interests of Mussalmans". The entire programe of the Afghan Jirga was incorporated.
Haji Mohd Amin, an ex-Redshirt leader of Suleman Khel, P.8. Badaber, who had absconded to T.. during, the previous Congress civil disobedience movement, arrived in Peshawar District from T. on 19-11-1942, under the instructions of Badshah Cul I, it was said. The lhji joined the Afghan Jirga and became ita virtual leader. The Haji announced that t the programme of the Jirga would be to remove the brothel from Peshawar and stop non-Muslim book-sellors from selling Islamic books.wack A Working Comittee of the following mombora was formed to carry out the party's programme 21. Man Abdullah Shah of azara.
2. Arbab Abdul Ghaffar Khan of Tohaal.
3. Mokaram Khan of Shabkadar.
4. Gul Mohd Khan of Prang.
6. Zain Khan of R.
6. Qari Fida haskin.
7. Mohd Afzal Khan of Badaber.

afghan_jirga_p102.text:
a 2
A new election of the office bearers of the Jirga was held in Utmanzai on 8-12-1942 and the following were elected :-
1. Abdul Ghaffur Khan of Tehlol.
2. Amir Nawaz Khan Jalia.
3. Pir Lal Badshah.
4. Mokaram Khan of Shabladar.
5. Bahadur Sher of Utmanzai.
Members of the Working Committee.
1. Abdullah Shah of Mazara.
2. Sarfaraz Khan of Utmanzai.
3. Bahadur Sher of Utmanzai.
4. Mohd Zaman of Khubi.
5. Said Shah Badshah of Nowshera.
6. Khan Dad Khan of Sandasar.
7. Mohd Afz-al Khan of Badaber.
8. Ghulam Hussain, Mian, of Kangra.
President.
Secretary.
Treasurer. Salar-i-Azam.
Naib Salar-i-Azam.
The Afghan Jirga came into lime-light when its volunteer started to picket the brothel in Peshawar City on 11-12-1942. The Municipal Committee also came to their support by passing a resolution relating to the removal of the brothel from the City. Muslim League leaders like Aurangzeb Khan and S.Lal Badshah and Ahrar leaders like Abdul Qayum Popalzai too lent their assistance by joining a deputation of the Afghan Jirga which waited upon the Deputy Commissioner, Peshawar, in this connexion. The Government took a hand in the matter as well. A large mumber of prostitutes were prosecuted under the F.C.R. and the brothel was cleared me of the prostitutes on 8-1-1943. The picketing by the volunteers' kxxxixtaxkxnx was stopped a few days later. The number of volunteers had risen to 70 on 22-1-1943. The non- Muslim shopkeepers agreed to stop selling Islamic books. Agitation to remove the dancing girls from Mardan has also been started and speeches by Haji Mohd Amin and others have beer made asking the authorities to turn the dancing girls out otherwise picketing was to be resorted to. The Haji announced in a meeting that the Deputy Commissioner, RAK Mardan, had agreed to consider the question of evicting the dancing girls.
So far no branch of the Afghan Jirgn has been opened in districts other than Peshawar. Mohamed Ramzan Khan, Pleader, of D.I.Khan however, is trying to open a branch in D.I.Khan and has succeeded in enlisting 3 prominent persons as members.
Maslaus 15/3/43

"DAILY SARHAD" (PHAWAR) OF THE 30th MARCH, 1943.
AFGHAN NATIONAL CONFERENCE
Peshawar, 30th March,
A report states that, under the auspices of the AFGHAN JIRGA, an Afghan National Conference will be held in the 2nd week of April and that brisk preparations are being made to make it a success. Both the urban and rural Muslims are working for this end. It is believed the Conference would be a great success.
No. 42526/PPA., Peshawar, dated the 2nd April, 1943. Copy forwarded to the A.I.G., of Police C.I.D., NWPP., and the Deputy Commissioner, Peshawar, for information.

afghan_jirga_p015.text:
JIRGA:
£.P.8. Daily Diary of 16-11-42
On 13.11.2(1500 hours to 1600 hours) a
meeting of the Afghan Jirga,attended by about 300(50) persons
was held at the Shabqeder Adde under the presidency of
✓Mien
Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahsara.
Arbab Abdur Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A.,of Tahkal,
Mullah Taus of Dalazek,Haji Abdullah Khan, Halimzai of
Shabqadar and Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzare, delivered speeches. LArbab Abdur Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A. of Tahkel
exhorted his listeners to unite and follow the creed of Islam. He said that the Musalmans would be ruined from the surface of the earth if they kept apart from the ir Muslim brothren. He cited in this connection the examples of the Turks, the Germans, and the Sikhs who had got everything by unity amongst themselves He especially mentioned the impending invasion of the Japanese for which the Hindus were glade in the inner most of their hearts, and said that to oppose such situations the Musalmans should come together without any further delay.
Mullah Taus exhorted his listeners not k
to be friend the Hindus and not to associat with them, but to join the party of the Musalmans.
Haji Abdullah Khan also spoke against the
Congress and asked his listeners not to have anything to do
with the Congress are its leaders.
Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzare observed that
the chief cause of his leaving the Congress was that the movement was the t of the Hindus and he did not see any amelioration of the conditions of the Muselmans. He appealed to the Pashtoons to join the Afghan Jirge.

On 19.11.12 (2140 hours to 2245 hours) a meeting of the ghen Jirge, attended by 300/350 persons, was held near the Hujra Mokaram Khan in Shebgader villege. Mohd Remzan Khan plender of D.I.Khan was in the chair.
The President of the meeting and Haji Mohd Amin delivered speeches exhorting the Musalmans to follow the creed of Islam and work unitedly for the benefit of the Musalmans. Mohd Ramgan Kahn pleeder, however, briefly described the purpose for which the Khudai Khidmatger movement had been originally started, and which the present leaders had lost sight of totally.
MISCELLANUOUS:
Haji Mohd Amin arrived in Shabqadar on
19.11.42 at about 1600 hours. He was accorded an enthusiastic reception by the public. He offered his afternoon prayers in the Adda Mosque,Shabqadar, and delivered a short speech on the religion of Islam and unity amongst the Muselmans before a congregation of about 60/70 persons. After prayers when he came out from the Mosque Doctor Khan Sahib with some Redshirt Volunteers in uniform met him, The Doctor is reported to have told him that the Redshirts were in a battle with the Britishers and were bent to evict them from this country. Haji Mohd Amin is reported to have replied to him that he could join him only if his efforts were for the unification of the Muselmans and their xalifare welfare, but under the present circumstances when the Redshirts were one in and all with the Congress he was unable to do so. Haji Moh Amin then proceeded towards the village of Shabqadar and it is
germanated and the Haji was then taken again in a procession of 50 volunteers of the Afghan Jirga through the Qissa Khani bazar up to the Azad Mosaffar Khane outside the Kabuli gate, where the procession was dismissed xxx and the Haji left for Tehkel in the company of Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan.

After the Juma Prayers (14.45 hrs to 15.15 hrs), a public meeting under the auspices of Afghan Jirga, et ended by about 500/600 persons, was held under the Presidency of Maullana Raufullah, b/o S.Abdullah Shah, of Mezare.
At the outset Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan, M.L.A., of Tahkel, informed the audience that Mohd Ramzan Khan,pleader of D.I.Khan, who was to preside over the meeting, was unable to come to Peshawar on account of his x illness. Therefore Maullane Raufullah of Mazara will take his place as President of the meeting.
Maullana Fidah Hussian ari, sterted the proceedings of the meeting with the recitation of some versesat of the Holly Quran, and Zain Khan recited a poem in Pushto, the subject of which was anti-congress.
Maullana Fida flussain,kari, in a short speech informed the audience about the aims and object sfxkkaxkfxkaXXйings under which the Afghan Jirga had been formed, saying that their leaders were leading them in the wrong direction.
He was followd by Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan M.L.A., of Takkal. He had hardly spoken a few words which referred to the Congress, when a few perosns of the opposing views began to stert disturbance in the meeting. However, they were given a mile beaten by the volunteers of the Afghan Jirge, ani were turned out from the Mosque. It is said that Babu Fazal- Illahi of Pakha Ghulam was one of them, who had been beaten.

prominent amongst thore present, Arbab Abdul Ghe four Khan, M.L.A.,of Tehkel and Haji Mohd Amin, delivered speeches, while M.Fida Hussain,President of the meeting, moved a resolution hich was unanimously carried out. Maulana Fids Hussein moved a resolution that this meeting of the Musalmans of
Peshawar City request the Frontier Government to give its sanction to the resolution of the Peshawar Municipal Committee regarding the City Brothel, as early as possible.
On 25.12.42 about 40 volunteers of the Afghan
Jirga, under the leadership of Hashem Khan of Tehkely, picketed all the approaches to the City Brothel, without any incident till 1230 hours, when a bout 25 'Gondas' with lathis *x*x from Gulab Khana street of Peshawar City, entered the Brothel. Some of them were under the effect of liquor. The Volunteers of the Afghan Jirga did not put any obstable in their ways because they were bent on mischief. However, on the arrival of a Police Party the Badmashes went away from the Brothel. There was no picketing on the brothel from
1300 hours to 1545 hours, because the volunteers had gone for offering the Jumma Prayers. In their absence the brothel had been crowded by the customers. The volunteers returned for duties at about 1600 hours, when again at about 1630 hours five 'Badmashes'arrived from Gulab Khana Mohallah. The 'Gondas"

characters of the brothel, arranged two other Bed Characters to stand side by side with the Afghan Jirg Volunteers at the various approaches to the brothel, who were esking the customers to enter the brothel without any fear; while Salim and Hatesh were abusing the volunteers. The Volunteers remained quiet.
It is reported that Haji Mohd Amia has asked Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khen of Tehkel, to prepare za two sign boards to be put up at the two main enterences to the City Brothel. The sign boards will have the words "BAZAR ISLAM ABAD" written on them.X

secret On 15.12.42 from 1700 hours to 1800 hours a/meeting, of the Afghan Jirga,was held at their Headquarters, in Lungi Faroshan Bazaar,Peshawar City. Heji Mohd Amin, presided. Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan, of Tehkal,Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzara, Zain Khan.Afridi,and Mokaram Khan of Shabqadar, participated in the meeting. The following items were resolved upon:-
1. That a public meeting should be held on 16.12.42, in which the Ulemas and all the Islamic Organisations should be invited to join them in their compaign against the
prostitute.
2. That the Allama Mashriqi, the leader of the Khaksars, should be approached in writing to allow the Khaksars
to assist them in their efforts against the prostitutes.
3. It was also resolved to request the Municipal Commissioner, to also co-operate with them in the removal of the prostitutes.

Peshawar City.
30 picketers picketed all the approaches to the City brothel under the command of Aukaran Khan. The picketing was peaceful.
on the 16th, one Moti Jan præstitute died, who was buried the same day according to the Muslim rites. The Afghan Jirga volunteers are looking for the Mullah, who attended here funeral rites as they wish to prmouce a 'Fitwa' against him.
The members of the Afghan Jirga are advertising that after Id about 300 volunteers will come to picket the Chakla.
The Afghan Jirga leaders are I still wooing the Khaksars in an endeavour to join the picketing but so far their efforts have not been crowned with success.

ARGUAN JINGA
The City brothel is still being picketed. On the 17th December, Hasham Khen, brother of Abdul Ghafur,MLA, supervised the picketing. No untoward incident has been reported. The prostitutes are reported to have collected RB.80/- and they intend to wait in a deputation on the D.C. with a request to remove the picketing.
People, who have their property in the city brothel, are labouring under the handicap as they cannot realize their rent-money as they cannot go freely thore.

On 18.12.42 from 1500 hours to 1530 hours a meeting of the Afghan Jirga was held in the Mohabat Khan Mosque after the Jumma Prayers. The audience numbered 250 and Qehri Fide Hussain of Nows here, was the present of the meeting. Qahri Fida Hussain,Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A., and Umar Khan of Tehkal, delivered speeches, while Zain Khan of Regi recited a religious poem in Push to. Qahri Fide Hussein and Umer Khen appealed to the Musalmans to assist them in their compaign against the closing of the brothel. Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkal,also sap spoke it in the above strain, describing the unhappy and irreligious results on account of the existence of the brothel. He also said that the Government should also assist them xataskart whole-heartedly.
N.B.
A sum of Rs.26/- was subscribed and 10% from the outside villeges enlisted themselves as volunteers of the Afghan Jirga.
The volunteers of the Afghan Jirge picketed the lanes leading to brothel as usual on 18.12.42 to 20.12.42. On 21.12.42 some employees of the Peshawer Municipal Committee,accompanied by one Abdul Qadar,Mohalader of the brothel,were seen noting down the names of the prostitutes and the number of the houses wherein they lived, presumeably with a view to take some action against them. Amir Nawaz alias Jaliya, of Charsadda,Secretary of the Afghan Jirga, arrived in Peshawar on 25.12.42,and inspected the. picketing arrangement during the night time.

Amir Neves alise Jalia,Secretary of the Afghan Jirg, inspected the working of the volunteers on their duties and assured them that this curse of the prostitutes would be removed in the xx very near future.

AFGHAN JIRGA.
About 30 volunteers picketed the City brothel on the 30th December. No untoward incident occurred.
Maulane Akhter Ali, editor of the 'Zemindar', Lahore, accompanied by Sulten Shah of Bhanamari, Arbab Abdul Ghafur,MLA., Mohd Akrem & Deh Beh adur, Abdul Shah of Mazare and a few others visited the brothel on the 30th December. The shopkeeppers of the brothel asked the Maulana as to how long this picketing would xxx last. The Maulans replied as long as the brothel was not xxx removed.
An employee of the Municipal Committee and Abdul Qedir, Mohalleder of the brothel, are taking down the nemes of the prostitutes. It has given rise to the impressi on that it is intended to lodge eviction suits against them, i.e. the prosti- tutes.

On 8.1.43 meeting of the Afghan Jirge, attended by about 4000/4500 persons, was held from 1515 hours to 1615 hours at the Hastings Memorial, Peshewer City, under the Presidency of Haji Mohd Amin of villege Soleman Khel.Arbab £**¤¤¤¤¤¤¤¤¤««*x*x*xx Abdul Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A., of Tehkel, Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzera, Rahim Bakhsh Ghaznovi, and Haji Mohd Amin, delivered speeches while Zain Khan of Regi, recited so me verses in the praise of the Holy Prophet of Islam. ix Almost all the speakers congratulated the citizens of Peshawar, the members of the Municipal Committee, the authorities and all those who had helped the Afghan Jirga in their usa de crusade against the Prostitutes and closing down the city Brothel. He said that he did not want to be free from the Foreigners but still under the yoke of Hindu majority, and added that the Musalmans wanted to be free in their Ex own ways as envisaged in the Pakistan Scheme of Mr. Jinnah.
After the termination of the meeting Haji Mohd Amin,Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzara and Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A.,followed by about 33/40 volunteers of the Afghan Jirga proceeded through the bazars in the form of a procession, and then went to the City Brothel, xxxwhere they the usual slogans of the "Nardi Takbir" "Chakla Barbad" and "Mohallah Ghe rat-i-Islam Zindah Bad" we re shouted. THE "SARHAD" (PSSHAYAR) OF THE 10th JANUARY, 1943.
NOT WE, BUT DR. KHAN SAHIB, WHO BY GIVING
HIS DAUGHTER TO A SIKH HAS CAST AN
UGLY BLOT ON THE FOREHEADS OF THE FRONTIER MUSLIMS, IS AN NEWY OF THE COUNTRY'S FREEDOM
ARBAB ABDUL GHAPUR KHAN'S SPEECH AT A
MUSLIM PUBLIC MEETING
IN PESHAWAR
1219
21/1
23/1
Peshawar, 8th January,
F
Under the auspices of the AGHAN JIRGA, N-W..P., a meeting of the Peshawar Muslims was held in the promises of the Hastings Memorial today, Haji Mohd Amin presiding. After recitations from the holy Quran M. Abdullah Shah moved a resolution urging upon the Government to prbhibit non-Muslim book-sellers from selling the religious books of the Muslims. He delivered a speech in support of his resolution. Kazi Zara Hussain seconded it, after which the resolution was declared carried. Rahim Baksh Ghaznavi moved another resolution expressing satisfaction at the removal of the "Chakla" from the city and thanking the public, the AGHAN JIRGA and the M.C. Peshawar for their cooperation in this work. The resolution also appealed to a the M.C. Peshawar to change the name of the said bazar from "bazar-i-tawaifan" (prostitutes bazar) to "bazar-i-gnairat-i-Islam" (bazar standing for the self-respect of the Islamic faith). Khan Mirza Mohd Salim Khan seconded the resolution.
There after Arbab Abdul Ghaffur Kaan Khalil addressed the meeting. He narrated the history of the "Chakla" agitation from the beginning to its end, adding that before launching picketing they had invited members of all the local Muslim organisations some of whom had cooperated with them and some had kept aloof. With the grace of God they had succeeded in their mission and "Chakla" had come to an end in Pesnawar. Continuing, he said: "There were some who carried on propaganda to the effect that their movement was "Government's movement . All those who had joined their movement were, by the grace of God, patriots. Produce the like of Haji Mohd Amin who had made sacrifices comparaole with his for the freedom of the country. After passing years as a "C' class prisoner in jail he rendered religious and national services in the independent territory for some eight years. Their political life had started under the banner of the Afghan Jirga, which was founded in 1929. The leaders of the Afghan Jirga were the first to be imprisoned in connection with the political movement. But their selflish leaders changed their flag for the tri-colour flag (of the Congress), and cut themselves off from the Islamic brotherhood. The charge was brought against them that they were opposed to the country's freedom. But I say that not we but DR. KHAN SAHIB, who by giving his Muslim daughter to a Sikh had cast an ugly blot on the foreheads of the Frontier Muslims, is the enemy of the country's freedom. Today, I declare from this stage that after issuing the statement he did after marrying his daughter to a non-Muslim, he has no right to be called a Muslim. He is an apostate, outside the psle of Islam. How can such a person, whose being a Muslim is in doubt, gan supervise Muslims Wakaf's? And how can he represent Muslims in the Assembly ?..... I also wish to make it clear that we would not rest satisfied until freedom is achieved. But this freedom would not be for GANDHIJI but for Muslims and the Afghans. We do not want that after the British some other non-Muslim Govt. should be formed here in India. We openly declare that there would be Islamic Government in India. All non-Muslins are equal us. We cannot give preference to any one from amongst the

afghan_jirga_p063.text:
-2123
British, Germans, Italians, Japanese and Hindus. No one of them has the right to ruke over India. We Muslims alone have the right to rule over India and we would do so."
M.HATI MOHD AMIN appealed to the Muslims to unite for the sake of God and the Prophet.

EXTRACT FROM "TRIBUNE", Lahore, dated 29-10-1942.
Peshawar, Oct.27.-
AFGHAN IT EHAD PARTY FORMED.
Neworganisation in Frontier.
Political discussion at Ghaziabad in Mohmand tribal area, have resulted in the arka creation of a new organisation called the Afghan Ittehad Party, which practically amalgamates the recently formed Pushtoon Jirga, according to reports reaching here.
Badshah Gul, the Mohmand leader, has accepted the leadership of the new party, while Arbab Abdul Ghafoor, M.L.A., ex-Parliamentary Secretary, President of the Pushtoon Jirga, and Khan Sarfraz Khan of Utmanzai, have accepted positions under him (Badshah Gul).
ACCORDING TAXT HEX DECLARATION
According to the declaration of aims and objects, the new party will oppose the Congress and the Khudai
Khidmatgars as "they do not serve the best interests of the Lussalmans". The entire political programme of the Pushtoon Jirga has been incorporated.- A.P.I.

The "Inqilab" Lahore dated the 21st January, 1943
The non-Muslim Book-sellers of Peshawar should stop selling the holy Quraan, otherwise the fghan Jirgah will be forced to picket their shops.
After the successful completion of the task of ridding Peshawar of the prostitutes, the Afghan Jirgah is now contemplating picketing the shops of those non-Muslim book-sellers who sell the holy quraan and other sacred books of the Muslims. It is hoped that an ami cable settlement will be reached with the non-Muslim book-sellers and occasion for resorting to picketing of their shops will not
arise.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan told the representative of the Orient Press in an interview that the Afghan Jirgah was carrying on negotiations with the non-Muslim book-seller and was trying to impress upon them the vital necessity of maintaining the existing communal harmony in the Province. Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan expressed the hopes that these negotiations will succeed and the non-Muslim Book-sellers will discontinue selling the holy quraan and the books of Figah and Hadis. He, however, stated that in case they refused to do so, the Afghan Jirgah will feel compelled to resort to picketing. In conclusion, Arbab Abdul Ghaf Der Khan demanded that the Frontier Government should immediately EnfaкEE bring into force the Act Passed by the Provincial Assembly in this behalf.

afghan_jirga_p085.text:
JIRGA
al.D; J.P. S. Daily Beans
Dated 30.1.43.
On 29.1.43 from 1445 hours to 1520 hours the
Afghan Jiras held a meeting, attended by about 200 persons,
in the Mohabat Khea Mosque after the Junna Prayers.
No President was elected. Zain Khan of Resi recited
s poem in Pushto while Arbeb Abdul Ghafoor Khan delivered a speech. He said that everywhere the conditions of the Muselmans was Zainrautating deteriorating,hich wes due to the fact that they were in revolt to follow the tenets of Islam and had discarded to follow in the foot-steps of their H-oly Prophet. Continuing he said the t Independence was heavenly, but it res foolish on the part of the Musalmans to run after it and leave aside their Holy Quran. He exhorted the Musalmans to tn be as such in true sense and it would be then that they would surely achieve Independence. Proceeding he deprecated the rumour that the prostitutes would be re-inhabited in the City Brothel by the first day of the next month and termed it as simply mischievous. He said that they would not return and in this connection he expressed his gratifications to the authorities, who had assisted the Afghan Jirge to a great extent. He also referred to the sale of the religious books of the Musalmans by the non-Muslim dealers and said that this matter would also be brought within a week amicably to a successful end. Proceeding he observed that he never believed in the Congress leaders except A.G.K., but when he joined hands with the Hindus he dissociated himself from his company. N.B.
During the speech of Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan
one person of unknown identity, remarked that the Arbab was formerly a Congressite and now had started the Afghan Jirge and was bent to ruin the poor people. Some of the

afghan_jirga_p086.text:
-41167
members of the Afghan Jirge,who were standing at the main Gate of the Mosque, turned him out from the Mosque.
After the termination of the meeting Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khen, accompanied by Zain Khan and other members of the Afghan Jirge, proceeded to their new office si tueted in the premises of the City Brothel, bow named as "Kherat-iIslen". At the office it was decided that the Holy Quran should be read out on the next Sunday, and also an Imam
was appointed at the Mosque of Shah Burhan, si tuated in the vicinity,

afghan_jirga_p009.text:
We understand that the Pushtoon Jirge would render valuable service owards setting the Fronviciel affairs of the Frontier Mislims in order. The Arbab Sahib, who knowe the inner secrets of the Congress, will tell the Mlima how the Congress has a fer bem misleading the simple-minded sthana and what its motives were in doing so. We are confident that the Fukhtoon Jirgan will discharge this duty satisfactorily. But the Political goal of the Muslims of India will have to be kept in sight and for this purpose the Jirge should negotiate with Qaid-i-Azam, Mohd Ali Jinnah. It will be within the Province of the Jirgs to take stock of the Provincial matters, but there is no other organisation, except the Muslim League, through which the common national goal of the Muslim nation can be achieved. This is a test for the Musalmans of the N.W, F.P. and they should prove themselves equal to it.

It is learnt from a very reliable source(interception) thet Qazi Attaullah,M.L.A.,hes sent a letter from the Peshewer Central Frison to Arbab Abdul Ghe foor Khen,M.L.A., and President of the Afghan Jirgs, requesting him that it was undersirable on

afghan_jirga_p095.text:

12/4/2. L.

Dated 11.2.43
On 10.2.43 Mian Abdulloh Sheh of Mahzare,
Arbeb Abdul Ghafoor Khan,M.L.A., and a few other members of the Afghan Jirga, held a private meeting in their off ice, in thexpreixex Mohallah Shah Burhan from 1700 hours to The Arbeb said that the present office will
1800 hours. be the office of the Privincial Afghan Jirga with its branches in every district. He said that he is considering xt over the removal of the curse of prosetitutions from the other districts of the Province as it had been done in Peshawar,and that he,xxx accompanied by a few Ulemas would tour throughout the province to acquaint the Pakhtoons with the bad results of the prostitutions. Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzara said that there were still some persons who wanted the prostitutes to return to their places again. Itwas, the refore, desirable that the volunteers of the Afghan Jirga should remain on watch.
I.P.S.Peshawar. 11.2.1943.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p100.text:
Police apartment.
Confidential.
1931
Special Diary, 6/- 17-6-31.
Keshawar District.
A meeting of the shirts of Hazarkhani, Lasho Khel, Deh Bahadur, Sarozal, Mussozai, etc., was held at the Punj Pir 21arat on 15-6-31, under the presidency of razl-1-Rahim of z Hazar Khani. The audience numbered about 350 including
about 70-80 red shirts.
Halim Shah, a red-shirt of Badaber, read out two Pushto poems on liberty.
Ashiq Shah of Deh Bahadur delivered a speech preaching non-violence. He said thatthe shouts of Inqilab Zinda Bad" should not be raised before the Police and British officera and they should not be annoyed by throwing stones on their cars and motor-cycles. The red-shirts should not be hated, on the other hand, afforts should be made to increase their numbers. He regretted that the Government was not abiding by the terms of the Gandhi-Irwin truce in that it had not set free some of the political prisoners. Preaching non- violence he said that if the red-shirts were £%%777
not strong enough to observe it strictly, they should send their resignations which would be forwarded to the central Committee, Utmanzai. Referring to the Reforms Inquiry com- mitte, he remarked that it was no good. Referring to the Frontier laws he said that these were unsuitable even to the savages. It was through these laws that Habib Nur had been hanged X within 24 hours, although the poor man had not fired a single shot on the A.C. Charsadda. He also said that the Government should be requested to reduce the land revenue by 60 per cent and that it should be realised in kind, not in coin. Their demands on the Govern ment were of food and clotning. The Frontier Province
should be granted equal reforms with the rest of India.
186 VI. 0.1. G., G.D., N.W.F.P.,
Rees these
Peshawar.
Zahnr.
no Jin
Conidi. Clert LS. Supdt; of Police

On the night of 17.6.31,a meeting under the Presidency of Abdul Qayum of Buted Teri,was held at village Bufed heri,P.5. Burlari singh in which 400/600 people of Naudeh Bala,achini Bala and Payan Haji Balo, Pavka and Abdarra participated.
Mulla Khan Mir Hilalf" with b Congress Volunteer attended the meeting. They recited a national aong Abdul dus of Sufed Dheri delivered a speech
in which he spoke on la d Revenue and scaricity of Bara water. He said that the Govt instead of giving water for the cultivation of their land,has distributed it to Janrád, Can Peshawar,dity and Dairy Form. The income derived in this way is One Lakh and 24 thousands Wish We the poor people suffer by it. Moreover the Government levies Land Revenue,which they are unable to pay owing to the failure of crops. This revenue was imposed in the time when the prices of grain were high. Now the prices have gone down. We request that grain instead of cash should be accepted by the Govt as a revenue. He muggested that Govt should be move regarding these two points which was seconded by Mohd Araf and Aelan of Sufed heri.
Mulla Khan Mir delivered a speech asking the Khudai Khidmatgare to remain non violent in opposition to the Green Shirt".
Dham Ram 18.6.31

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p104.text:
Police Department.
Confidential.
1951.
Special Diary, D/- 20-6-31.
Peshawar District.
A red-shirt meeting was held at Nur Shah (Sper Sang) on 15-6-31, under the presidency of Maulvi Ilyas. The audience numbered about 2000-2500 including about 900-1000 red-shirts. About 15 flags of various colours were also brought in the meeting.
Maulvi Hafiz Muhammad Aslam of Ghilzai Kandar Khel opened the proceeding by reciting some verses from the Coran, and then read out a 'Pushto' poem warning the red-shirts against the newly created "Green Shirts".
Nasr-Ullah, Mullah of Matti, read out a 'Pushto' poem 3 on freedom and revolution.
Two red-shirt boys read out a 'Pushto' poen cond erming slavery and advocating sacrifices in the cause of freedom.
A red-shirt read out to the audience an article from the Pukhtun' of May 1931 dealing with the sacrifices of the red-shirts in the cause of the Nation.
Muhammad Mullah of Garha, read out a 'Pushto' poem on freedom.
A red-shirt Mullah praised the red-shirts and cursed Government reporters.
Maulvi Muhammad Ilyas, the president, advised the audience to follow the laws of Islam and treat each other kindly. He praised the red-shirts as well wishers of the public.
Mir Muhammad, a one-eyed man, read out a 'Pushto' poem in which he remarked that a society of "Green-shirts" had been brought into existence to oppose the red-shirts, and that the formers were nothing but deceivers. In con- clusion he advised the red-shirts to observe non-violence as if they did otherwise, i it would benefit Government.
E

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p105.text:
(2)
A young red-shirt read out a 'Pushto' poem saying that the people were longing for the return of the ex-King - Aman-Ullah Khan as they had tired of the British Government.: Abdul Ghafur of Tahkal interrupted and stopped the boy from proceeding further.
Maulvi Muhammad Sharif madeZspeech saying that the present backwardness of the Muhammadans was due to their giving up the teachings of the holy Qoran. They were quite in large numbers but bound with the chains of slavery. He advised them not to fight against each other and to re- frain from gambling and drinking. Referring to the "Green Shirts" he said that they had been brought into existence to oppose the "Red Shirts". The clothes made no difference if the wearers did not follow the right path. The red shirts red
prefer to wear red clothes as the enemy did not like the
red colour.
Abdul Ghafur Khan of Tehkal related to the audience the services Abdul Ghaffar Khan and his red shirts had rendered to the nation. Referring to the Reforms Committee he re- marked that it was working in the interest of Government and that they had no confidence in it. He also said that the Government should reduce the land revenue by 60 per cent. and should realise the remaining 40 per cent. in kind. Advocating unity he remarked that the new society which had been organised to oppose the red-shirts was named the "Khid- matgaran-1-Rasul", but XXX it should be called "Khidmat- garan-1-Caroe". It was the old policy of the Government to divide and rule. He advised the audience to observe non- violence and not to resist the Police at the time of arrest. The meeting ended with a prayer.
The following persons have severed their connections
with the Red-shirt organisation:-
1. Khalid Khan, Lambardar of Kaggawala.
2. Haider Khan, Pensioner Daffadar of Kaggawala.
3. Gulab Sher of Masho Khel.
4. Shamshad of Masho Khel.

He addressed the % villagers with a short speech asking them to promote unity amongst them- selves, to refrain from using violence against Government servants and others, and not to be afraid of any weapon which may be used against them.
A committee of the following persons was formed in
this village:-

Mulla Abdul Quddus made speech against the distribu- tion of the Bara water to the Cantt: gardens, and against high tales of
the land revenue. He asked the audience to request Govern- ment to reduce the land revenue. Muhammad Arif and Aslam
supported the speaker. Mullah Khamir informed the audience thesocities had be organised to oppose the red-shirts and that they should be very careful and avoid quarrels etc. Aslam Khan, contractor, regretted that they were not follow- ing the teachings of the holy Qoran and advocated settelemen of cases according to the 'Shariat'.
5. The following are the office holders of the Badaber
red-shirt committee:-

The number of red-shirts in Hazar Khani and Chamkanni
villages is 20 and 63 respectively.

They also alleged that Red Shirts had been beaten by the Police, and threatened that, if Hari Singh and his son charged the accused, they would be murdered. haq Shah, President of the Central Committee of Deh Bahadur (for Mohmands), also arrived and made a joint inquiry with Abdul Ghafur Khan, and tried to in- duce the Deputy Supdt. of Police to desist from arresting the accused on a charge which they declared to be groundless, saying that the matter would be reported to the Local Government and to H. They then marched the Red Shirts, numbering, about 2,000/3,000 from east to west passing in front of the Police Post, with flags of various colours, and drums and music, shouting Inqilab Zinda Bad". After this, Ashaq Shah and Abdul Ghafur brought Muqarrab, Murad, Mausam, Muntazir and Latif, and they were arrested by the Police. The case was first registered under sections 342/392. and Sikandar, Lambardars of that village, www to Tehkal where he was to hold a conference to effect a com- promise between them and the Mullagoris of T.T. The message also said that if they refused to come to lahkal he would himself come to them and hold a meeting at mindukas. nis invitation is said to have been refused.
4. un 16-6-31, Abdul Majid, a nephew of naji Abdul Malik of Kafur Dheri, beat one of his tenants named Mir Aftab for having enlisted as a red-shirt. The man was sent to nospita

of Tehkal led a party of
5. un 19-6-31, Abdul Ghafur Kha r kaayor
about 60 375 red-shirts to Hindukas where he had proposed t to hold a meeting. The meeting was not allowed to be held as the plot of land proposed for this purpose belonged to the Nawab of Tehkal. They then went to the canal Rest House and Sherdad Garhi but failed to hold a meeting on account of difficulties of a similar nature. ultimately at about 5-20 p.m. they returned to Kochian village and held the proposed meeting in a field. The proceedings were opened by a minor boy who recited a Pushto poem on Freedom. Maulvi Muhammad Ilyas of Kochian made & speech
supporting red-shirt movement and advising people to follow the teachings of Islam. ne remarked that the movement led people on the path of Liberty and victory. Those who were enemies of Islam were the enemies of God.
Maulvi Muhammad Sharif of Koshian made a speech saying that Khudai Khidmatgars were working for the benefit of the country and not for any motive of their own. If the land revenue was reduced the whole country would benefit.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p112.text:
(3)
1915
Abdul Ghafur Khan, son of Arbab Juma Khan made a speci saying that the Nawab of Tehkal had no respect for the "Shariat and therefore their was no use of discussing matters with him according to the "Shariat'. He (the Nawab) had held a meeting to discuss with the mullahs whether the red-clothes were allowed in Islam. The Mullas disapproved the wearing of the red-clothes. The Green Shirts had been brought into existence at the instigation of Mr: Caroe who had, in this connection, consulted S. Mehdi Shah, Bara- nai Muhammad Umar Khan, and Khan Bahadur Aftab Gul. It was wrong to believe that the Arbabs of Tehkal would discuss matters according to the "Shariat". Their "Shariat was the will of Mr: Caroe. He further said that they should fear
God, not the Nawab of Tankal. They should not feel that the latter had ordered them to vacate Hindukas. A day would come when, seeing them well united, he would request them to come to Hindukas. They should be fearless like Bilal (a slave of the Prophet).
After the conclusion of the meeting the red-shirts wen to kafur Dheri to see Mir Aftab who had been beaten by Abdul Majid Khan.
6. It is reported that on 21-6-31, a party of 12 red- shirts under the leadership of 'Captain' Hakim made a round of Fattu, Abdur-Rahima, Wad pagga, Ranogarhi, Chabian and Najoi villages. They enlisted about 21 new red-shirts. 7. It is reported that on 20-6-31, Mir Akbar, son of Ghulam of Nahaqqi with a party of 15-16 red-shirts went to Meaura village and held a parade there. Mir Akbar is the same man who attended the last Congress Session at Karachi and is now trying through the Tahsil authorities to get a Lambordarship.
2 JUN 1931
Lahms. nd. Di
Confidential Clerk,
For Senr. Supdt. of Police,

In connection with the Sarbandcase the red shirts
of Mathra, Badaber, Burj Hari Singh assembled at Sarband on 22-6-31.
Abdul Ghafur Khan of Tahkal and Ashiq Shah of Den Bahadur are said to have taken active part in making demonstrations on the spot. Both of them have been arrested this morning by the Deputy Commissioner and D.S.P.Saddar under section 225-B/143-IPC. Messages have been circulated through out the villages to hold a
general meeting at Tahkal today in comection with their arrest.
Dhani Ram 2416131

a large number of people from Nathra, Daud zai, Burj Hari Singh and Padaber assembled at Tahkal Bala on the evening of 24 June. After saying their prayers they went off quietly. Sheikh Abdul Rahman of Tehkal Bala has been elected as President, in place of Abdul Ghafur han.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p117.text:
Police Department. Confidential.
1931.
Special Diary, D/- 26-6-31.
Peshawar District.
A meeting of the red-shirts was held at Tahkal Bala, in honour of Abdul Ghaffar Khan on 25-6-31. The audience numbered about 7000-8000 including about 1000-1500g red shirts and 500-600 women. Abdul Ghaffar Khan read some verses from the Coran after which he advised the audience to maintain unity amongst themselves and to free their poor nation from the clutches of the tyrant. He observed that God created the Mussalmans to free other nations fro slavery but they had themselves become slaves. They shoulć before helping other nations to Liberty, get themselves freed
from slavery. He congratulated the audience on the arrest of Abdul Ghafur Khan of Tahkal and said that the jail was a better place for them. They should always pray that foolish European officers (like those who had arrested Abdul Ghafur) might remain their administrators and commit such improper acts (like the arrest of Abdul Ghafur) as would keep their agitation in tone. If Abdul Ghafur had not been arrested the other day, how could the present meeting, and that of yesterday, have been con- vened. He further observed that they (the people) were not afriad of British Guns and Aeroplanes now. They were to die once only, might it be from fever, cholera, plague or the bullet of the British. The last mentioned death would, however, be the most preferable. They did not now fear from going to jails. He then lectured the red-shirts on non-violence advising them to make no re- tort if they were beaten, abused, or threatened by any body. Addressing those who were putting obstacles in the way of, and propagating against the movement, he request- ed them not to bear malice against it if they could not help it. They should, at least, refrain from helping

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p118.text:
(2)
The
the British against them, remain neutral, and see what God ultimately did. Many powerful kings had ruled in the world but when the started tyranny over their subjects, they were ruined by/Almighty. He would also ruin them (the British) in the same way. Addressing the women he congratulated them for giving birth to such brave sons (like Abdul Ghafur) who had sacrificed their lives and property for the sake of the Nation. He also exhorted XX them to use Khaddar and boycott British made cloth. He was very glad that Abdul Ghafur's mother had sent her congratulations to him (Abdul Ghaffar Khan) on the arrest of her son. He hoped they would all follow in the wake of Abdul Ghafur Khan. The meeting dispersed at about
9. p.m.
