c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p120.text:
Police Department.
Confidential
1931,
Special Diary, D/- 27-6-31.
Peshawar District.
A red-shirt meeting was held at Deh Bahadur in honour
of Abdul Ghaffar Khan, at about 3 p.m. on 25-6-31. The audience numbered about 6000, about three thousand of which were red-shirts and 200 women.
X
Ke
Abdul Ghaffar Khan delivered a speech in which he con- gratulated the audience on the recent arrest of Abdul Ghafur Khan and Ashiq Shah who had been sent to jail for the sake of Liberty. He remarked that, had Abdul Ghafur and Ashiq Shah been sent to jail for theft or some other crime, it would have been a cause of annoyance to him. The Government herself was breaking the terms of the truce. She She might wanted to provocate people to violence so that may have some excuse for suppressing the movement by firing at the people. He warned them against resorting to violence. He advised them to adhere strictly to the terms of the truce. He further remarked that they (the red-shirts) could not judge their power themselves, but the Government could. They had shaken its very foundations. He asked the Police reporters to note down his words accurately and convey them to the Government. They were not now afraid of lockups and jails. The work that they had started would have to be completed. The time had passed when people used to fear lockups, jails, and machine guns. The Khans and the Mullas should not be treated harshly. Those who wore red-shirts were Khudai Khidmatgars 1.e. the servants of God. He re- quested women to also join the movement. After concluding his speech he left for Tahkal.
Ghulam Muhammad of Lund Khwar also accompanied Abdul
Ghaffar Khan.
The following agitators were also present at the meet- ing:-

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p121.text:
(2)
1. Sanobar Hussain of Kaggawalla with 300 volunteers of Anjuman-1-Zemindaran.
2. Idris and Shamroz of Masho Khel.
3. Abdul Aziz, sonof Arbab Muhammad Aslam Khan of Landi.

Confidential.

Special Diary, D/-6-7-31.
Peshawar District
[Translation of a report by a Police Short hand Reporter).
A red-shirt meeting was held at Mian Gujar village,
Daudzai police station, at about 11 a.m. on 1-7-31, under the presidency of Haji Abdul Khaliq. The number of audience which included about 2500-3000 red-shirts, 40 Congress volunteers, 120-130 members of the Anjuman-1-Garibanan, Peshawar, and 200-250 women, was about 5500-6000.
A Maulvi opened the proceedings by reading a portior of the Qoran.
A red shirt boy read out a Fushto poem in praise of Abdul Ghaffar Khan.
Khan Muhammad a red-shirt read out a Pushto poem on unity and liberty.
Maulvi Muhammad Rafiq after reading out some verses from the Qoran addressed the audience with a speech thank- ing God that He had given them a sincere leader who had infused a new spirit in them. He observed that the present movement
movemtn was quite in accordance with the spirit of the
holy Qoran, advocated unity and abstinence from blood feuds etc.
A red-shirt named Badshahgai, read out a Pushto poem advocating unity and mutual love and praying for the destruction of the enemy. The people were also exhorted to turn the British out of their country. On this shouts of "Allah-o-Akbar Islam Bare Fakhr-1-Afghan Zinda Bad" were raised.
Ghulam Ahmad Gul, red shirt, delivered a short speech saying the Government should give them their rights They would carry on their fight as long as it did not give them freedom. Their fight would be a fight of words. They had no machine guns but could say plain words. Their mouths could not be shut. If the Government trie shut

One Fazl-1-Haq, a red-shirt, red out a Pushto poem advocating liberty and red-shitism.
Ghulam Ahmad Gul read out another Pushto poem exhort the Pathans to get ready for Liberty and to offer their

They held a dialogue the object of which was to impress on the minds of the audience the benefits of the Swadeshi, etc. The foreign goods were con- demned and the British were accused of utilising the In- dian money in manufacturing machine guns etc., to use against the Indians.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p130.text:
Van (the Farangi)
war strin
(8)
2295
clothes and commenced the fight. On seeing our young men
united, the Farangi shot down our children, blockaded our houses and made our brethren sit in the sun. All thi we bore with patience and the result was the defeat of this big 'Sarkar'. To-day the Farangi asks the Pathans why they are angry with him. Today he says he is our cousin. I ask the Farangi what he gave us for our ser- vices, and why after 13 months he is making himself our of the sacrifices of brother? All this is the result
the red-shirts. Today they are held in respect all over the world. Their stories are being related in America and Germany". (Shouts of Allah-o-Akbar and Inqilab Zin- da Bad). "O my brethren; on one side we have gained res pect, on the other we have come out honourable, and thirdly we have stopped offering presents to the Farangi. Now the Farangi has come to know what we are. You (the audience) may have realised the fact that the Farangi does nothing for you when you flatter him and offer him presents. He yields to power and unity. Our power lies in red-clothes. The Farangi asks us to-day 'What should I give you? - What do you want?' We have told the Farangi that this question will be de- cided in a Peace Conference which is to be held after two months. We now tell the Farangi let the reporter note carefully that our people are dying of hunger, their children are dying of hunger, and you eat five times a day, wh while we cannot afford to have a single meal a day. You ride in motor cars and live in bung lows, while we cannot afford to build a poor cottage. Reduce the land revenue by 50 per cent. as we cannot pay it. Your are leading a luxurious life, but we d cannot see our children dying of hunger. Do you (the audience) look upon the Farangi as a bugbear. I
say he is not a bugbear but he is a man like ourselves

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p131.text:
(9)
We too are the creations of God. You (the Farangi) should allow them two times meal and reduce the land revenue. How can they cope with their daily expenses if they pay you the land revenue. You are getting Rs.5000/- a month while we are dying of hunger. It is immossible that you should enjoy life when we are dying of hunger. I tell the Farangi that we cannot afford to pay him so fatly. We cannot pay him more than Rs.300/- p.m. or if it be insufficient, Rs.400/-, and if this amount he think insufficient, then we will allow him another hundred rupees 8 as a substitute for the flatteries and presents (dalis). More than this amount (Rs.500/-) will be 'Haram' (prohibited by religion). If he said that he could not live with this amount, then we will tell him to leave our country and go away. We will not pay him what he wants but we will pay him so much that he may be able to fill his belly like ourselves. We cannot allow him to eat five times a day at the expense of our bellies. We want him to increase the salaries of our In- dian officers, as they cannot live properly with their present pays. The salaries of the Head Constables, the Thanadars s and the Jamadars should be increased because they have to entertain those who entertain them during their visits to the villages. If they do not take bribe how then they can cope with their expenses. They have also to maintain their own families and, in addition,
they have to maintain their prestige. It is a disgrace them
for K to refuse tea to the khans who feed them during their visits to the villages. What can a constable do in such a XXX small salary. People say the Farangi does not take bribe. I say why the Farangi should take bribe when he gets Rs.2000/- a month and in addition he is not bound to entertain anybody (Khans etc.) with tea or anything else. That should a poor Indian do if not takea
ג

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p132.text:
(10)
and the people bribe. His salary should be increased so that he may live comfortably. My third demand is that we should be given a share in the Government. We want such share in the Government as would enable us to fill our bellies and educate and bring up our children properly. We, the Khans, are not hungry, but we want these things for our nation. The reporter of the Farangi should con- vey my demands to his master (He repeated the above de-: mands again). These things will not be given to them 1 (the people) on begging. The Khans and the Pleaders have many times, before this, begged for these things by means of resolutions, but the Farangi gave them nothing. Now the red-shirts have started this movement. They will get every thing they want if they maintained unity and brotherhood amongst themselves. I pity the Government servants and those of my brethren who help the 'Sarkar and for the betterment of whom I am stry- ing. They should recollect what treacherous deeds have they committed against their country and religion for the sake of the 'Farangi'. They parangi has

A red-shirt meeting was held at Nasarpur, near Mir Ali Baba's shrine on 26-6-31, under the presidency of Abdul Rah- man Shah of Wad pagga. The number of the audience was about 400. Ibrahim Khan of Jhagra, organiser of the meeting, spoke against the Frontier Regulations and advocated unity. Nisar Hussain and Fida Hussain, sons of the Kari Mullah Sb. of Kurvi, also made speeches on the same subject.

station area is reported to be 301.

XAbdul Ghaffar Khan thanked the audience for coming over to attend the meeting and said that inspite of the facts that their country was much fertile and its inhabitants are very hard workers but yet they are in worst condition. The main cause of their degraded condition is the disuinon existing among them. He appealed to the audience to unite together and to decrase their expenses. He went on to say. the Indians are starving but on the other hand the English are enjoying Не gave out that their females are working with bare footed in the feilds but have they ever seen ant English lady in this condition. He remarked that all this is due to their disuinon and ignorance. He went on to say that the present struggle has been started to remove their poverty. Further he said that the English would never albow them to enjoy their rights by flattery. It is due to the agitation that they are asking them to put up their rights before them. He expressed his pleasure for participating the females in the meetir the and exhorted them the use of Khaddar. He said that the He English men are human kind like them and they have goed g no right to rule over them. The Indians should be rul

strictly observe their principles and to remain non vio.
to acheive their ends. X

The proceedings were opened by the recitation of a Pushto poem by Bahadur Khan of Tehkal dealing with the liberty.
One Abdul Rab, Captain of the Red Shirts of Ghunda Karkana,P.S. Khan Mahi,read out a poem describing the fearlessness and bravery of the Red Shirts.

A red-shirt meeting was held at Chamkanni at about 10-15 a.m. on 10-7-31, under the presidency of Arbab Abdul Aziz Khan of Landi, President of the Mohmand Tappa red-shirt The audience numbered about 4000 including about 2000 red- and 300 women.
shirts, 500 members of the "Garibanan
The meeting was opened by Bahadur Khan of Tahkal Bala who recited a Pushto poem on freedom.
Abdul Rab, 'Captain' of the red-shirts of Ghunda. Kar- kana, Khanmahi police station, read out a poem in praise of 1 the red-shirts.
Sardar Shah of Chuha Gujar, read out a poem on freedom, sacrifices, etc.
Muhammad Zaman Khan of Pir Piai, read out a Fushto poem the purport of which was that the red-shirts were not afraid of Jails, Machine guns, etc. In this poem the Govern- ment was asked to give up practising tyrannies, etc.

He praised Chan Badshah for his bravery and the pluck ne had shown in saving the lives of many of the drowning persons, and presented him with a silver medal for his services. He then related to the audience how the foreigners (the british were leading luxurious lives while the people were dying of hunger and said that the movement had been started to bring the people to their senses so that they may guard themselves

On 14-7-1931, the reds of an Gujar were expected to visit the house of late sarogai in Mohallah Chirvakoban with a view to condole his family. The processions of

Police Department.
Confidential.
1931.
Special Diary,
D/- 4-8-31.
Peshawar District.
On 31-7-31, when proceeding to Masho Khel, the D.S.P., Sadar met a party of about 38 men of whom 28 were red-shirts, on the road between Masho Khel and Masho Gagar near the Khara Khel Jhangi. They were led by Abdul Karim, a member, and Arsala Khan, son of Baid-Ullah, a "Captain" of the red- shirts of Balarzai. They were stopped and questioned as to 1 why they were contrvening orders under Section 144-C.P.C. by moving about in a group in uniform. Abdul Karim replied that they were returning from Masho Khel where they had gone to attend the funeral of one Muhammad Ali who had died the same morning, under the orders of their local 'Salar', Said Alim, son of Mir Alim of Badaber. They were ordered to disperse and warned not to form a gathering in uniform. They moved on to Masho Gaggar which is at a distance of half a mile from the place.

On 28-7-31, at about 11.30 a.m., Khan Abdul Ghaffer

Khan, with D.D. Gandhi, Khursheid Behan, Mian Ahmad Shah, ident sul and Ali Gul Khan, arrived in Tehkal village and went to the the land Hujra of Arbab Juma Khan. They were greeted by the Red-shird put who were standing on both side of the road leading to the H Hujra. They paased about 5 minutes in the Hujra and then went to the house of Arbab Juma Khan where about 500-600 women had assembled. They came back to the Hujra after abouty for 20 minutes and held confidential conversations with the President of the Red-shirts. D.D. Gandhi was seen taking notes in English.
Arbab Juma Khan requested them repeatedly to have their meals in his Hujra but they could not acceded to his request as it would spoil their programme. They were, how- ever, served with fruit etc. Devi Dass Gandhi declared/the Object of their visit was not to hold meetings and deliver lectures, but it was to study the situation 2 in the Frontier Province. He also said that demonstrations etc. were against their wishes. They left Tahkal without being accompanied by any Red-shirt.

greet- - stayed Gul2. Large numbers of Red-shirts had assembled near Khazana Charsadda Bala, Nahaqqi, Gulbela and Taudah villages on the tand taroad to receive Abdul Ghaffar Khan. Arches were raised and shouts of "Long Live Revolution" were raised when any lorry or car passed by. At about 1-15 p.m. a red shirt came from Peshawar with a message crowds should dis- perse and the arches pulled down. This was done.
3. Abdul Ghaffar Khan with D.D. Gandhi, Khursheid Behan, Mian Ahmad Shah, S. Lal Shah and Abdul Mannan, President of the Chamkanni Red-shirts arrived in village Tukhtabad at about 1-30 p.m. The Red-shirts were lining the road between JAVY BAYA

About 3-30 p.m. Abdul Ghaffar Khan etc., took their meals and then left for Nahaqqai. On the way to Nahaggi Lal Shah and Abdul Mannan of Chankanni left the party for A Peshawar. At Nahaqqi the leaders verfe greet ed by the Red-shirts in front of their office. They stayed their for about 7 or 8 minutes and then proceeded to Gul- bela. At Gulbela they inspected the Red-shirt office and then went to Charsadda.
The gathering at Takhtabad numbered about 1150 of which about 70/80 were women and at Nahaqqi, about 400 red- shirts and 40-50 women were present.

On the evening of 3.8.31,Habibullah and Mo bullah, Red Shirts of Landú Akhun Ahmed demanded Rs 30/- from Mohd Akbar,Mohd Umar and Mir Akbar of the Village who had married a girl from Hazar- Khani on 2.8.31 as a subscription for the Reds. Mohd Akbar stated that they had got nothing for the present. The Red Shirts insisted upon the payment of the subscription and took them to their Office. The complainants pone one golden ornament (Jhumka) and secured Rs 10/- for the payment. Mohd Akbar was sent by the Red Shirts to get Rs 20/- more as a subscription. He went straight to the P.S. Burj Hari Singh and charged the following 12 persons, lobullah, Nur Akhmad, Nanogai Faqir, Habibullah, Ahad, Nur Mohd, Shahwali, Torai, Akbar Feroz and Aslam. A case U/S 342/395 hays been registered in Police Station Burj Hari Singh.

sir,
Inquiries into the matter show the following facts:- Mohd Akbar Patyara' requested the red shirt leaders of Lanii Akhun Ahmad to join his brothers (Mohd Umar's) marriage ceremoney and to bring the bride from village Hazarakhani. The red shirts did so em on return to Lanii Akhun Ahmad they asked Mohd Akbar to pay according to the custom adopted in villages nowadays, Rs. 30/- as subscrip- tion as well as for their feast. Mohd Akbar refused to pay the sum demanded because he is a poor man. On his refusal he and his brother were taken by the red shirts to their office where they were beaten and detained.
Some people say that B. 20/- were taken from Mohd Akber and some people say that they were only taken to the red shirt office and beaten.
A case under Section 395, 342 I.P.C., has been registered by the Burj Hari Singh Police, and the followin red shirts have been taken into Police custody:-
Mohibullah, Habibullah, Abdul Ahmed, Nur Mohd, Aslam, Shah wall, Toral, Akbar, Amir Mohd, Faqir and Nur Ahmad.
For favour of perusal.
syf. Muhd Hay at Khan

7.I.R. No. 96 under Section 29 PCR dated the 20th August 1531,
Police Station Badaber.
Sir,
Inquiries in the above mentioned case go to show the following facts:-
1) Mohd halil, son of Rabu Gul Mohd, of Peshawar, city who has landed property in village Chari Mali Khel, Police Station Fadaber, was Secretary of Red Shirts Mashokhel. He joined the ped shirts without the consent of his parents. Iast night at about 9 p.m., Fabu Gul Mohd, (a clerk in the Chief Commissioner's office) accompanied by 3 minor sons, Jumma Khan, and ir Passan, of village Chari ali Khel, went to the Red Shirts office after his son. He saw his son present in the office. The son welcomed his father while the father just began to beat him with a cane.
The following red shirts present in the office were ordered by Mohd Afzal, brother of Abdul Sattar, President of Mashokhel red shirts to attack upon them. On this (1) Badshah, (2) Behram, (3) Usman, (4) Farid, (5) Shah Mohd, (6) Qadir and two other members of the red shirts whose names are unknown by complainant, began to beat them with sticks, and also snatched their licensed arms (one six chamber revolver and D.B.Shot Gun 12 bore). A sword and an axe were taken from Mir Hassan and his son Jaffar who were in company of Babu Gul Mohd.
2) Babu Gul Mohd, with his companions was detained in Red Shirt Office and Jamadar, Nauroz, of the Mashokhel Red Shirts was sent to Thana, to report that an armed gang was captured by the ashokhel, red shirts in the office. The Sub Inspector Bedaber registered the case u/s 29 FCR and reached the spot, where he found the facts quite contrary to the report made by Neuroz Jamadar of red shirts.
The bad aber Police propose to register a case u/s 342 IPC against the red shirts. The present case u/s 29 FCR is going to be cancelled.

1931.
special Diary. 5/- 29-8-31.
2757
It is reported that the Red-shirts of Chankarni, on 23-8- 31, had it proclaimed in their village that the Shias (the Frontier Constabulary was meant) were coming to loot their village. In this connection a meeting was held in the night which was attended by about 500 persons from the surrounding villages. Mullah qari of Banda and Maulvi Muhammad Yakub of Chamkanni were also present. Maulvi muhammad Yakub delivered a speech saying that the traitor Government, and the informers who were ruining their brethren for petty considerations, should be boycotted. They should unite and keep themselves ready for sacrificing their lives, and should entertain no ent fear of the Government. Mulla Kari also made a speech in a similar strain.
As the meeting was in progress some body cried out that the Shias had entered the village and were plundering their houses. At this the meeting dispersed and the villagers ran away and kept patrolling the streets for the rest of the night.
ced
ur
Amir and Fazal Ahmad, Red-shirt leaders of Chamkanni, were responsible for spreading the above false report in the village.
It is said that the villagers of chamkanni are collecting arms to stand in match with the shia invaders.

Mir Aftab, Ahmad Jan, Abdul Wadud of Nuhaggi, Abdul Ahud Khan and Hustum of Tukhtabad, and Muhammad Amin, Tumbar dar of Pusht-1-Bang, erected a gate on the Charsadda road for the reception of Abdul Ghaffar Khan. Ahmad Jan was clad in red and the rest were in white. At about 1-45 p.m. Abdul Ghaffar Khan arrived at Nahaqqi. He was accompanied by his relatives, friends and working members of his party sitti in 5 cars and one lorry. A national flag was hoisted on his car. He was received with cheers and shouts of Allah-0-Akbar and words 'Fakhr-i-Afghan ba Khair Amad' were inscribed on the gate. He delivered a short speech, the purport of which was, that you people carried out my plans and suffered great troubles and hardships for the sake of the country and made good deal of sacrifices and succeeded to some extent. Don't be pleased, by this that I am released. If the Government will not give us equal reforms with other provinces, we will again start the agitation on the same line as we did before You should be ready to make more sacrifices when the opportunity arrives. Increase the number of Khudai Khidmat- gars for this purpose.
Sd. Zahir Gul Khan D.S.P., Sadar. 12-3-31.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p162.text:
291..
Sher Ali Khan, President of Youth League Parang, accompanied, by Murtaza Khan, 'Salar', of Utmanzai, visited Deh Bahadur today. Presidents of Youth Leagues Khalil, Mohmand and Khalsa 'Teppas' were present in the Red Shirt Office of Deh Bahadur. They held a secret conference in the office for about 12 hours. No out- sider was allowed to get in. The following matters are reported
to have been discussed: -
On 27-10-31, Mian Ahmad Shah of Kazi-khel had visited village Shahab-khel where he stayed for the night. He inter- viewed leaders of Mohmand 'Tappa' with whom he had changed his views, informing them of his principles and those of Abdul Ghaffar Khan. He is believed to have told them that he was working through his religious motives for the uplift of his country-men and that Abdul Ghaffar Khan was the follower of the Congress. It is reported that the leaders present told him that they will consider over it and will let him know of their decision later on. Murtaza Khan and Sher Ali Khan are believed to have discussed this matter with the leaders, in favour of Abdul Ghaffar Khan.
Some dispute had arisen on 28-10-31, over the holding of a meeting at Sheikh Mohamadi in contravention of order under section 144-C.P.C. Said Alam, President of Youth League, Badaber, and Abdul Manan, Vice President of Youth League, Masho- Gaggar, wanted to hold a meeting while Mir Hussain of Shahab-khel and red shirt leaders of Sheikh Mohamadi were against it. As a result of this hot words were exchanged and eventually no meeting was held. Since then there were ill-feelings between the leaders and Sher Ali Khan and Murtaza Khan came to Deh- Bahadur to pacify them.
They are reported to have advised the Presidents of the Youth Leagues present to collect in future susbcriptions in their areas and to put it in a locked cash-box and to send it to Central Office, Utmanzai.
For information.
2000/3/1.

Ibrahim Khan is stated to have pointed out the difficultie that stand in their way by having two centres in Khalsa, and strongly advocated for one centre. He later resigned his post as "Sadar"; following which precedent Muhammad Afzal khan re- signed too.

The members of the Working Committee of Tappa Daudzai, gathered in the Hujra of Abdul Karim Khan of Tukhtabad and carried out the following resolutions, on 30-10-31:-
"In the harvest of sugar-canes every red-shirt should contribute 5 annas and every Sufeid Posh 4 annas per head provided they give it willingly. To collect thes contributions, Fazl-i-Kadir, President of Hariana, Tawwab Shah of Kankola, and Abdul Ahad of Tukhtabad are nominated".
It was decided to buy a lorry with these collections which will run on hire. For urgent purposes it will also help the red-shirts in conveying them to different places.
In this meeting Abdul Ahad Khan of Tukhtabad, Ayub Khan of Chaghri Matti, Abdul Qadir of Shakkarpura, Fazl-i-cadir of Hariana, Tawwab Shah of Kankola, and Mian Fazl-1-Ilahi of Chargali, took part.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p165.text:
Police Department. Confidential.
1931.
Special Diary, D/- 11-11-31.
Peshawar District
A Red-shirt meeting was held at Tukhtabad village, Daudzai police station, under the presidency of Muhammad Sharif of Kochian, from about 12.15 to 3.15 p.m., on 4-11-2 The gathering numbered about 5000-6000 including about 3500-4000 Red-shirts and 100-125 women. About 40-50 flags representing g the Red-shirts of the various villages in the Distt. were also brought to the meeting.
The following prominent Red-shirt leaders of the Dist. were also present:-
1. Mir Badshah, Sadar of Mian Gujar.
2. Mir Badshah, Nazim, of Mian Gujar.
3. Shad Muhammad Khan of Mirzai, Batagram p.s.
4. Moulvi Abdullah of Mahazara, Batagram p.s.
5. Abdul Karim of Kattozai.
6. ari ullah of Banda Mallahan.
7. Saif-ur-Rahman of Banda Mallahan.
8. Abdul Ghaffar Khan of Utmanzai.
9. Abdul Aziz "General" of the City Red-shirts.
10. Mahasal Khan of Shabkadar.
11. Haji Abdul Ghaffar Khan of Utmanzai.
12. Abdul Ahad Khan, Sadr-i-Azam, of Tutkhtabad.
13. Fazal Ilahi, Dalar, of Chargulli.
The following 9 resolutions were passed in this meeting unanimously:-
1) "This meeting expresses its sorrow at the con- viction of Obeid-Ullah Khan, and hatred at the cruel attitude the Government has adopted in regard to him.
2) This meeting urges Government to acceded to all the demands of Obeld-Ullah Khan, other- wise, the meeting challenges the Government and it has already been decided, that all the Red-shirts of the Province will prepare themselves for offering their heads to get these demands acceded to".
3) This meeting congratulates Obeid-Ullah Khan on his sacrifice, and prays that this sacri- fice of his may bring about freedom of the Frontier Province, and India:
4) This meeting congratulates the Khans of sud- hum, and Amir Muhammad Khan of Hoti on their recent arrests
5) This meeting puts forward its emphatic demand that the constitution on the basis of shari- at, as proposed by Maulvi Kafait-Ullah may

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p166.text:
(2)
be sanctioned and brought into practice:
6) "This meeting requests the members of the Jirge to get people act upon resolution No:5:
7) This meeting urges Government to publish for the information of the public the decision arrived at by the Niamat-Ullah Committee".
8) "This meeting expresses its hatred in regard to the tyrannical treatment which is being meted out to the political prisoners of the Frontier, and warns Government to refrain fro practising tyranny and provoking people".
9) This meeting demands of the Government that half of the revenue of the 'Kharif' crop should be remitted".
A verbatim translation of the speeches made by Abdul Ghaffar Khan at this meeting is herewith attached.
The signatures of the below named persons were taken
on the shorthand notes:-
1. Muhammad Farid Khan of Shah Alim.
2. Faqir Muhammad Khan of Shah Alim.
3. No:713 Hd: Constable Abdul Rauf Khan of the Daud- zai police station.

Musa Khen s/o Sherif Ullah Khan, the Sadar of the Chamkant red-shirts collected about 14/15 red-shirts in uniform and drille them from 3 to 4 P.M. on 19.11.31.
2. P.S.B.H.Singh.
Ubed Ullah Khan s/o Dr. Khan Sahib, after completing his torm of imprisonment, is said to have come to his father's bung- -low, where Abdul Ghaffar Khan also has arrived. Yahya Badshah, the Sadar of Pelosi Attozal, red-shirts, Spin Badshah, Taj Mohd and
Abdul Rehman of Tahkal Bala and Nasar Ullah of Swati went up to see them.
It is learnt that the marriage ceremony of Mir Mohd s/o Gul Mohd, a red-shirt of Sarband, took place in the evening. The processionn returned from Pawaka at 7 p.m. 7/8 red-shirts were with them
3. P.S.Badaber.
Arbab Abdul Aziz Khan of Lendi has not returned from Kohat as yet.
During the investigation of F.I.R. No.124 of 1931, section 302 I.P.C. in which two red-shirts were killed. Mir Hussain Shah "Sadar" of Shahab Khel refused to show the facts he is therefore removed from the "Sadarat" by the red-shirts.
Sayed Sadiq clerk of Deh Bahadur- Red-Shirts in the Malood Sha- -rid, hold at Sorozai Bala, on the night of 19/20.11.31. and in which 10/15 red-shirts, 25/30 others had joined spoke that all should join the red-shirts as the action of red-shirts is for the benefit of every body. Rod-Shirts were also asked by him to k non
keep mane violent till last.
4. P.S.Mattani.
Ajab Gul, the outlaw, is stated to be hanging about Adez 1. of He frequents Bagh, Maryamzai and Mera Telaband. Mr.Wood S.Police on special duty is being informed.
5. P.S.Daudzai.
Fazli Qedir, of Harysha Bala, and Tawab heh, of Konkola, both

both members of the Working Committee, (red-shirts) "Tappa D - -2 1" are stated to have gone after and collected a subscrip, ve of Rs. 15/- from Nawab Khan, Lambardar, who is himself the Sadap [ of the red-shirts. Nawab Khan will collect subscriptions late. from the villagers when the sugar-cane and the rice crops are d ready. These two persons are now busy collecting subscriptions in villages Khazana and Nachappa.
Taj Mohd s/o Mohd Ali Khan of Shah Alam, and Said s/o Abdul Aziz Khan, of Na chappa, both "colonels" of red-shirts, who had gone to Distt: Hazara with Abdul Ghaffar Khan have returned to their respective villagers.
The red-shirts of Mian Gujar have rented a house of Abdul- -Ahad Khan of Takhatabada at Rs. 2/- per mensum for a period of 6 months.

Confidenti
un 20-3-31, Abdul Ghaffar Khan arrived in a tongs
at about 1 p.m. at Deh Bahadur from Tehkal. A reception party of about 100 men were present on the Kohat road After that he went on foot up to the mosque of Muhammad Akbar Khan of Deh Bahadur. After saying his Jums prayers he delivered a speech in the Hujra of Muhammad Akbar 100 Khan. The number of audience was about 600. All of them were from asho Khel, Badaber, Suleman Khel, Shahab Khel Landi, Kaggawala and Peshawar City. The purport of his speech was that he was arrested by the Government and released. He did not know why he was arrested and why released. He was not released at his own request neith- er the Government had presented to him any terms. Most of the leaders were still in jail.
He further advised people to unite and break the chains of slavery, wear red clothes and try to liberate themselves. He exhorted people to leave Government services and serve God, phe Afridis, the Mahsuds, the wazirs, the Mohmands, the Khalils, Daudzai, Hashtnagar etc. were not different people but were one and the same. If one receives an injury the other should also feel. He said that he would visit the village a second time if there was a group of Khudai Khidmatgars there otherwise he won't. He would not visit any village on the invitation of Khans etc., but would do so if in- vited by the khudai khidmatgars. The audience promised to comply with his request. At the dispersal of the meeting Abdul Ghaffar Khan left for Peshawar City to Join the meeting which was to be held at Shahi Bagh.
The following are the names of persons who invite Abdul Ghaffar Khan to Deh Bahadur:-
1. Akram Khan, son of Habib rhan of Beh Baha 2. Sanobar son of cul Faraz of Kaggawala. 3. Ashiq Shah, son of Harif Shah of Deh Bah

Confidential Diary. P.S.Sadar dated 26.11.31.
Abdul Ghaffar Khan arrived Chamkani at about 4.30.p.m., today and was accompanied by 3 hoys and one "Khudai Khidmatgars".
OUR
t being a Thursday, people from the beighbourhood were present in the village in numbers. Hearing of Abdul Ghaffar Khan's arrival, about 700/800 men collected in and around the mosque attached to the Shrine in the vi the villlage, where (in the mosque) Abdul Ghaffar Khan Tenodesfumo w
the
atu
had halted.
Two of the boys first recited a poem and the third recited one separately. Sardar Shah s/o Wali Shah, Abdul Khaliq s/o Sarwar of Ghuha Gujjar then recited a poem. These poems related to independence Then Abdul Ghaffar Khan arose and spoke that order u/s 144
me
idC.P.C. was promulgated and liberty as to convenning meetings taken. Sitting in the mosque he would speak a few words which would be religious and not political. Reciting a verse from the Holy Quran, he translated it and said slavery was a sin, and nothing surpassed it. The country was ours, wealth was ours and the Englishmen enjoyed at the expense of our country and wealth. We should liberate our- -selves with the weapon of fortitude. The men should bear repression with patience. We will certainly be free one day. We should not be glad over meetings, but act with patience. Our Prophet worked out and propagated Islam to the East and West with patience. But our elders in weak spirit and in coward lost their country, freedom and Islam. Afghan should gain this back by the dint of fortitude. In humble words he ended his speech by appealing to the audience to unite and to live by mutual wellbeing and truth.
Musa Khan the "Sadr" of the Chamkani Red-Shirts entertained Abdul Ghaffar Khan at tea, after which the latter left for the City.
Ro
Debaritu.
D.S.P.Sadar 28.11.31.

About 6/7 Rad-shirts, in uniform, of Habaki, P.8.Daudret, accompanied Doli" (marriage prosesston) to China, Hezarkhand, and returned
th it to their village leter. Ho revolutionary cries were raised. For some time, Babu Ilahi, "Bad" of Pakha Ghulam Red-Shirts, hadbeen trying that Pelche should become the centre of the Red-Shirts. This was not accededt to, hence as a protest Bobu Ilahi resigned

Tor dul, retired Captain, of Bohar T.T., now a resident in the Jurisdiction of P.5.Mattant beers enmity towards Yaqin Shah of Pastwanni, T.T. About 5 days ago 1/5 men of Mettani went to Past- wanni to purchase sharponl; and when they had done so and were returning via Bohar T.T., relatives of Tor dul hold them up and confisanted the charcoal, on the grounds that they had purchased the charoonl from their enemies.
People of Aza Khel are furious over this act on the part of the Bohar-Walas, and state, that when the Boher people will be going down to Peshawar through their village, they will take their revenge. P.8.Mattani. dated 28.11.31.
Shahi Khon "Sadr" of the red-shirts of Aza Khol, hed told the people that the eating rice cook as "Khairat" on the death of a person amounts to eating the flesh of swine. This has caused the displeasure of the people of Azakhel, and they are bent upon boy- -cotting the Red-Shirts as a whole. Douran Khan, Lombardar, is taking a prominent part in this; and is trying to take "fitawaa" from the mullahs against the red-shirts, so that no one should join the funer- -al processions of any red-shirt because Shahi Khen spoke against the "Shariat".

CONFIDENTAL DIARY.
2.8.Badaber.
Sh.Pezli Pahim, "nder" of the Red-Shirts is stated
to have ordered all the Red-Shirts under him to report depert ure away from the headquarters, in future. The Red-shirts have not approved of this and have said, they could not comply with it. Beder station, dated the 1/12/31.
From 10.A.M. Red shirts from the Pabbi jurisdiction began to pour in to village Bande Ismail, the number of the Red- Shirts was approximately 200, and others in ordinary clothes 300, About 300 were female.
At about 12"Noon", Abdul Ghafar Khan arrived with Red- Shirts. He first went into the "Centre", at Banda Hallahan. Letter he went to the meeting place. "Sader" of Khoshki, was pre siding. c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p020.text:
Confidential.
The Sub: Inspector, Burj Hari Singh, repots that ob the evening of 19-3-31, Abdul Ghaffar Khan of Utmanzai A arrived in(village) Tehkal, at about 6 p.m. by a motor car. He was received in the Hujra of Taj Muhamad Khan and Patch Muhammad Khan, pleaders of Tehkal. It is said that preparati had been made beforehand and 2 men were sent to Palosi vil- lage to invite villagers to the proposed meeting. After the Khuftan prayers a meeting was held in the Tehkal mosque under the presidency of Abdul Ghaffar Khan. The whole gathering, including the villagers from Palos1, numbered and about 200-250. Majority of them were children,/spectators, chiefly belong to the zeminder class. It is said that in spite of invitations no leading man attended. Two red-shir+ who accompanied Abdul Ghaffar Khan opened the proceedings with a 'Pushto' poem, after which Abdul Ghafur Khan, son of Arbab Juma Khan of Tehkal introduced Abdul Ghaffar Khan to the audience saying that he was the same Abdul Ghaffar Khan who had been sent to prison for serving the nation. Abdul Ghaffar Khan then got up and made a speech in which he appealed for the enlistment of red-shirt volunteers. He also suggested that a Central "Anjuman" should be formed in Tehkal Bala, with its branches all over the ilaqa. He also spoke on unity, and the meeting dispersed in the night. In the morning after breafast, Abdul Ghaffar Khan, accompanied by Taj Muhammad Khan and Fateh Muhammad Khan - paid a visit to Nawab Arbab Dost Muhammad Khan of Tehkal. Before leaving Abdul Ghaffar Khan gave out that he was leaving for Delhi. He and his Khudai Khidmatgars then left for Deh Bahadur.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p202.text:
CONFIDENTIAL.
P.S.SADDAR.
2.12.31.
DIARY
Abdul Ghaffar Khan arrived Phendoo at 2.30. p.m. on 2.12.31. and went straight into the office of the Red-Shirts for inspection. People had assembled for him in the Mosque where he went later. Two lads recited songs. Sterwards Abdul Ghaffar Khan delivered his usual speech. He had recited verses from the Holly Quran first The people were exhorted to practise patience and unity and to help each other. They were told that arrests will take place and that repression would follow; but they should bear it end be patient. This would bring success. About 200/250 persons took part, 50/60 were female. Leaving phendoo, Abdul Ghaffar Khan took the road lending to Chuha Gujar and was obviously going to Peshawar,. But the red-shirts prevailed upon him and he remained for the night. He went to the Iujra of Zakariye Khan and had his ASC dinner there. Later he went over to the Mosque for prayers then he delivered his old speech, which lasted up to 10.30 p.m. He put up at Zakirya Khan's Hujra for the night.
It is stated that Mohd Afzal Khan has been appointed to "serdar" or Argooni (red-shirts). Mohd Kahrer Khan has left off the red-shirts movement. The Red-shirts have camped at Banda Mallahen. This "general Parade" took place and having commenced on 1.12.31. was to last up to 3.12.31.
Poshinwar.
3.12.31.
D.S.P. Saddar.

These people had assemb- led from Shet-Khan Mushtra-Zai, Masho Kael, Shahab Khel, Garhi- Mali Khel and Salman Khel. Fazli Rahim of Sheikhan, Gulab of Garhi Mali Khel, Sattar of Masho Khel, Said Alam of Badaber, Yagub Shah of Bahadar Kil, Mir Hussain of Shahab Khel, Haji- Ghulam Rabani of Sulman Khel, and Musali Khan Sader of the Red- Shirts were present.
Haji Ghulam Rabani recited a poem about independence. Then Bad Shah Gul, a small boy, read a poem. Abdul Ghafar Khan delivered his speech afterwards.
Leaving Shahab Khel, he reached Sulman Khol and went stra- ight into the office of the Red-Shirts. After doing this he left for Sheikh Mahammadi. There i Sheikh Mahamadi, he
went into the office of the Red-Shirts where about 70/80 Red- Shirts were present. A meeting was held in the Mosque of Yaqub- Khan where the office is Located. c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p021.text:
NORTH WEST FRONTIER PROVINCE POLICE
Abstract of Intelligence.
No. 3 dated 28.3.31.
paragrapg 232, 233+
233. Peshawar, 24-3 31.-The red shirts movement has extended to Tehkal Bala (Peshawar) under the leadership of Abdul Ghafur Khan, an expelled student of the Islamia College, Peshawar (paragraph 215), the son of Arbab Juma Khan, a clerk in the office of the Deputy Commissioner, Peshawar,
Eleven men have so far been enlisted as red shirt volunteers, important among them being Muhammad Hashim Khan, another son of Arbab Juma Khan, Zafar Khan, son of Arbab Fateh Muhammad Khan, Pleader, and Said Akhar, a No. X bad character of Tehkal Bala.
The leading Arbabs of Tehkal Bala are said to have sent word to Arbab Juma Khan asking him to prevent his son from taking part in the red shirt movement, but he has replied that he cannot do so as his son is only doing what is good.
A basin filled with red colour is lying in the hujra of Arbab Fateh Muhammad Khan, Pleader, from which the volunteers dye their clothes.
Abdul Ghafur Khan (son of Arbab Juma Khan), Arbab Fateh Muhammad Khan and Taj Muhammad Khan, Pleaders of Tehkal, are inducing people to wear red clothes and enlist as volunteers.
A sister of Arbab Juma Khan is married to Arbab Fateh Muhaminad Khan and Abdul Ghafur Khan is a grandson of Nawab Sir Dost Muhammad Khan of Tehkal Bala.

The Red-Shirts of the Chamkaniland Bazar- Garhi were collected by the best of Drum, in the office The Rod- Shirts were told off to duties in connection with the traffic work No Red-Shirts from the adjoining villages had come on the Thursday. Akbar Shah of Chaghri Matti, a Red-shirt, recited a poem on Liberty.
Mian Fazl-1-Ilahi of Char Guli proposed a resolution condemning the action of the Political agent, Malakand, in maltreating the inhabitants of Swat, Ranizai, etc. Aslam Khan supported this resolution also.
Abdul Cadir Khan then delivered a speech in which he remarked that the Government had left the Coran free, it did not permit people to act according to its injunctions. It took legal action against the picketers on complaints by the prostitutes. According to the Muhammadan law the Muslims were not permitted drinking, adultry, gambling etc., but the Government would not allow them act according to it. Abdul Ahad Khan had been arrested as he asked his countrymen to refrain from sin and crime and tried for their betterment. He had never asked the British to give up their rule but he wanted independence like that enjoyed by the Americans and Egyptions. They wanted to abolish autocratic Government
and to establish democratic Government. Under the injunctions

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p223.text:
(2)
of the Goran all the Muslims were equal and, therefore, de- served a democratic Government. They would have freedom.
Muhammad Yusaf of Ambadher advocated the abolition of bad customs and un-Islamic rites, etc. He said that every Muslim should be God-fearing. They should give up the habit of flattering Government or the Khans, and strive for Inde- pendence.
Gul Muhammad, shopkeeper, of Charsadda read out a poem on freedom, the purport of which was that the foreigners should leave their country which should become free. The "todis" would be put to humilation when freedom was attained and they would have to return to their fold.
Hazrat Shah and Taj Muhammad of Tahkal supported Muham- mad Yusaf and Gul Muhammad and the meeting was concluded with shouts of "Inqilab Zinda Bad" and "Fakhr-i-Afghan" Zinda Bad". Beside those of Daudzai tappa, Red-shirts from Prang, Tahkal, Matta Mughal Khel, etc., also took part in this meeting.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p224.text:
Translation of a vernacular diary reporting the proceed- ings of a Red-shirt meeting held at Dheri village on the night of 17-12-31.
18 at
A Red-shirt meeting was held in the mosque of Dheri village from 9-30 to 11-30 p.m. on 17-12-31. The gathering numbered about 50-60. The meeting was held on pretext of a "Maulud Sharif".
The proceedings were opened by the recitation by a party of Red-shirt boys of a poem which was religious.
Qabil Shah, son of Ahmad Shah, a timber merchant outsiden the Ram Dass Gate, announced to the congregation that a speech would be made by Abdul Quddus, Editor of the "Angar" newspaper.
B
Abdul Quddus came on the stage and after reading some verses from the Qoran explained their meanings to the congre- gation. He said that the "Ashab" (successors of the Prophet) had also undergone troubles but they had remained firm and had born them with Patience. They should follow their ex- example and meet their troubles with Patience and Forebear- ance. The best weapons they had to oppose the Government were Patience and Forebearance. Some people thought that the Red-shirt movement was against the Shariat" but they were wrong. The Red-shirts, who were looked down with contempt, would, if God wished, get the country freed. From about 150 years not only the English but the whole of Europe was enjoying the wealth of their country and they themselves were dying of hunger. They would not, however, allow the British to prey upon their resources any more. The Revenue which was collected from the people should be used for the betterment of the people only, and not squandered on luxur- 1es. The expenses of a Farangi's dog were not less than Rs.60/- - a month, and a separate man was employed to look after it. There were hospitals for their soldiers and dogs but there were none for them (the people). There were many

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p225.text:
(2)
big villages in the Khalil and Mohmand tappas but there was no hospital in any of them. A British soldier was getting
B
n
a salary of Rs.80/- per month but the Indian got Rs.20/- only although he could handle a rifle as well. They (the British) had distributed the Bara water into the Cantts., to please -ing their women (Mem) and the crops of the Zemindars were suffer- heavily. In spite of all this they were realising the Revenue etc., in full. They people had so far been ignorant. Hereto- fore no body took the name of the Pathan, but now the whole Europe wished to have a glimpse of the Red-shirts whose photos were being taken and sent to European countries. Some people asked of what use were the Red-shirts to the Nation. The reply was that all the Government officials in the Fronti er were receiving their salaries from the Revenue collected from the people. The Army was also being maintained on the Revenue. Was it not a success that their salaries had been reduced, and in the Punjab the revenue had been decreased by 60/- per cent? The Red-shirts shoul carry on their agitation as if they relaxed their activities, the whole work would be spoiled. They should take lesson from Europe where every party had its own leader who could guard its L interests to the last.
a
He further said that he had one day been called up by the Rana Sahib who wanted to have/talk with him. He replied all his questions in a fitting manner. This was due to the fact that, by the grace of God, they had now ejected from their minds the fear of the English. In by-gone days they could not utter a word before them out of fear. All this was the result of the Red-shirt movement. They should not fear from any body. Many a great ruler had come and gone. I they (the British) had armies and warships, they (the Peopl leo had weapons like Patience and Forebearance.
The meeting ended about 11-30 p.m.

Inquiries go to show that every Thursday a party of
about 10 Red Shirts is sent away for traffic control around Chamkani Ziarat. Two Red Shirts with a small box on which is inscribed "Subscriptions for Khudai Khidmatgaran", are posted on duty on the road to collect subscriptions from the tonga-walas and their passengers at a distance of about 500 yards from the shrine. They receive whatever they are given. The box containing the money is then produced before Musa Khan "Sadar" of the Chamkani Red Shirts in the evening, who deposi- -ts the cash with one Ganga Singh, a Local cloth-merchant.
The Red Shirts have not yet made any demand on the Shrine Treasury but it is clear that this collection of sub- -scriptions by them will effect the Ziarat Fund badly.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p234.text:
An unfortunate incident occurred in the
hunting field on Sunday the 6th December. An English lady had a bad fall and as seriously injured. She was immediately attended by Major Dimond, 1.M.S., who was also out hunting, and it was decided that she must
be carried to the Grand Trunk Road, 2 miles away for conveyance to hospital from there. Assistance was sought from the village of Kasim close by and eventually a boy of 15 came with a charpoy.
Major Dimond and Colonel Gray placed the lady Villages
on the charpoy and 4 elderlytarted to carry her towards the Grand Trunk Road. when the party reached Kasim village a crowd collected and ordered the bearers to put down the charpoy and this was obeyed. Major Dimond explained to the leaders of the crowd that it was a serious accident and appealed to their good feeling to allow assistance to a lady in distress. One of the leaders a young man of about 28 said that he was a Khudai Khidmatgar ( servant of the public) and that his "Captain had given orders that no assistance was to be rendered. Major Dimond, Colonel Gray and a loyal Khan who was out with the hounds, then started to carry brarers
the charpoy but the previous ers then intervened again, picked up the charpoy and moved off with it. in spite of the jeers and insults shouted at them by the crowd.
When the party reached the G.T,Road the boy who k had brought the charpoy said that he was afraid to return to his village as he would be punished by the Red Shirts. Major Dimond who has toured the whole of the Peshawar District in the last 8 years on medical duties states that he has never before encountered such unreasoning hostility on the part of Pathans who are renowned for their hospitality.

The Methra Police report as follows.
on 9.4.31. at about 10 .m. A
Abdul Ghefur Khan s/o Arbab
Juma Khan of Takkal Bala accompanied by Ghulam Nabi s/o Allah Dad, Ghulam Sarwar e/o Ghazi Khen, Bahadur Shor of AloAmin Jan e/o Fateh Khan and Ghulam butcher of Kochian arrived at Tongastand of village Mathra. Abdul Ghafur Khan delivered a lectura for fifteen minutes.The purport of the lecture was that unite yourself and decided your domestie quarrels. After that he proceeded to Shahi.Outside the village Usman s/o Yar Kohd, Sheria s/o Abdur Rahim, Abdul Jalil s/o Aslem and Tahir met him. Nobody else except these persons attended the meeting. In the Elangan mosque he delivered the same Aecture.
The Doudani Police report that Ali Ahmad Captain of red shirts of Laram with his party numbering about 40 went to Pakha,but there was no meeting and returned to their home in the evening.
Abdulla Khan of Dang Lakhti is making propagande at Oidar, Khawajgi and Niami and is inducing the people to have themselves enlisted as Khudai Khidmatgers.Abdulla Khen with 30 red shirts went to Adezai P.S.Batagram to attend a mest -ing.
The B.H.Singh police report that on 9.1.31. about 50 red shirts made demonstrations with beat of drums at Tahkal- -Bola.
Latohel

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p037.text:
Confidential.
Special Diary, D/- 13-4-31.
4°
A meeting of about 2000 persons, including about 500 red-shirts, was held near Abdur-Rahima village, in the Sadar Sub: Division, under the presidency Maulvi Muhammad Ji, Imam Sahib of the Nawab of Tehkal. The organisers of this meeting were Babu Ilahi and Mulla Ashur of Pakha, and some Utmanzai volunteers. Muhammad Baman a 'Lieut' of the red-shirts of Charsadda read a 'Pushto' poem describing the tyramies of the British. Maulvi Abdul Rauf of Gullo- zai, Daudzai p.s., read a poem advising people to unite and help the red-shirts. Muhammad Yusaf of Ambadher made a speech saying that they were only on a truce with the Government, and that the enlistment of red-shirts was not unlawful. He also advised people to give up indulging in religious controversies. Mian Ahmad Shah Bar-at-Law made a speech saying that the Khans were trying hard to bring disunion among the people and that they should not be trusted. Referring to the arrests of the red-shirts at Babra, he advised them to carry on their work calmly and coolly. In future they should not hold meetings without the previous sanction of the Central Committee at Utman- zai. The truce with the Government was a temporary one. They would abide by its terms but the Government would break it very soon, as it would refuse to fulfil their demand for freedom. The Karachi Congress Session had decided that the Frontier Province should be put on an equal footing with the rest of India. They were trying to secure the release of those political prisoners who were still in jail. Abdul Ghaffar Khan, who had arrived during the progress of the meeting, addressed the people saying that it was not the time for making speeches but some practical work should be done. He questioned the

A menting wee held today on the Sahbi Road going to Akber Pura under the presidentship of Tej Bohd s/o shed Mohd of Bu ni.Abdul Ghaffar Khen with certain rod shirts reached there by a car and two lorries from the direction of Pabbi at 3 p A took part in the mosting.He was ressived in the meeting by e procession of red shirts. The total attendance was about 3000, of whom 300 were women of Badni; 50 Hindus of Budini & Akbar Pure and the rest audience were of Gallozei, Memusel, Mian Gujar, Dilazak Jele Bola, Akbar Pura, Kurvi, Banda Mahelian, Benda Arbaban, Abdur Rahima, Fatto, Chabian and zokhi villages. The following subjects were dealt with in the meeting moo- ting.
A meeting was opened by Heulvi Abdur Ruef of dullosat, sho rocito Curan Sharif and then in thes curse of his addrear on the religious questions, he declared the wearing of red shirts to be "sunnat".
Two or three ret shirt boys recited poems eulogising relig -1ons and freedom. In the poems it was further pressed that wo -ity, amorifices, and giving up bad habits will result in the freedom of nation & the country, 1.0.good habits and conduct are the indispensable for making the land free.
Wiser lus ein s/o Keria Ullah of Bende Wehellen in the cour -so of his epooch on freedom after thenking the "Pokhrel Afghe en fr his joining the mating said that young men must come out in the field for working for freedom and unite together to have the country freed and that any body who attempted to eranto a row in the ecurge of the epe eh will be a today or a doverment men.llo further said that a nation enn achieve freedom with unity just as -ing Aman lich then was exp❤ walled from his country in spite of having areoplanes, gune ote. by his nation.
Mohd Idria of Bends Schallen in the course of his specch anid that "Pokhr-1-Afghan being indianosed, he the speaker would lay before then certain facts on his behalf.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p041.text:
He said that a nation can be that nation and men who can wield authority over their country, their property and questions relating to "Sharam". By Shares, he meant the matters relating to women.He also pointed out that the people who tried to impress upon the
red shirts that all of them were not officers alike or that their de resemble that of a woman are the Government mon.To create dimunity
mong Hindus and Muslims and to create disagreement between Ulams and the public is a work of Government for which they, the Govern ment are trying their best.
In the course of his speech the "Fakhr-1-Afghan" said that our elders could get nothing out of the Govt.by crouching and cringing and in this their elders i.e.Khans, Arbabs and Nawabs were on the wrong path. All that we have been given by truce or that the Afgh- -an and Muslims will get is due to the sacrifices of the people of Utmanzai, Takkar, Peshawar and Bannu. We have paid a very heavy price for this treatment in the form of the blood of our young men and will have to pay further such prices for it.I want you to in- -crease the number of red shirts and thus to make known to the Govt. that the young men are prepared themselves in great numbers If we get nothing within the prescribed period,we shall have to put in a non-violent fight for freedom without the use of arms. This means that we will be the oppressed and God helps the oppre -ssed. The country and its wealth are ours which the foreigners use for their own enjoyments while our infants and children go about undressed and without food. Are not you people,pleased to see that we are trying to get bread for ourselves and for the coming generations. I implore you to cast away the ill-feelings existing between you for at least a period of ten years and to create uni- -ty and sympathy among yourselves. If this fails to result in s benefit to you, you should work on the old line.The meeting ended with cries of "Long Live Fakh-ri-Afghan" and the cries of Allah-o. Akber.Abdul Ghaffar Khan went twoards Budni to inspect the office and then started towards Utmanzai via Peshawar in a motor.

lice Department, Confidential.
1931.
Special Diary, D/-1-5-31.
Peshawar District.
A meeting of about 80-90 persons was held in the mosque of Sahibzadagan, Masho Khel village, on 28-4-31. A resolution protesting against the distribution of the Bara water was passed. It was also proposed that the Deputy Commissioner and Revenue Commissioner should be approached in this connection. Maulvi Abdul Rahim of Peshawar City advocated unity and added that Mahatama Gandhi was their leader in the present movement which had been started to liberate the country. He had made a truce with the Government and one of its terms was that all the political prisoners would be released. The Maulvi was sorry to say that Haji Muhammad Amin and 8 the pol: prisoners belonging to the Malakand Agency had not been released
The following persons were the conveners of this
meeting:-
Sanobar of Kaggawala.
2. Mir Alim of Bazid Khel.
3. Maulvi Abdul Rahim of Peshawar City.
4. Morad Khan of Landi Akhun Ahmad.
5. Abdul Majid, son of Abdul Manan of Kaggawala.
6. Said Amir Shah of Mussazai.
7. Murtaza Shah, son of Tahir Shah of Kaggawala.
Sanobar Hussain is said to have enlisted 32 red-shirt volunteers. Some of the names shown in his list are be- lieved to be fictitious and to have been added to swell the list with a view to induce people to become red-shirts Haji Abdul Rahim, Treasurer, Anjuman-1-Zemindaran, Masho Khel, did not attend the above meeting. It is re- ported that he is thinking of withdraw his name from the 'Anjuman'.
The guests were entertained by Muhammad Hassan of Masho Khel.

485. Peshawar, 27-4-31.-A nephew of Hamid; Ullah, Butcher, of Masho Khel, died on 24 April 1931. Many villagers joined his funeral. Sanobar Hussain (paragraph 480) of Kaggawala and Sayed Mir Badshah (paragraph 480) of Mussazai also joined. They gathered the people in the mosque of Sahibzadagan. It was proposed to form an Anjuman-i- Zamindaran at Masho Khel. Saidan Shah, son of Musharraf Sahibzada, was appointed President, Ghulam Habib, Secretary, and Abdur Rahim (paragraph 480, son of Akbar Khan, Treasurer.
A red shirt committee was also formed by the above named office-holders. The following persons were enlisted as red shirt volunteers :-
(1) Muhammad Jamil, son of Hamid, of Masho Khel.
(2) Bashir Ahmad, son of Mulla Shewa, of Masho Kbel. (3) Umarai, son of Faqir, of Masho Khel.
(4) Sherdil, son of Haroon, of Masho Khel.
(5) Fateh, son of Sardar, of Masho Khel.
(6) Abdul Wadud, son of Mahmud, of Masho Khel.
(7) Fazal-i-Rahim, son of Ado, of Masho Khel.
The Masho Khel people consulted among themselves and decided that when the tahsildar came for realising the additional police charges, they should refuse to pay the money. They should first approach the authorities for the abolition of the additional police. This was proclaimed in the village by a beat of drum.
2830.

489. Peshawar, 1-5-31.-It is reported that Khanan, Awal Din, Badshah. Mir Afzal, Wahid Gul and Sher Ullah, red shirts of Dab village, Daudzai Police Station, hal built three sham tombs in the names of Muhammad Afzal Khan of Kareri, Muhammad Zaman Khan of Gul Bela and Muhammad Amir Khan of Babozai, who had recently received rewards from the Government. On the sub-inspector's visiting the spot all the abovenamed red shirts apologised and pulled down the graves.
490. I. B., N. W. F. P., 1-5-31.-About fifty red shirts of Pabbi and Khalsa Ilaqa went to offer Id greetings to Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan (paragraph 486) on 30 April 1931. On their return from Utmanzai they paraded in the main bazaars of the city from Katcheri Gate to Edwards Gate in a form of procession. They had a drum with them.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p055.text:
Peshawar Saddar
554. I. B., N. W. F. P., 4-5-31.-In a meeting held at Paoka (Peshawar Saddar) on 2 May, at 6-30 p.m., Abdul Ghafur Khan (paragraph 503) of Tahkal asked the audience (numbering about 180) to enlist themselves in the red shirts and Congress movements.
In a meeting (attended by 200 men) held at Sufed Dheri on 3 May, at 9 p.m., Abdul Quddus, Qadiani (Ahmadi) exhorted the audience to register their names as red shirts.
555. Peshawar, 8-5-31.-On 4 May 1931 some of the more prominent red shirts at Akbarpura villages were to be seen in the bazaar of that village, telling the people not to buy foreign goods of any kind and only to use swadeshi articles. They also said that the price of meat, Dal, etc., were too high in the bazaar and produced a list showing the correct prices of each article with the threat that unless the shopkeepers complied with this list their shops would be picketed.
On 6 May 1931 the villagers of the four Zakhi villages collected in Akbarpura and later in the day came to the Nowshera Tahsil in the form of a deputation to ask for the removal of the punitive police post located at Akbarpura, the cost of which they state they are unable to pay.
A similar plan was adopted by the Urmar villagers, who two days previously produced a written petition before the Assistant Commissioner giving reasons for their inability to pay the cost of the punitive police post at Urmar and stating that the Urmar Post should cease to be a punitive post.
It is remarkable that both these villages should at the same time adopt a similar attitude as regards meeting the cost of their respective punitive police posts. In each case the deputation of villagers was accompanied with cries of "Inqilab Zinda Bad, " etc., although no red clothing was to be seen. Both villages deny that any such cries were raised or that they had come on the instructions of any one else, but merely to lay their case before the Assistant Commissioner. It seems likely that this question of the cost of the two additional posts will be turned into a political weapon.
283685
2830-RS

A red-shirt meeting was held between Sper Sang and Nur Garh villages on 12-5-31. About 2000 persons from R$ Kafur Dheri, Shahi, Chaghri Matti, Sher Dad Garhi,
Kochian, Allo K1111, Tehkal and Palosai villages attended. The following leading red-shirts also joined with their followers:-
1. Ayub Khan, son of Gul Muhammad Khan of Chaghri Matti, with 20 red-shirts and 60 others.
2. Said Akbar and Gul Akbar, sons of Mir Ahmad of Sherdad Garhi, with 24 red-shirts and 80 others. 3. Mulla Yasin 'Sadar', and Amin Jan 'Captain' of Kochian, with 51 red-shirts and 100 others.
4. Ghulam Sarwar, son of Ghazi, Ghulam Nabi, son of All Dad, Captains of Allo Killi, with 80 red- shirts and 50 others.
5. Wali Muhammad, son of Faqir Muhammad, and ahir and adir, sons of Abdul Rahim of Shahi Bala.
6. Abdul Ghafur Khan, son of Arbab Juma Khan, and
Bahadur Khan, son of Shah Pasand of Tehkal, with with two hundred red.shirts from Tehkal and Palosai villages.
The parties from allo Killi, Chaghri Matti and
Kochian were accompanied by five flags.
a
Arbab 9 Abdul Ghafur Khan made a speech advocating unity and saying that the red-shirts were the well-wishers of the people. All Paths were breth-?& ren. They should refrain from committing crime, back- biting, robbery, gambling, debauchery, etc. Abdul Ghaffar Khan, whom he called 'Bacha Khan' (King) had undergone great hardships for the sake of the people as a result of which 12000 Hindus and Muslims had been released from the jails and were now again serving Should
their country. The people imas not fear Lambardars, Zaildars, Jagirdars, Khans, Arbabs, etc., as they were servants of the 'Sahibs' and had no respect for

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p061.text:
(2)
"Shariat". Their 'Shariat' was the Indian Penal Code.
ons
The Sahibs" were leading luxuriant lives on the re- sources of the people which went to them in the form of land revenue, etc., while the people have not even dry bread to eat. They had come to rule over them from a very far country but had unity amongst them, and similarly if the people promote ote unity among themselves their condition would improve. The red-shirt leaders were undergoing hardships for the sake of the people. He regretted that none of the villagers of Sper Sang had enlisted as red-shirt but they were, on the other hand, opposing them and hating the f red dress One Abdul Karim of Panam Dheri asked the speaker to close the meeting and said that these question would be discussed in another meeting.
At this the meeting dispersed and people proceeded to their respective villages.

About 400 to 500 peop of villages beikh Mohammed1, Shehab Khel, Sheikhon, Ehel, Chari Veli Khel attentel the meeting, including 112 rei ebirte in uniform. Three women slso attendel thi meeting.
Bahadur Khan a red shirt of Tahkol recited a pushto poem in praise of National 19.
A Peshaveri youth aged about 14/15 years, whose nome could not be ascertained got up and said that the people should liberate themselves from the bondage of slavery and should not afraid of the Khana and Malike.
lu
Maulvi Abdul Qalyum of knalil Tapa got up and read
some Verses of the Holy Quran and alvocated unity.
Abdul Majid, & red shirt of Sufed Dheri recited a Pushto poem, exhorted the audience to abandon negligence and to wake up.
Abdul Ghaffar, a red shirt of Tehkel Bala, read a Pushto poem in which he explained the meaning of slavery by saying that in their own country they had no control of their property and this was slavery.
Ashaq Shah of Behadur Killi who was wrapped in
haddar got up and thanked the red shirts for their
attending the meeting. He said that Phan Abdul Ghaffar Khai was engaged in an urgent work and therefore he could not
attend the meeting.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor President then got up ani said the the Khalil and/ohmands were brothers and they should unite. He said that on account of the exersions of the red chirts their condition has been improved. He said that they were looked upon es dogs by the English before this but their behaviour has changed. The Government's work is to disunite us. He urged the hearers to open committees in each and every villaze and enlist sa Khudai Khidmatgers,

hose duty will be to wear home-made clothes and thus
benefit their country people. Khudai Khidmatrers should hove a good charecter. He said that they were starving and were unable to pay their land revenue while the Britishers were living lexuriously and had ample stock of bread ani butter. He exhorted the audience to set their country free by non-violence and prayed for the release of Mohd Amin A sad of sulenkbel. He satisfied his father and that rond Amin will soon be released. Special Diary, b/- 23-5-31.
1. A meeting of the red-shirts was held at shahab kh village on 17-5-31. Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan of Tehkal attended this meeting with about 40-60 red-shirts from Tehkal and Palosal villages. Azam Khan, Anwar Khan, Mah- mud Jan and Ahmad Gul, leading red-shirts of Sufeld Dheri were also present with about 30 or 40 of their followers. 2. on 16-5-31, Abdul Ghafur Khan, son of Arbab Juma Khan of Tehkal visited Pakha Ghulam village with three com- and Mullah Ashur panions on the invitation of Babu Ilahi/of that village. They formed a red-shirt procession and XXXXXXX%% visited rattu, Abdul Rahima, Wad pagga, Budhu and Garhi Baloch Abad villages to induce people to become red-shirts V3. The red-shirts of Pakha and Budhu villages propose to hold a meeting on the return of Abdul Ghaffar Khan from his tour of the Kohat and Bannu Districts.
A meeting was held at Chamkanni village on 18-5-31 by Abdul Ghafur of Tenkal. Red-shirts from Budhu, Tarnab, budni, Urmur and Chuha Gujar villages attended. Lal Bad- Shah, and Ganga singh, shopkeeper of Chamkanni, were also pressent. Abdul Ghafur, mullah Yusaf, and bahadur khan made speeches advocating the use of Khaddar and asking people to become red-shirts. Mulla Gul Jamal read a Pushto poem o on the same subjects.

Internal Section Diary dated 25-5-31.
A meeting on behalf of the red shirts was held at Ziarat Shahidan near Bazid Khel, P.S.Badaber on 24-5-31, from 1-30 to 4-30.p.m., which was attended by about 5/6000 people from the surrounding villages, including 5/600 red shirts, 30/40 local Congressites, 50/60 members of the Anjuman-i-Zamindaran and 70 females.
Mulla Khan Mir, recited a poem in Pushto saying that the time has gone when the Afghans were afraid of the English. They (Rathans) should refrain from fighting with each other. The red shirts demanded the liberty of the country etc.
Gul Akhmad on of the red shirts who accompanied Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan recited a poem saying that differences should be sink down and they should be united.
Abdul Malik recited a poem saying that a pathan could call Pathan himself when he will get liberated his country. 2 red shirts of 14/15 years, companions of Abdul Ghaffar Khan recited a poem. They said that they were not afraid of the jail etc., of the Govt: Ch! 'Ghaddar' (English) leave the country happily. There is none worst than you. Hazrat Shah, resident of Ilaga Badaber recited a poem saying that they should not be afriad of Khans and Maliks. Abdul Ghaffar Khan after reciting some versus from Coran delivered a speech. He said that they were slaves and have got no power to spent theor money and are fighting with each other. They have no regard for their nation and we were earning money by back biting. No British will be prepared to go against his nation. Ch brethren build your houses. He appealed them (his hearers) to come to the field if they were really Pathans and get liberated their country. He remarked that they (Pathans of the Ilaga) were dying on account of the sacricity of the water by the English were making their gardens but the water of the *Bara' He asked to sacrifice their lives in the interest of the country. They have got no power at all. The British were killing them for the sake of the money. The red shirts

2830 A
Tn arnol
were wearing red clothes only that they should be recognised. A Khudai Khidmatgar could wear his uniform when a a Govt: servant could wear his uniform. The red shirts will not rest till their country wes liberated. They have gird up their loins to do so.
The following 4 resolutions were unanimously carried out in the meeting:-
Moved by Sanobar Hussain and seconded by Amir Mohd.
1) This meeting requests the Govt: to remit the land revenue like other provinces. 60 % revenue should be remitted and 40% should be charged in the form of crop instead of cash. The rates of crops have been reduced hence the land revenue should be drcreased also.
2) This meeting brings into the notice of the Frontier Enquiry Committee that the general public did not like the Frontier laws and that complete Reforms should be introduced to this Province.
3) That this meeting looks with pleasure that the nation abide by the agreement held between M.Gandhi and
Lord Irwin.
4) That this meeting announces the boycott of the Inquiry Committee and requests the public not to participate in it.
Resolutions No.2, 3 and 4 were seconded by Mulla Khan Mir, M.Abdul Rahim and Amir Mohd. They made brief remarks while they seconded.
Ghulam Samdani tailor also recited a poem of national
Chter on liberty.

1271
A meeting was held to-day from 11-50 a.m. to 12-30 pm in Chamkenni, under the presidentship of Khan Mir Hilali, General Secretary of the City Congress Committee, Peshawar. Ganza Singh, a shop-keeper of chamkanni had organised this meeting. About 5000 people of the villages of Police stations Purj Hari Singh, Nathra, Radeber, Daudzal, Sedder and Pabbi had attended the meeting. including about 500 red shirts in uniform and 40 women of Tahkal side.
Rauf
National poems in Pushto were recited by Abdul/of
Golozai, Saifur Rahman of Sufaid Dheri, and a third man whos
name could not be ascertained.
At 12-30 p.m. the meeting was adjourned for the sake
of offering after-noon prayers.
At 1-45 p.m the proceedings of the meeting were commenced in ane open place towards the west of Chamkanni village, Khan Mir Hilali presided.
First of all Saifur Rahman of Sufaid Dheri got up
and read a national Pushto poem.
Khan Mir Hilali, the President then got up and appealed the people for maintenance of peace. He said that the meeting was a peaceful meeting and if any-body was found to create disturbance, he will be expelled from the meeting under the order of the President. He directed red- shirts to collect sticks, spears and axes from the specta- tors which they complied.
He put up the following three resolutions and said that the resolutions which he was going to put in the capacity of a pretdent of the meeting should be considered as having passed and need not be seconded:-
1) That this meeting declares sorrow on the demise of Raja of Mahmud Abad and consider his death as a blow for the Muslim community.
2) This meeting declares its pleasure and satisfaction that the public are strictly observing the conditions of Gandhi Trwin pact.
3) As the income of the Auqaf of the Shrine of Mian mar Sahib is being spent illerally, therefore this meeting proposes that this should be spent over religious education only.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p086.text: The President declared that the Punjab Government had announced a reduction of 60 % in the lund revenue and that a similar reduction would be quite acceptable to the people of this Province also. Addressing the red-shirts - he said that as Khan Abdul. Ghaffar Khan is too busy a man his participation in meetings should never be announc ed beforehand unless and until he personally promises to attend. Such announcements create misunderstanding and disappointment among the public.

Enquiries made on the spot go to show that a
meeting, under the presidency of Mir Hussain Shah of Shah Khel was held in the Shrine of Mian Haji, village Sarozai, P.S. Badaber on 31.5.31. About 10,000 men
including 3000 red shirts participated in the meeting.

of

Out of the persons mentioned above Sayed Shah is a Jamadar, Abdul Rauf is a Thanadar, Sultan Shah is a Subedar of the Reds. Abbad Shah, Mohayuddin Shah and Yukub Shah are red shirts. Ashiq Shah is the President of the Reds of Tappa Mohmand while Sanobar Hussain and Sayed Amir Shah are members of Naujawan Bharat Sabha. No body admits of Afridis having taken part in
the meeting. Sayed Ashiq Shah is said to be a link between Afridis and Tappa Mohmand that is he is in touch with Afridis.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p092.text:
Police Department. Confidential.
Sadday sun
1931.
Peshawar District
15th
2/2
information has been received that 11 or 12 armed Afridis came to village Sheikh Muhammadi 8 or 9 days ago. They spent the day in Sarbaland khan, Lambar- dar's garden. Torsam, the X% elder son of sarbiland suggested that a report regarding the visit of these Afridis should be made to Arbab Muhammad Akram Khan but the latter did not approve of this suggestion fearing the arrival of the military which would result in the destruction of their garden. The Afridis were fed by Sarbiland. They then interviewed Musalli, son of Amin Knan of Sheikh Muhammadi, from where they went to Deh Bahadur to see Ashiq Shah. Nothing about them has been heard afterwards.
Note by S.S.P.- I have instructed D.S.P., Sadar, to take action against Sarbiland if this report | is verified.
Shamroz Khan, Lambardar of Kaggawala informed Bada- ber Police that a meeting was held in his village on 3-6-31 at which about 30 red-shirts were enlisted with
following as their office-holders:-
Sabz Ali, son of raiz-Ullah..
M. Ghulam Habib, son of Mukarram. Ghulam Haider, son of razal.
Sadar Niab Sadar Drummer
The meeting was arranged by Murtaza and Muhammad Alim of kaggawala. Aslam Khan of Bazid Khel and Murad Khan of Landi Akhun Ahmad were also present. Aslam Khan spoke on unity and urged people to enlist as red-shirts Sabz Ali, the Sadar, in whose Hujra enlistments were made, is brother of Faqir Lambardar.

c_red_shirt_3_saddar_1931_p093.text:
SECRET
Copy of a report dated 29-8-31, by the Deputy Superinten dent of Police, Sadar Sub: Division, reshawar.
As regards the report that a gang of Akka Khel Afridis had entered B.T. on 27-8-31, I made secret enquiries and in- formation has been received from a reliable informer that the were about 8 persons of the Malikdin Khel tribe who had brought some correspondence for Dr: Khan Sahib from Tirah. The correspondence was delivered to Dr: Khan Sahib through Shams-ud-Din of amar-ud-Din Garhi, where the Afridis put up for the night, or for the part of the night. Shams-ud-Din brought for them some bandages for 2 wounded persons from Dr. Khan Sahib. They left amar-ud-Din Garhi next day.
Further information regarding the above-mentioned party has been received this morning for the same informer to the effect that they had been sent by Khushal Khan of T.T. and have gone to spin Khara Mohmand T.T. to see sahib (the broth- er of late Mullah Sahib of Manki). It is said that they have been sent by Khushal Khan to spin Khara for money.
A. I.G., Police, N.W.F.P.
2. D.C., Peshawar.

778. Peshawar, 7-6-31.-Muhammad Ali and Painda Khan of Masho Khel and ten others of the same village were noticed discussing the recent arrests of the Masho Khel Lambardars who had refused to pay the land revenue. They were overheard saying that all the villagers have made up
their minds to go to jail if those who had been arrested recently were not set free. Anybody who refused to join them would be fined Rs. 10.
It is also stated that about 1,000 villagers went to Landi and interviewed Arbab Muhammad Akram Khan in connection with the above arrests. They declared before the Arbab their intention of courti rrest if those who had been arrested for the non-payment of revenue were not eased. On being told that the abovementioned lambardars had been released ersed.
