war in the near future.
Under such circumstances they were
duty bound to free India as liberty was their birth right.
How could they, he observed, be such shameless creature as not
to fight for the liberty of their own country when Britain
was fighting for the liberty of foreigners.
Proceeding, he said that now time had come when Congress would demand from them to come into the field and get ready to free their country.
Continuing, he said that Muslims prepondrated in the Frontier, so the Muslim League of India could under no sircumstances tolerate that 95% of Muslims should follow the Congress programme. For the last 10 2/3 years strenuus
efforts had been made to wean away the Frontier Muslims
from Congress, so that it may not claim that it was a Congress province.
Referring to the Muslim League of the Frovince, he said that every body was well-aware that its supporters had spent their whole life in the service of the English and did their best to oppose the K.K. movement and placed obstacles in the way, but failed to deceive the masses.
If any C.I.D., reporter was present in the meeting, he would like them to convey his message to Sir Aurther Persons that after tremendous sacrifices and strong agitation the ka black law of the country (F.C.R) was only suspended and not repealed. However, when Congress ministry came into office,
a propaganda was set a foot that crime had increased, but in reality Wazirs had desorted to orime owing to the Foward Policy. Their Premier, he said, reintroduced the F.C.R., but he also decided that only rare cases be tried under it and he also took steps that corruption was stamped out. After

c_red_shirt_pesh_1939_40_p085.text:
resignation of the Congress ministry en irresponsible Government dame into office which gave out to enforce the F.C.R. as it had been introduced by the Congress Govern- ment. However, now the difference wes that the present jiro mebere pronounodd wrong judgements and then repealed them thamaax themselves.
Corruption, he observed, had become rampant and the Government was in the know that how the xnxx same
persons were called upon to eat as jirge members against whom agitation had been launched as they did not deal out justice. However, now Congress had ordered that its members will not sit in jirges
Proceeding, he said that Congress had also asked Muslim League not to sit in jirgas, but it did nothing. However, now Muslim League had desolved against the jirga system and under such circumstances when Muslim Loague and Congress both were against it then he could not understand how Sir Aurthor Farson could venture to tremple upon the legitimate feelings of the masses It behoved the present Government to suspend the FR. otherwise they would take some tangible steps and, in it on the right path as they did n in the past. just possible that they leunohed such an agitatundia, hould
will have to obtain hit the permission of the
National Congress.
Referring to Government servants, he se
ce was
they had again adverted to their old methods e had realised that manses could not stand in the but they should note that the phase of the world h undergo a change. However, if the English labour under

c_red_shirt_pesh_1939_40_p086.text:
the impression that they could live on their strength in the world then it was entirely wrong because Indie had decided to end
Imperialism in the whole world.
Proceeding, he said that it me far from their mind to take undae advantage of the present war, but their position was that the country was their and xxx owing to their negligence the English were deriving benefit from it.
Continuing, he said that they had come to the decision thet they were not prepared to accept Provincial Autonomy without majority in the Centre.
Congress, he said, had taken the wise step in laying down office in provinces. It really blundered in coming to an understanding with Muslim League because it was composed of those who were not prepared to come to my understanding at all. He knew Muslim Leaguers very well and his conviction was that they always followed the English. It was really useless, he pointed out, to except such a party to free the country.
Congress alone, he pointed out, will take practical steps. Proceeding, he said that he had issued a press statement asking Hindus and M. Gandhi, not to open negotiation with Muslim League, but permit Abul-Kalam Azad to do so.
Continuing, he said that he visited many places in India, but the Frontier province was more awake then others and should the time come the Government will realise that this province was more of congress than others.
The rest of his speech was in the above key.
Sadhu Singh Socialist of Peshawar City, said that the
present war broke out when Congress was in office, but since its resignation rates of commodities had considerably gone up. During the regime of Congress Government the youths of

c_red_shirt_pesh_1939_40_p087.text:
the country protested against high rates and the Government fixed the rate of wheat-flour at 94 seors per rupee, but with the oing out of their populer ministry they had become viotim of untold repression.
On the top of it, he said, people were being hauled up for making water in streets. However, he would admit that the above action was commendable, but the Government was equally bound to construct urinals throughout the city and it was a pity that the Committee had neither erected them nor had taken steps to improve the condition of roads.
Referring to recruitment, he said that they were not prepared to supply recruits as long as their country was not free Referring to fines on Tanga drivers, he said that in the beginning they (Tanga Drivers) were fined 4/-/- each, but
now they were fined 50/- rupees each.
Similarly, he pointed out that people who made water
in the streets were fined up to Rs. 5/-
The rest of his speech was in the above strain.
Milap Singh Azad speaking last said that the above
speaker had remarked that they would supply recruits after Indie had become free, but he would say why they should do so even then for the sake of the English, as Russia and Germeny wore nedther their enemies nor had killed their forfathers.
Proceeding, he said that they did not beg liberty from the English, but wanted to have as it could not be attained by begging. They had to attain it under all circumstances whether
the English may give it by hook or crook.
Regarding recruitment, he said, Congress, would decide would
it itself and it was also give its opinion whether after the
freedom of India they will allow the English to live in India
or expell them.

'Ingalab', he said, was coming with the full speed and
in a few days or months the map of the world will be changed. Some people, he pointed out, stood in the ray of Mujehids who wished to come into ole oh with the English. However, hazi Mujahids, he said, were fighting the British Government in view of their forward policy. If hazis should not commit dacoities then what they should do, as they did not wish that the English should step on their holy land. They were brave people and had risked their lives in fighting against the English. It was really marvelous, he observed the t in the presence of the Police and Constabulary, Wazirs arranged to get access in to Bannu City and kidnap ped Hindus. He would say that the k hands of the English worked behind such a country and they were responsible for the dacoities because they wanted to represent the Hindus that these people (tribes) mill surely devover them if they will take the name of liberty any more. By resorting to such tactics, he said, the English wished to entangle them. On the one hand, he said, the Mujahids Ghazis were killed, thile on the wht other hand poor Indians were meetin. the same fate, as British soldiers were seldom killed.
Addressing Hindus and Sikhs, he said, that it was a fact that they were in a minority and therefore, he would request them to sucrifice themselves for the sake of 95% Muslims and frer India It was due to A..K. that they had been sivon an opportunity to see M.Gandhi, Pandit Nehru and others.
Eulogising Dr. Khan Sahib, he said, that when justice
demanded, he sent his own son to jail.
Concluding, he spoke on 'Inagalab' and said that the
struggle was near at hand and they will have to resort to C.D., to
free India. The rest of his speech was in the above strain.

The president of the meeting in the opening speech raid that the present meeting was to sympathise with the people of Turkey who had suffered heavily in the recent earthquake.
Sadhu Singh Socialist recited poon on liberty.
Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi speaking next said that in the recent Earth quake there were about 80000 causalties out of which 30000 had ri died and 16000 had been perished.About 90 villeges, he pointed out had been ruined and the people were in a very deplorable condition. Oring to these tremendous losses, he said, Turke deserved every sympathy, not only that they were Jusline, but they were human being. Turks, he stid, vere very brave people and they were ruling over a part of Europe.
Continuing, he se id that many European powers wished to cultivate the friendship of Turks and His Majesty the King Emperor paid a visit to Constertinople in the same connection. Germany, he pointedout, made efforts to win the cooperation of the Turks in the present war, but failed.
Froceeding, he said that & the present juncture when the atmosphere was very cloudy the loss sustained by Turkey was very deplorable and he would request that people should collect funds and he hoped that the Municipal Committee Peshawar would make sone donation out of its treasury.
He concluded his speech by rax moving a resolution of sympathy and requesting the Hurbipal Committee and Congress Cormities to make tremendous efforts for the collection of funds.
Milep Singh Audhile supporting the resolution,said, had they democracy in the country, their president would have sent a telegrum of sympathy with purse of 4/5 crore £ for the Turkish people, but it was pity that Indians were slaves

IPs. Daily Diary 41. 616 May 1950
CONGRESS COMMITTEE PESHAWAR CITY.
107]
A private meeting of the City Congress Committee vas held at 8.30 p.m. on 4.5.40 in their office under the presidency of lakin Abdul Jalil Nadvi. The following participated in the above meeting.
1. Mohd Afzal Kotanger.
2. Mohd Usman Naswari.
Kundan Lal.
Abdul Karim alias Charkhi.
and four other members.
After discussion the following decisions were
arrived at:-
1. That a procession in honour of the Mark Martyrs of Bazar-a- Kalan should be taken out on 31-5-40. It should leave Ninak Mandi Park at 2. p.n. antafter going through the Qissakhanibazar, Mundi beri, Ikatut, Gun should end outside the Gunj Gate where wreaths should be placed on the graves of the Martyrs. From there the procession should come back to Bazar-e-Kalan via Gorkhetri and stop near Martyrs Monument where again wreaths should be placed on the Monument and speeches should be delivered. From there the procession should go to Chauk Yadgar where at 8.30 p.m. a public meeting should be held.
2. A general meeting of the members of the City Congress Committee should be called in order to discuss the distribution af and signing of the Satyagurha Pledge-forms by the members of the various ward-committees.
3. The application given to the City Congress Committee by the Saraf occupants of the Saraf-Chhapors (huts) in connexion with the ordersof the Revenue Commissioner ordering the removal of the Chhapers (huts) should be put up before the committee on 11-5-40. In this application the Parafs have requested the congress committee to come to their help and get the order cancelled because the Sarafs have no suitable place to open their shops and their business will suffer.
4. In order to remove differences and party-feeling exisitng among the membersof the committee a sub-committee should be formed to nuke inquiries and submit a report.

c_red_shirt_pesh_1940_p131.text:
THE
TRIBUNE. 26-5-40
FRONTIER NEWS
(From Our Correspondent) Peshawar, May 24.
Revision Of City Congress
Constitution
At a meeting of the Working Committee of the Peshawar Con- gress Committee held last night, Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi presid- ing, the constitution of the Com- muuttee as revised by the Constitu- tion Sub-Committee, was endorsed with minor modifications.
The new constitution follows closely on the lines of the old constitution but some modifications have been made to facilitate smooth working of the Committee. Some of the fundamental changes proposed are grea- ter representation to the Ward Congress Committees, imposition of some restrictions on the Ward Congress Committees for raising public subscriptions and the election of the members of the Working Committee. Previous- ly the members of the Working Committee as also the office-bear- ers and the President were elect- ed by the general. House. Accord- ing to the proposed, revised cons- titution the President and the office-bearers are to be elected by the general House but the mem- bers of the Working Committee
are to be nominated by the Presi- dent in consultation with elected office-bearers.
Peon Assaults Vice-President
other
By another résolution the Work- ing Committee condemned the ac- tion of Yusuf, an employee of the Peshawar Congress Committee, who had insulted and assaulted Maulana Khan Mir Hilali the Vice- President of the Peshawar Con- gress Committee and the Secretary of the Peshawar District Congress Committee a few days back in the office of the Committee. The Committee while condemning the action of Yusuf and recommending disciplinary action against him congratulated Maulana Khan Mir Hilali for his non-violently bear- fing the insults. Extract from the Weekly Confidential Diary of the Peshawar District dated 31.5.40.
(ix) A public meeting of the City Congress Committee attended by about 6000 (100) people, was held at the Namak Mandi Park in Peshawar City from 21.30 to 23.40 hours on 27.5.40 under the chairmanship of Hakim Abdul Jalil "Nadvi". The proceedings were opened with a recitation of verses from the Holy Quran by Khan Mir "Hilali". Bakhshi Faqir Chand "Vaid" read an Urdu poem purporting to mean that they choarfully go to gallows proving that they had sentiments of martyr- dom and they would be in a position to celebrate the "Lartyrs Day" when they would expell their enemies from the country. Pandit Jawahir Lal Nehru referring to the situation in the West remarked that nothing could
be said about what would be the state of India in the near future. It was therefore incumbent on each individual to save his camp. Personally he was accustomed to see the disastrous effects of bombs from aeroplanes as he had experienced these scenes.in Spain and China some two years ago. It was absolutely at Hitler would establish his suzerainty in wrong that India if the British went anay. Indians should, however organise themselves to oppose the miseries and save their country as they were not toys to be sold by one to another. peaking on the policy of non-violence, he remarked that in the absence of power non-violence was the best impliment to oppose an enemy equiped with weapons of violence. Poland was crushed because it used violence against its enemy who was more powerful. Had s she acted on the policy of non-violence she would never have met such consequences. If Germany was now successful in her activities against weak kingdoms, day she would surely come in contact with some bigger kingdom like America or Russia irrespective of the
one

c_red_shirt_pesh_1940_p134.text: fact that sho had friendly relations with the latter whose conquests had, some time put her to suffer a groat loss. Criticising the Pakistan Scheme, he impre ossed on the audience that it was impracticable to divide India into two parts. It had remained one country and would ever remain as one. If Indians were desirous of achieving success they should unite like the British, who, with unity, extended their smaller kingdom into a big Empire. The speaker informe the audience that for the present he was busy in conduc ting affairs of the "National Planning Committee" at Bombay which was formed to devise plans for the future of India in case it secused independence. This Committee, he said, was composed of 20 Sub-Committees. Referring to the deputation which waited upon H.E. the Governor, N.W.F.P., requesting permission to proceed to Waziristan for inquiry into the affairs thore, he assured the public that it would have returned successful. Even now if they were given permission they would create friendly relations between the Government and the Tribesmen. The lodging of the Civil Disobedience Campaign was recently discussed at one of their meetings and it resolved that it was now inevitable but steps would be taken according to the prevailing state of affairs in this country. They were not sitting idle and had therefore converted Congress Committees to the Satyagraha Committees under orders of Mr. Gandhi.Their work was in progress and the setting aside of their differences was essential. He had visited this country after two years and next time, he hoped, he would visit this province as a part of free India. Abdul Ghaffar Khan made his usual type of speech in Pushto that a big flood was approaching. The people were wasting their time in hearing the Radio news

Extract from the Wookly Confidential Diary of the Peshawar district dated 31.5.40.
(vi) Pandit Jawahar lal Nehru arrived at Peshawar Cantt. Railway Station by 8.55 A.M. train on 26.5.40. He was received by about 80 Redshirts and 100 persons including Abdul haffar Khan, Dr. Khan Sahib, Pir Shahin Shah of Kohat, Caim Shah, Pleader, Hakim Abdul Jalil "Nadvi", Milap Singh "Azad", Amin Jan of Kochian, Mohd Usman Naswari Pandit Raghu lath, ladan ial, Pleader,and Ram Saran "agina", Sadhu Singh, Dwarka Nath "Bagh", Shah lal and Ghulam Rabbani, Socialists. He was taken in a car to the residence of Mohd Yunis, Contractor, in Peshawar City. On his way to Peshawar he was greated by about 100 persons and 33 Redshirts in uniform at Nowshera Cantt. Railway Station under cries of "Hindustan Azad" and "Inqilab Zinda Bad". In the evening he attended a tea-party arranged in his honour by Gopal Das Khera in Peshawar Cantt. where he
Was
garlanded by Mohd Ayub in the presence of Abdul
Ghaffar Khan, Dr. Khan Sahib and about 30 others notables. On 27.5.40 he attended the Provincial Congress Committee and the Redshirts meetings and in the evening he, accompanied by Abdul Ghaffar Khan and Dr. Khan Sahib, visited R.B. Mehr Chand Khanna at his residence in Peshawar Cantt. The same night he participated in the public meeting organised by the Congress Committee in Namak Mandi Park, Peshawar City. On 28.5.40, he accompanied by Abdul Ghaffar Khan left for Nowshera where he addressed a public meeting.

(11) A Congress meeting, attended by about 500 muslims was held in Mohabat Khan's mosque, Peshawar City, after Juma prayers on 11.10.40 to protest against the arrest of Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan, M.L.A., Sayad Ashad Shah of of D eh Bahadur, who presided, opened the proceedings by congartulating Abdul Ghafoor Khan at his arrest in the cause of his country and remarked that the time was com- ing nearer when all of them would tread the same path by fighting a struggle under the policy of nonviolence and offering sacrifices toachieve freedon for their country. Khan Mir Hillall moved a resolution congratulating Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan at his arrest, condemning the harsh policy of the Government and appealing to the Frontier people to prepare themselves for further sacrifices, He then made a speech impressing on the congregation that they were awaiting the decision of the All-India-Congress meeting at Wardha and would let the world know how Indian youths would fulfill their pledge.
Hakim Abdul Jalik "Nadvi", speaking on the same lines, referred to the arrest of Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan and remarked that such arrests could never refrain them from saying the truth. Ashaq Shah of Deh Bahadur, speating for th second time moved a resolution: demanding the Government to extend a lenient attitude towards Maulvi Abdul Rahim Popal zai and other political prisoners. Dr. Khan Sahib, criticising the harsh treatment of the jail authorities remarked that the Government's bombing could never frighten them and it would be a dx cowardice on their part to take notice of it. A meeting attended by about 400(40/50) was held inside the
Hanhatnagri Gate on the 11th March(9. p.m. to 10.30 p.m.) under the presidency of Malik Mian Mohd alias Manda.
The President in his opening speech thanked the audience for electing him to preside over the meeting and sought their cooperation towards its autcessful termination
After Anwar Shah of mohalla Mullah Ashub had intoned a few
verses from the Holy Quran, Jan Mohd Compounder to Dr. C. C.Ghosh began to address the audience. During the co rae of his speech,
he said, that today was an auxcious in the history of Congress because the strugglexffor the liberty of the country commenced on that day and the tyrant Dyer opened fire on empty handed Indian
in the Jillaianwala Bagh with a result that zany innocent
Indians were killed. Today the had met to celebrate the memorary of those martyrs and the whole of India was doing the
Caze.
Proceeding, he said that war was raging feriously and overy nation was struggling for its progress. It would be remembered that like Last Great War of 1914 Germany and Britain were at war. Continuing, he said that during the last var Britain promised Doninion Status to India if she became victorious and on this very understanding .Gandhi was deceived and he promised to help.

c_red_shirt_pesh_1940_p046.text:
However Britain emerged victorious and so she was bound
to implement her pledges with India and set her free. Indians, he observed, held meetings and demonstrations requesting the English to fulfill their pledges and some of them convened a meeting in Jillianwala Bagh but General Iyer fired upon them with a result that thousands of poor Indians were killed. Those martyra, he pointedout, made sacrifices for their country and it was an axion that martyre always live though their bodies Before 1919, he said, no one cold venture to take
vanial.
the name of liberty but since then Congress was struggling hard for its attainment and had declared complete independence as their goal. However, now again the English were harping on the old tune and his promise Dominion Status to India immediately after the war but he would like to remind them that now India was not of 1914 but it was of 1940 and was too wise to fall ango into their(English-men) trap. No body,

The City Congress Committee has adopted a novel method of doing propaganda as occasionally they installed loud speaker in the Provincial Congress Office and poems are recited and speeches are made.

CONGRESS HARDAN DISTRICT.
Xo 12.4.40 at 3. p.n. a private meeting attended by
50 prominent congressites of Tajja Rajjar was held in the
Hujjra of Kamdar Khan M.L.A. of Kalu Khan under the presidency
of Rehmatullah of Yar Hussain.
Sahibzada Khurshid of Adina requested the treasurers of
Village Congress Committees to pay the money collected by them
by selling the membership forms. The treasurers paid him a sum of Rs.275/- on account of 1100 forms sold at the rate of -/4/- per each. It was decided that one fourth of the sun should be alotted to villages, one fourth for the Tappa Treasury, one fourth for the District Congress Committee, and the remaining for the Provincial Congress Committee. It was also decided that for every twenty forms sold in a village one member should be elected for the Tippa Congress Committee and in case of forty and sixty forms sold the number of members should be increased perportionary.
Kadar Khan M. 1. A. remarked that the village purchasing less than twenty forms would be entitled to send members for the
Satyagaraha Committee.
Rehmatullah of Yar Hussain tendered his resignation from
the Presidentship of Tappa Congress Committee which was accepted
after a great discussion.

I. P. S. Daily Diary Dated the 16th April 1940.
X CONGRESS COMMITTEE PASHAWAR CITY.
A special meeting of the working committee of the party was held at 8.30 p.m. on 15.4.40 in its office under the presidentship
meeting.
of Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi. The following participated in the
Mehta Madan Lal Pleader.
2. Kundan Lal.
Saran Singh Teamerchant Chuak Yadgar.

Jen Mohd Compounder.
Faqir Chand Vaid.
Mohd Afzal Kotanger
Mohd Usman Naswari.
Abdul Karim Charkhi, and
2/3 others
A discussion took place over the celebration of the Martyrs Day on 23rd April and a programme was chalked out as under.
1. A procession should be taken out from Namak Mandi Park at 2 p.m and should reach the Martyrs Monument in Kissa Khani Bazar by 2.30 p.m. where wreathes would be placed on the monument, the procession will then proceed to Chauk Yadgar where speeches should be made. It should then go to Shahi Bagh by 4 p.m. where a public meeting should be held and the leaders should deliver speeches.

2. It was further decided that/counter act the Muslim meeting to be held in Mahabat Khan's mosque, on 19.4.40 in support of the Pakistan Scheme, a meeting of the City Congress Committee should be held on the same day 8.30 p.m. in chauk yadger and the speeches against the scheme should be delivered.
3. It was also decided that one thousand cards bearing the photo- graph of the martyrs nonment should be printed and sold to the public at one anna per card.
4. A sub committee consisting of Khan Mir Halali, Mohd Usman Naswari, Mohd Afzal Kotanger Jan Mohd Compounder was formed to make propaganda amongst the public against the Pakistan Scheme.
5. Hakim Abdul Jalil, president oity Congress committee was asked to inform the D.C. Peshawar of this programme on 16.4.40.

A private meeting attended by the following was held in the city office on the night of the 30th Maroh, under the presidency of Hokin Abdul Jalil Medvi.

after recitation of some poems Khan Mir Halali advised the crowed to get themselves ready for the forthcoming struggle.

MARTYRS DAY PESHAWAR CITY
(CONGRESS)
A procession of congressites and redshirts numbering
persons, including
about redshirts in uniforms started from Nimak Mendi at 2 p.m. on 23.4.40. The prominent among the processionists wore A.G.K., Dr. Khen Sahib, Abdul Qalyum Bar-at-Law M.L.A. (Central) Mian Jaffar Shah, Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi, Abdul Rahman M.L.A., Mobha Madan Lal.
The following Socialists also joined the procession when
it was inside Peshawar City.
1. Ram Saran Nagina, 2. Sadhu Singh,3 Dwarka Nath alies Baghi, . Krishan Lal.

the tale of the procession.. They were headed by Abdul Qaiyum Popalzei.
The procession reached the Martyrs Monument at about 2.30 and Mien Jaffer Shah
p.m. where A.G.K./laid wreaths on the Martyrs Monument built by (A.G.K) the Congress.
He/also made a short speech in which he referred
the audience to the incident of 1950 and said that they had collected there to hour the memory of those martyrs hho had sacrificed their lives for the sake of the country. What the India achieved during the last 10 years was due to the sacrifices of these martyrs. The speaker further said that those who sacrificed their lives for the sake of the country and nation nevert died. They always lived in the memory of their nation. Meny Xhens and capitalists had died, but no one remembered them. He exhorted the audience to take a lesson from thie and should be prepared to sacrifice themselves to gain their freedom.
After this the procession as usual marched through the

attended by about 700 (200) people, we hold at Shahi Bagh, Peshawar, on the afternoon of 10.1.41, under the chairmanship of Abdur Rahran Khan of Gula Garhi. Khanair "Hilali" moved a resolution congra tulating Maulana Abdul Kalam Azad" on his convictio and sentence to it years imprisonment and considering such convictions of Political lendere a success to reach the goal. The resolution alno assured the sacrificers that every one was ready to follow in their footsteps to achieve freedom. He then made his usual type of spooch giving references to the Congreso demands and their resigning the offices. lie impressed on the audience that the non-arrant of ratyagrahis proved that they had succeeded in attaining a liberty of speech which was one of their demands, Concluding his speech, he appealed to his hearers to Join Congress and follow its policy of non-violence. Milap Buch "Azad" Bocorded the add resolution and speaking in the came therae appealed to the congregation to fill the Jails by following the Mulana. Deved Mukarram hah moved a resolu tion car emning the attitude of Mohd Nawaz Khan and Mohri Rafiq Khan in not reigning the marabership of the District Board and appealing to then to do it now under orters of the Congress. Hakima Abdul Jalil "Nadvi "explained the above resolution and remarked that they, being Pathans, should fulfil thoir pranine by resigning their offices in the District Board as they had succeeded on the Congress ticket. It is heard that the congress workers of Peshawar City are propagating that the conquerred France had handed over all their air basis to Germany and the latter would easily fly to Iraq in order to help the Iraqi troops It was also heard that Turkish Government was going to be changed which due to Germany influence over Turkey.
It is learnt that Ashiq Shah of Deh Bahadur, a congressite has toured in Ahmed Khel, Bazid Khel and Kagawala and have informed the masses about the coming revolution. He exhorted them to unite and boycott foreign

On 17.9.47 a private meeting of the Redshirts, Was held in Jattan School, Peshawar City at about 1200 About 21/22 persons attended the meeting. The following prominent figures:- were the
Taza Gul Tea Seller, Chauk Yadgar, Shamroz s/o Sulten Mohd Chauk Gadi Khana, Illahi of Koocha Zargaran, one unknom s/o Niaz Mohd Garibanan,Najib Gul, Dostal, Khandai, Hamidullah, Makmad Ali, Prio,Sayed Ali of Mohallan Fazal Haq, Shino of Koocha Tanduran,Wilayat Shah of Daki Nal Bandi, (Redshirts) etc.
It was decided by the meeting to spread false propaganda against Pakistan Government, further more they should also get help of the Khaksars in this respect. The Redshirts should not allow the Government to carry out its work and if the Government was allowed for a period of six months, the Redshirts and Khaksars would not be exist any more. Anothor scheme of the meeting was to prepare lists of those persons who were arrested under section 40 F.C.R. and a copy of the Xxx lists should be sont to A.G.K.

(1) On 14.5.41 Ali Gul Khan, President, Provincial Congress Committee, Peshawar, met Hakim Abdul Jalil and discussed the advisability of an Anti-Govt: movement in connection with the Xhar Iraq revolt, but was not supported by him. The President then went to Abdul Ghaffar Khan and Mian Jaffar Shah who favoured this proposal. It is reported that Abdul Ghaffar Khan had sent a mess anger to Mahatma Gandhi at wardah to seek his opinion in this affair. On the return of messagn messanger a meeting in this respect would be held on 20.5.41.%.

It is said that the Hakim Abdul Jalil has informed (vii) all members of the Congress Committee that Abdul Ghaffar Khan would arrive at Peshawar on 20.5.41 and interview all the Frovincial members in connection with the rag
affairs.
(viii) A private meeting of the Congressites was held in the Provincial Congress Committee office on 20.5.41. Amin Jan took the chair. They decided that Abdul Ghaffar Khan should tour in the City to remove internal differen- The President decided that Lal Mohd, wali Mohd and Haji Mohd Naim should tour in Peshawar to increase the members of Redshirts on 25.5.41.
ces.
(ix) Another meeting of Satyagraha was held in Provl: Congress Committee Office Peshawar und er the chairman- ship of Amin Jan at 17.00 hours on 20.5.41. Hassan Gul, Baz Mohd, Rahim Sher, Nur Faraz and 2/3 others attend ed the meeting. The President said that Satyagraha should be started from 21.5.41. Nur Faraz will offer Satyagraha in Tappa Khalil Mohmands, Mohd Said in Daudzai and Rahim Sher in Tehsil Nowshera. Amin Jan and Sadullah of Kochian and Ahmad Khan of Haji Zai Panchayat Members will tender their resignations to the Asstt: Commr: according to the declaration of Ali Gul Khan on 21.5.41. It is said that Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Ghulam Rasul and Amin Jan will proceed to Hangu on 21,5.41.

Muslim soldiers did fight to inflict punishment on their Own Khalifa or to deprive him of his terrinot
the subject to refuse to assist the ruler who misrulca. At the same time I admit that non-co-operation practised by a mass of people is attended with grave risks. But in a crisis such as has overtaken the Mus salmans of India no step that is unat tended with large risks can possibly bring about the desired change. Not to run some risks will be to court much greater risks, if not virtual destruction of law and order but there is yet an escape from non-co operation. Mussalman representation has requested Your Excellency to lead the agi- tation yourself as did your distinguished predecessor at the time of the South African
A
trouble. But if you cannot see your way to do so and non-co-operation becomes dire necessity, I hope Your Excellency will give those who have accepted my ad- rice and myself credit for being actuated by nothing less than a stern sense of duty."

MUSALMANS MESSAGE
NON-CO-OPERATION TO BEGIN
1ST AUGUST
reIn pursuance of the decision arrived at by the special meeting of the Central Khilafat Committee held at Allahabad in regard to the non-co-operation movement the follow ing letter signed by about 90 Sunni Musal- mans from various parts of India, in cluding Yakoob Hasan, Mazhar-ul-Huq, Maulana Abdul Bari, Maulana Hasrat Mohini Kitchlew, Main Mahomed Chotan and Shaukat Ali was sent to the Viceroy from Bombay on Tuesday:-Your Excel. lency-We, the undersigned claim to present the largest body of Sunni Mushun opinion. We have most carefully read tho Turkish Peace Terms and we consider them to be in direct violation of the re ligioas sentiments of the Mussalmans They violate obligations imposed upon the Sunnis and wound the susceptibilities of all Mussalmans. They are contrary to the pledges of British Ministers on the strength of which it has been admitted it was possible to draw upon India for Muslim recruits during the war. We hold the British Empire, which is the greatest Mahomedan power in the world, cannot treat the Turkish Empire which represents the Khilafat, in the same man- ner that it may treat a defeated enemy. Indeed, we contend in certain respects Tur- key has been treated worse than other powers. We respectfully submit in the treatment of Turkey the British Govern- ment are bound to respect Indian Muslim sentiment in so far as it is neither unjust nor unreasonable. In our opinion the posi- tion taken up by the Indian Mussalmans is simple. They cannot bear the thought of the temporal power of the Sultan being adversely affected by way of punishment for his having joined Germany under cir- cumstances which need not be, examined here. But we have no desire to ask for anything that would interfere with the principle of self-determination. We have no desire to uphold any misrule such as has been attributed to Turkey. Our dele- gates in Europe have asked for an inde- gate the charge of wanton cruelty said to pondent commission of inquiry to investi- have been practised by Turkish soldiers in Armenia. We cannot look with indiffer- ence upon the partition of Turkey and her empire for the sake of punishment humiliating her. We would therefore re- for quest Your Excellency and your Govern- ment to ask His Majesty's Ministers to secure a revision of the peace terms and
tell them that on the failure to do so your Excellency will make common cause with the people of India. We make this sug gestion as your Excellency has repeatedly declared your Government has consistently and often pressed upon the attention of His Majesty's Ministers the case of Indian Mussalmans in this matter of vital con- We cern to the vast majority of them. feel therefore we have a right to ask your Mussalmans of Excellency to reassure India they still retam your active co-oper ation and powerful Advocacy in the prose cution of their claims, even to the point of resignation of your high office should His Majesty's Ministers fail to secure a revi- sion of the terms consistently with pledges and sentiment mentioned above. We ven- ture respectfully to suggest had India been a Dominion enjoying full self-government her responsible Ministers would have as a matter of course resigned as a protest against such a serious breach of pledges and flouting of religious opinion as are in- volved in the peace terms. If unfortunately Your Excellency will not adopt our humble suggestion we shall be obliged as from 1st August next to withdraw co-operation from Government and ask our co-religionists and Hindu brethren to do likewise. We ask Your Excellency not to regard our state- ment as a threat or in any way as a mark of disrespect. We claim to be as loyal sub- jects of the Crown as any in India but wo consider our loyalty to an earthly sovereign to be subservient to our loyalty to Islam, The latter enjoins upon every Mussalman to consider those who wantonly injure, the status of the Khalifa to be enemies of Islam and to resist them with arms if necessary. We recognise that even if we had power we must not resort to arms so long as any other means are at our disposal. We feel the least a Mussalman can do in these cir- cumstances is not to assist those who are guilty of trying to reduce the Khalifat practically to nothingness. It would there- fore become our painful duty to refuse to co-operate with the Government which ac- Icepts the peace terms and advises the ac- ceptance thereof by us. We shall hope that such a serious step as non-co-operation will not become necessary but should it unfor- tunately happen to be otherwise we assure Your Excellency we shall strive our utmost to avoid violence. We fully recognise our res- ponsibility. We know any eruption of vio- lence must check and injure the peaceful demonstration contemplated by usand what is more sacred the cause which is dear to us as life. We shall therefore take up non- co-operation in progressive stages so as to cause the least necessary dislocation embarrassment to Government and so as to feeling. enable us to control and discipline popular

ration noven ent was the withdrawal, from

(iv) About eleven Congress workers assembled at the Provincial Congress Committee Office in Peshawar City on the evening of 25.1.41 and dis. cussed the "Independence Day" celebration. Ali Gul Khan, in reply to the question of Bahadur Khan of Tehkal Bala, said that the Government had no idea to stop their procession. Had their been any such thing a notice would have been served on them. He further appealed to the gathering to make propaganda for the success of the function and remarked that expenses on the occasion would be met from the Chanda funds. Mohd Usman "Naswari" informed the congregation that the Redshirts of the Hashtnagri Ilaqa
were not prepared to join the procession.

On 21st June, in the evening, about 480 volunteers of the Muslim National Guard in uniform, assembled in Lundi Beri Peshawar City and hoisted a flag at the house of one Gul Badshah, in the prosence of a large gathering. Some 200 shots were fired in the air by the volunteers. On this occasion, one Sadat Khan, Ahrari, announced his decision to join the Muslim League. Fida Mohd, Pleader, address- ing the volunteers advised them to take out the procession peacefully and not to use any objectionable words to provoke the other party. The procession then started under the command of Sar-askar Fida Mohd, accompanied by League bands. Many of the volunteers carried rifles shotguns, spears and other weapons. The processionists passed through various bazars in the City and reached the Chowk Yadgar after passing through the vissa Khani bazar. The volunteers fired shots in the air enroute and on reaching the Chowk suddenly some stir was caused, and the processionists began moving backward, and a false alarm was circulated that some Hindus had opened fire. In the confu- sion that ensued numerous shots were fired by the volunteers in air, but no one received injuries. after the procession had gone towards the League office the Congress flag flown by the Garibans near a Restaurant was found lying on the road with its poles broken and the actual flag missing. The Deputy Commissioner and Senior Superintendent of Police rushed to the scene and the Chowk was cleared off the crowd. One person with some injury on the head appeared before the Deputy Commissioner and he alleged that he was beaten by one of the League processionists with the butt of his gun. d hit the wooden alleged that some shots had hit the wooden projection of the shop of

Later, about 40/50 Congressmen (Garibans) some of whom were armed, came to the Ch Yadgar, who seemed to be in an excited mood but the Senior Supdt. of 00 Police turned then back and the situation was brought under control after a shot while, Hon'ble Dr. Khan Sahib with Hon'ble Mohd Yahya Ja and others visited the Chowk Yadgar, Sometime later, at about 21.25 hours, one ohd Hussain of Mohalla Dabgaran was alleged to have been stabed in the buttocks by some unknown Redshirt outside Dabgari Gato case u/s 307 IP.C, has been registered. This last night's incident has apparently created bad feelings between the parties and some Red- shirts are reported to be collecting in certain localities and te

On January 4th, a poting, attended by about 500(10) people ma held at the insting's Memorial. Dr. Chun Sahib roaided. Before the meeting bean, he expressed his antonialment at the poor attendance despite previous notice having been given. Abdul aiyus, Ar-at-Law, proposed a resolution, which was later peased, congratulating Mulana Agad on his arrest. He then made a specch describing Maulana Azad's work and commented on the present civil disobedience movement. He said that after January 6th some 15000 Congressmen would maintain the Congress honour and if after that their demands were not accepted, primry Congress members mubering lakhs would be allowed to court arrest. He added that the fact that no arrests had been made of satyagrahis on the Frontier showed that the Government would give in to Congress. Abdul Akbar 10wn of Umergai and Cain Shah, Plouder, of Peshawar, unde speeches on ordinary lines. The final speaker was Dr. Wan Sahib, who coured the hardships endured by the British in the war with the easy way in which 40 crores of Indians submitted to foreign rule. He concluded his speech with the usual references to discipline and unity and the solution of their problem by the attainment of freedom

un 4th July, about 460 Redshirt volunteers, in uniform, marched from Nimak Landi Peshawar City, under the command of Lal Mohd and wall Mohd, Redshirt Generals: two of the volunteers carried pistols and two, daggers. When the procession was passing through Ganj bazar, some one threw a stone at it from a house, resulting in Some excitement amongst the members of the procession, one of whom fired three shots in the air with a pistol, Stones were thrown at the houses in the vicinity, resulting in window-panes being broken in 3/4 houses, including the house of ian Burhanud Din, Pleader, a Muslim League supporter. The procession terminated in Chowk Yadgar, where a meeting was held, attended by a large crowd, and speeches were delivered by Maulvi Gul Badshah of Toru, ohd Yunas, Qureshi, and others in support of Pathanistan. As a result of the above incident, a case has been registered u/s 307 I.P.C. by the City Police on the complaint of ian Burhanud Din, Pleader.

Qayum, Barrister, moved a resolution congratulating Maulana Abul Kalam Azad" on his arrest and assuring him that the inhabitants of Peshawar would follow in his steps by offering all kinds of sacrifices. He then made a speech explaining to the congregation that the arrest of Maulana Abul Kalam was a matter of great importance as it was not only a challenge to the National Congress but also to each and every Indianwho was desirous of attaining independence. He then informed the audience that Maulana Abul Kalam "Azad" had declared at Lahore that the Congress leaders if would defy law upto the 15th: instant and 'no action was taken by the Government against them, a mass civil disobedience would be declared to make the Government realise that Indians wanted a complete independence. In the end he strongly criticised the Hindu Maha Sabha and the Muslim League for strengthen- ing the clutches of the Government. Abul Akbar Khan, speaking in the same strain, referred to the European war and remarked that it was due to the anguish of the poor that the wild nations of Europe and Asia were Axa daggers drawn against each other. Explaining the creed of non-violence he impressed on his hearers that their policy was quite different to the other powers of the world who were using cannons and tanks like beasts. Dr. Khan Sahib was the last to speak. He exhorted the audience to do practical work as mere speeches could never bring freedom to India. He contradicted Abdul Akbar Khan for naming Russia amongst the other nations of Europe, as fight- ing like beasts, stating that Russia was the only country which always helped the poor and that he was not prepared to hear any propaganda against her.
Concluding his speech he impressed on the gathering

Peshawar City attended by a hue Fixaxtarry audience. S.Qaim Shah, Parliamentary
Secretary, claimed that Congress workers were being threatened that they would be hanged when Pakistan would be established. Ho stressed the necessity of establishing Azad Pathani stan in the Frontier Province, and said that the rule of Khans and Britons could not be tolerated any longer. Mohd Yunas Qureshi of Peshawar City referred to the Jinnah-abdul Ghaffar Khan meeting, stating that Jinnah had refused to agree to a separate Government and law for Pathans. He claimed that tenants, who were Redshirts, were being told to vacate their houses because they supported Congress, and he advised them to warn their landlords not to create any disturbance. If the landlords used force to eject them, they should set fire to their houses.Ghulam Mohd, alias Gama, suggested that Khaksars and Ahrars should seize the legs of voters during the referendum and not permit them to go to the polling stations.It was announced during the meeting that 7th July would be observed "Pathanistan Day Dr.Khan Sahib, Premier, condemned Muslim League leaders who had resorted to unfair tactics, and regretted that ladies from the Punjab had found it necessary to come to the Frontier Province to assist the Muslim League. He said that Muslim League supporters could not make sacrifices like those made by Redshirts, and would therefore, not be able to compete with them. He stated that those who cheated simple persons by issuing "Tawig" to them, were individuals of poor character, who should not be relied upon.

On 2.7.47 from 2140 hours to 2400 bours, a Congress meeting attended by about 2000(400)pasons was held at Bhanamani,presided by Faizullah Khan of Bhanemoni. Some of the attendence were armed with fire-arms. The main speakers in this meeting are Hussain Bakhsh Kausor, Mohd Yunas Khan brother of the Education Minister, Hon'ble Qazi Attaullah Khan and Hon'ble Doctor Khan Sahib the Primo Minister. Usual propaganda speeches were made, in this meeting. Hussain Bakhsh Kauser of Peshawar City Said in the course of his speech that it was the intention of the Khudai Khidmatgars for about last 18 years that they would establish a Pathan State including Campbellpur, Baluchistan and Manwall areas, inhabitted by the Pathans. He further said that the Azad Pathan State would extend to us Baluchistan. The Afghen people are supporting this claim of Pathanistan and are willing to join NEEX us in this Government (probably the speaker meant by saying this that N.W.F.Pand Afghanistan Would join together and form one Government).
Hon'ble the Chief Minister said in the course of his speech after repeating the sacrifices made by the Redshirts that those people who will cast votes under fear of some kind of other should remove their fears. The present Government based on your support, he said can not be removed by mere threats and pressure of the opposite party. There is no power on earth that could dissolve the Ministry and impose section 93 regime instea as whoever tried toxikka Would break his own head. Ho further said that he is afraid of Muslims fighting amongst themselves and impressed upon the audience to maintain comploto peace, He x said that he had been abused by the opposite camp many times but he bore all such things not out of fear but sense of responsibility as a man, who could not bear the abus es could not liberate cS

In view of the above circumetences the Muslim League has morally been badly defeated. Redshirt activity has been noticed in Peshawar City where on 10th May General Lal Mohd and Hussain Bakhsh Kausar inspected uniformed Redshirts at Chauk Ninak Mandi, Ghalla Mandi, Chauk Yadgar, outside the Hashtnagri Gate, and another party, the majority of whom were Hindus, in Patti Dhaunan/ All these volunteers wore brand new uniforms, adorned with black cuffs and collars. Some of then wore black stockings or footless hose. The names of these volunteers were entered in rogisters and the volunteers were addressed by Lal Mohd and Hussain Bakhsh Kausar, who informed them to ready to act under the instructions of Abdul Ghaffar Khan and other leaders, Lal Mohd said that the Khudai Khidmatgars would always protect the weak against tyrants. Hussain Bakhsh Kausar abused Muslim Leaguers, Shias and M.A. Jinnah. It is not yet clear whether these gatherings were of Redshirts atи or of the Zalmai Pukhtuns since the uniforms worn by the volunteers consisted of the uniform worn by the Zalma Pakhtuns.

The latter said that the alim League had preached that shariat would be enforced in Pakistan, but instead of Shariat, the uslim League had imported a cursed ropean as Governor from England, who had hoisted the 1slamic flag on Pakistan Day. Redshirts would continue their struggle and would take property from landlords and distribute it amongst the poor. Redshirts, who had faced the British Government, Would prosent for not be afraid of a Government like that of the uslim League. selves of the British Government on 15th August, which, he said, Was the result of Redshirt sacrifices made over a period of 17 years. A meeting of the City Congress Committee attended by about 300 persons including 30/32 redshirts in uniform was held at Katra Resham Garan under the presidency of one Jan Mohd Compounder on the 11th April, 1941, from 21.00 to 22.50 hours in connection with the observation of
"National Week".
Bibi Ghulam Fatma of Lahore delivered a short speech in which she criticised the Pakis tan Scheme and condemned the Muslim League for creating trouble in the way of independence. She also appealed to the audience to join Congress and muster under the Congress flag. Hakim Abdul Jalil of Peshawar City spoke
on the same lines and appealed to the public to wear Khaddar and to organise themselves by giving up their personal frictions.
Milap Singh "Azad" of Peshawar City in his speech condemned 'Todies' for praying at their religious places for the victory of the British.
Jan Mohd (compounder) moved a resolution congratulating Hakim Abdul Salam, president of the District Congress Committee, Hazara, on his arrest and condemning the action of the Government in arresting him without
any reason.

Adeputation consisting of Kanshi Ram, Ram Chand, Deulat Ren, Chuni Lel, halo Singh end a few other indus under the leadership of Mehta Kirpa Ram, met Mualene Abul Kelen Aged on the 13th instent in connexion with the Merketting Bill. The Maulana heered their view points and asked then if they did not want the Bill or desired some amendments. They replied that they were for the latter and he asked them to send their demands in writing.
The Maulana spent the whole day in interviewing Congress M.L.As. and recorded their complaints against the ministry.
It is reported that permission has not been granted to use
Shehi Mehman-Khens, for todays' meeting.
The Maulena may not to to Kohat and Bannu, but is likely to visit * Utmanzai, on the 16th instant.

Proceeding the addres, invited the attention of the Maulana to the Marketting Bill, which was against the spirit of the Congress merfesto an requested him to exert his influence so that trade me not suffer end it will have Bood effect on Congress.

He con- gratualted the audience for sitting quietly in the rain despite the fact thet a number of them had no warm clothes. amount of political freedom but he

il
and was the duty of British Officials in Agencies to offer unstinted co- operation to the Congress Government, but so far as he knew no such co- operation had been offered during the time the Congress Ministry had been in, power or, if it had been offered, it hed produced no tangible results. The present state of affairs was most unsatisfactory and the time had come when Congress could no longer tolerate it, and the Ministry was now consi- dering what course should be adopted. He asked the audience not to become impatient. The object of his visit to Peshawar was to study the various intricate problems in the province and the results of his inquiries would shortly be placed before them.

In the meeting held at Chawk Bazar Peshawar Cantt:, on 20.3.39, Sham Lal
of Pechower Cantt: was first to speak. He said that Congress was now consisting of two parties, one of which wished to accept Federation while the other was opposed to it. Under the proposed Federal Scheme, he remarked, that Indians would have no control over foreign affairs, the importation of fo- reign goods, the Army or the Railways. Rulers of the States enjoy luxuries on the money collected from their subjects who were starving. He concluded his speech by criticising Khans and asking his audience to prepare themselves for another struggle with the British Govt: Master Chuni Lal of Peshawar Cantt: spoke in the same strain and said that Hindu Maha Sabha and Muslim League were trying to wreck the Frontier Ministry. He remarked that the Congress Govt: could not do much for the poor as the main money-making departments were controlled by the Govt: of India, nor could they touch the pay of Govt: Officials.

Continuing, he said that the aim of the congress was to put en end to the loots made by the foreigners. He criticised the Khan Bahadurs end Rai Bahadurs for strengthening the hands of the British Government and attributed the present reforms to the sacrifices of the martyrs of Jalianwala and Kisse Khani.
Continuing, he said that Azadi could not be achieved through passing resolutions on papers. Had the Indians been free, he said, 4/5 lakhs of them would have gone to help Palestine.
Concluding, he appeяdex appealed to the citizens to help the
villagers.
Principal Chabildas of Lahore speaking next referred to the orders served on him to leave the Frontier under the Public Tranquilty st, when he had come to this Province during the last election, which he said was a kind of reward given to him by the then Government.
Proceeding, he saidthat there was congress governments in eight provinces but it was not worth the name because Union Jack was seen flying everywhere.
Continuing, he said that the congress government will be when there will be no poor in India. Discussing the poverty prevailing in the country, he remarked that it was not right for the Government to realise land revenue etc., when proper arrangements for drinking

Continuing, he said that the aim of the congress was to have self Covernment such as in the case of Russia, Turkey and Ireland
eto.
Referring to the present reforms, he held that it was
a trap to entrap Mohr Chand Khanna and Abdul Qayyum, but the poor congress sepoys had fallen in to it.
Eulogising the work of the Congress ministries, he sadd that they had shown to the English that they were able to manage their
affairs..
Spocking on poverty, he remarked that a sepoy án duty in the Cantonments, a servant of the public, was well dressed than his mestor (the public).
He criticised the ministers for not attending the meeting. A meeting of the Socialist party attended by about 150 people, ma held in Chork Bazar, Feshawar Cantti on the evening of 6.4.59 to observe their National Feek. Speeches were delivered by Mohd Yunis Qureshi, Conrade Shem Lal, Sadhu Singh, Chabil Das of Lahore and Ram Lal.
Mohd Vunia Qureshi narrated the events of the Jallianwala Bagh, Amritear, and remarked that in reward for their services in the Great War, Indians were awarded with the Rowlett Act instead of gaining independence as was promised. Referring to the Federation, Shan Lal informed the audience that the states representatives would be nominated by the Government who will have no authority to speak in the States affairs. He appealed for the use of Khaddar and other country made articles to remove poverty from the country. Sohan Singh recited an irdu poem discussing the labourers and the
capitalists.
Describing the poor state of villagers Chabil Das remarked that 95% of them were starving and in order to remove this curse they should strive for further independence. The foundation stone was laid by Mohd Afzal and shortly afterwards about 70 or 80 Khaksara arrived and brought with them a large stone pillar, a relic of the demolished Edwards Gate Ilahi Bakhsh, Salar-i-Ala, remonstrated with Khan Mir Hilali for having already laid the foundation stone and after some discussion a second ceremony was performed and a second foundation stone was laid about 5 feet from the first amidst shouts from the Congress

THE T'Rebure. /5-34
FIST FRONTIER POLI TICAL CONFERENCE
M. AZAD AGREES TO PRESIDE
Peshawar, May 9.
Advices received from Cal- cutta indicate that Maulana Abul Kalam Azad has agreed to preside over the first Frontier Provincial Political Conference, provided it is held in November next instead of either in September or October, as contemplated by the conveners. Besides, some prominent Congress leaders including Babu Rajendra Prasad, Congress President, and Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru, have also agreed to join the delibera- tions of the conference, which is likely to be held in Peshawar.
The Frontier contingent to the Con- meetings, of the All-India gress Committee at Calcutta returned to Peshawar yesterday. A.P.I.

Daily Diary Dated the 22nd liny, 1939.
Peshawar Ci Congress Committee.
A meeting attended by 600/700 persons, including 70
in redclothes was held on the night of the 21st May, under the Presidency of Khan Mir Halali to observe All Indie Political Prisoners day.
Opening the proceedings the president said that they had arranged the present meeting in accordance with the orders of the president of the Congress to celebrate the above day and he hoped that the audience would hear speeches attentively 1 as the Hon'ble Dr. KhanSahib would also speak.
After recitation from the Holy Quran and a poem by a Redshirt, Hakim Abdul Jalil said that the object of the meeting had already been notified by the press and the A.I.C.C. had ordered that all Governments should be requested to release the Political Prisoners who were still wrotting in jails.
Proceeding, he said that many requests had already been made to release the Political Brisoners but they had proved ineffective as the Government was intoxicated with powers and did not care to pay any heed.
Continuing, he said that when the first instalment of reforms was granted to india, it was announced that all Political Prisoners will be released, but they were still wrotting in jails in non-congress provinces.
Mapy Prisoners, he said, went on hunger strike and it
perplexed the Government as the only offence of the prisoners wes that either they wanted Swraj or Home Rule and none of them
had aspired to become a Governor or Lieut. Governor.
Continuing, he said that now they were in a position
to subject the Government to scathing criticism.
Proceeding, he said that Mahatma Gandhi took the quest-

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p246.text:
question of the release of Political Prisoners in his hands
and asked them to suspend hunger strike. A period of 1 years had elapsed, but still there were about 600 prisoners in the jeile of Bengal end the prisoners of Beber #2 Akali cese were till Bohind the bars in the Punjab.
Moreover, he observed that Howalder Chanda Singh
a Corali Prisoner, who had refused to fire upon an unarmed crowed in Kissa Khani Bagar had spent 9 years in jail, but hed not been released. However, he would request the Governments of Indie, Benel, Punjab end other provinces to release pàlitical prisoners otherwise they will ruin themselves. These Governments he said, could not realise the feelings of the injured hearts
be
but they should/told thet days of oppression when the Government If the Government, he said, wished to
could succeed more over.
impress the masses it should atonce release Political prisoners. Proceeding, he moved the following resolution which was supported by Mohd Alam Khan of Kohat of Milap Singh Azad
of Peshawar and adopted.
"This meeting requests the Government to release
all Political Prisoners and also demends that the Frontier Government should ask the Government of India to rolease Howal dar Chanda Singh and the Government will be responsible for any undue consequences if all prisoners were not released".
Speaking on the resolution, he said, it was futilo on the part of some governa nts, who hold that unrest would increase if political prisoners were ràlessed but he would refer to the Frontier Government who released Hathi Khel prisoners and nothing untowards happened.
Concluding, he said that there would be no resort
to violence if Political prisoners were released.
Mohd Aslan Khan and Milap Singh Azad xxx drew
a pathetic picture of the political prisoners and demanded

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p247.text:
thur release forthwith in order to avoid unpleasant consequences. Both the speakers did full justice to the subject.
Hon'ble Dr. Khan Sahib spoke on the present political
situation in the Frontier and said thet politics had become so Lomplicated that it had become Regu extremely difficult to find an amicable solution.
Referring to the Assembly Session Days, he said that during those days they might have heard mysterious rumours.
Proceeding, he attacked the infidility of Hukam Chand M.L.A. and said that his party would surely take action against him as he acted oontrary to its mandate.
Real
Continuing, he advised the audience
deceived the nation.
Zog the
who
ce from those who and ut of his off and da
Referring to press propaganda, he said Majority of it abused congress, he would like them to restrain themselves and not to become agitated to abuse was the weapon of the coward. Referring to the two martyrs' monument, he said, that
with
they should realised that it was due to the present Government that they had constructed them in-punity and should recollect the days
when the could not construct one.
Proceeding, he said
a that rhen the present Government had
Permission to erect a monument so he could not understand as to why the opposit party thought it advisable to construct their monument in the night. He said that he would attribute it towards cowardice on their part.
Posing
Proceeding, he said that they should judge people by their
deeds and not by their speeches.
Continuing, he said that a few persons had come to the province and were relating stories that congress governments were oppressing Muslims, but he would like them not to attach any importance to such a propaganda. Such persons, he said, simply

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p248.text:
placed obstacles in the way of liberty. He himself did not nako any distinction among Hindu-Sikh and Muslims, but people were not pleased with him, as some wanted to become Tehsildars and somo Tehanedars. His replies to such persons were that he would surely give them a job when he would be in position to give such employe- ents to 26 lakhs people of this province.
Proceeding, he said that there would be no difference between the present and the old government if the former were to
lend themselves in to personality.
However, he would like them to discard selfishness
otherwise they will revert to their old path.
Referring to the dispute which was going on between
Mofti Qazi Mohd and some Mohmand tenants he gave full details and remarked that he passed orders for the arrest of his own son as he could not sacrifice justice.
However, he would like them to understand that the person arrested in the above dispute were not political prisoners. He had brought all to the Peshawar Jail
Speaking on K.K. Gar organisation, he said that may
had crept in to with selfish motive and he would like them to be aware of such persons.
Referring to Dr. Ghosh, he said, that he was a simple fellow and did not realise that he had been elected by voters.
Adverting to Hukam Chand M.L.A., he said that during the last Assembly session he did his best for the defeat of the ministry and even requested R.B. Ishar Dass to absent himself on the day of voting (Marketting Bill), but the latter came to him and said that henceforward he would always support Congress.
Proceeding, he said that M. Gandhi was coming and after
consulting he would turn out Hukam Chand.
Proceeding, he accused Amir Chand Bonbwal of misleading

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p249.text:
es
Propaganda and laid the same charge at the door of 'Sikh
Smacher', but added that he held that such & propaganda boarded on 'Ba imani'
LIUS
Proceeding, he said that he was a state forward man of the fact that
and uttered solid truth and was consciecious to err is human.
Adverting, to the city monuments he said that both will be removed and replace by a new one by the Municipal Committee and he himself will pay the cost of the balcony closed by.
Proceeding, he said that they should not fight over a monument as there was no bravery in it. He himself was present when the speeches were delivered by the opposite part on the monument, but he was of the opinion that no one could defeat a man of action.
Continuing, he congratulated the audiere e that Congress Raja Marochor and Lieut. Mohd Zemen had joined Party
speech
The rest of his/ advised the audience to become
practical men, promote unity and discard selfishness.
The president in his closing speech remarked that it was a pity that the people did not distinguish between right and wrong and it was their wont to applaud every speaker.
Proceeding, he advised them to differentiate between
persons, who deceived and who served them.
Continuing, he criticised Aurang Zeb Khan said that the only service he had done tas to carry dead bodies during the firing of 1930 and throw them in to the river. Concluding, he announced that Ganga Singh 's sons martyrs day would be observed on the 31st May by organising
a meeting and a procession.

A condolence meeting attended by about 300 persons, including 20 In Redclothes, was held near Katre Resham Gepan "A" Division, on the night of 29th November, under the presidency of A11 Gul Khen to record sorrow over the death of Moulana Shoukat All.
Opening the meeting the president spoke on its object end requested the audience to heer the speeches attentively end silently.
Khan Mir Helali recited a poem on liberty.
Hakim Abdul Jalil Nedv during the course of a lengthy speech said that the late Moulana needed no intro- duction as his services were well known to every body, but however, he would endavour to give a brief sketch of his Life work.
Proceeding, he arid that he would like the audience to emulate the late Moulane in the service of their nation and the country.
Continuing the specker said that the late Moulana wes horn in Rempur in 1673 and he and his brother were still very young when their father passed away and they were bred up by their mother Bi Arme. It was due to the education of their mother that they beorme in line light in the political arena andnade tremendous secrifices. In those days Congress wes a moderate party es it passed resolutions regarding - services only and it was due to the Ali Brothore that Congress became a bold body. It was due to the sacrifices of the Congress that they have their own Government in 8 provinces.
Proceeding the speaker said that the late Moulena we Superintendent in the Excise Department, but he gave up

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p027.text:
up services in 1914 and throw himself in to the struggle of liberty along with his brother the late Moulena Mohd Ali, who hed returned from Anglend efter completing his education.
Continuing the speaker paid a slowing tribute to the services of the 11 Brothers end both more antarned Chandere for six years owing to their political activities.
During the Martial Law days, they became the right end left hand of Mahatma Gandhi who advanced the cause of
the Khalafat Movement with their cooperation.
Continuing, he said that both the brothers remain
with Congress till 1924 and they were responsible for moving the resolution in Karachi that it was unlawful to servexx the military and the police.
Continuing, he said that though Moulane Shoukat Ali parted company with Congress, but his all differences with it (Congress) terminated with his death and now they all would pray for peace to to his soul.
Concluding, he moved the following resolution, which
was adopted.
"This meeting of the citizens of Peshewer places on record its profound sorrow over the death of Moulene Shoukat Ali, sympathies subession his survivors and prays for enternal peace for his soul."
Milap Singh Azad supported the resolution in a suite able speech and paid a glowing tribute to the services of the late Moulana Shoukat Ali. His speech was pitched in the above strain
Hon'ble Banju Ram Gandhi, Finance Minister speaking last said that the year of 1938 had a proved very inauspicious for them as many outstanding personalities like Pandit Hem Rij Mahatma Hans Raj end Mustafa Kamal had passed away. They were still in sorrow over the death of M. Kamal when they received the shocking news of the death of Moulana Shoukat A11.

He regretted the propaganda against him to the effect that he had handed over the Beli Ram Ihata mosque to Hindus for a few bags of cement and said that such a thing could never be expected from a man who was fighting such a powerful Government. places.
Ashiq Shah of Deh Bahadar, support- ing the views of Arbab Abdul hafur Khan said that Islam taught them to assist Afridis and Wazirs, but they could not do so in the presence of the English. Criticising Khans, he said that they were the people who had supplied
recruits to fight against their brothern
in Turkeyn Tirah and Waziristan.

and a procession in connection thereof would be taken out.
(vii) A meeting of the City Congress Committee, attended by about 45 persons was held in the Provincial Congress Committee Office in Peshawar City on the evening of 25.1.39, under the presidency of Milap Singh Azad. t was decided that the Ward having 100 members and 25 volun- teers should have a right of sending 5 candidates to the membership of the City Congress Committee and the ward having only 100 members should send one candidate but Ragu Nath pleader opposed it saying
that no such instructions were received
from the Central Congress. He further proposed that members should have distinction over the volunteers but Qaim Shah

were the persons who always offered sacrifices. In the end a committee consisting of Qaim Shah pleader, Mehta Madan Lal, Abdul Jalil Nadvi, nagu Nath

give up their mutuel differencer Bus (viii) To observe "Independence Day" about 250 Redshirts assembled in Namak Mandi, Peshawar City, at about midday on 26.1.39 and processed through thes City, und er Hakim Abdul Jalil, Ghulam Mohd, Milap Singh "Azad" and Faqir Chand "Vaid", Ram Saran "Nagina", Sadhu Singh and Dwarka Nath, Socialists, to the Shahi Bagh, where a meeting, attended by about 800 people, was held under the presidency of Ghulam Mohd of Dabgari Illaqa. The flag was hoisted by Hakim Abdul Jalil "Nadvi" explaining the three colours of the flag. Speeches suitable to the occasion were delivered by Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan, M.L.A., Milap Singh "Azad", Mohd Yunis "Quraishi and Faqir Chand "Vaid". Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan translated the pact of 26th January 1926 from Pushto into Urdu and exhorted the audience to fight for complete independence. Milap Singh asked the people to be ready for the

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1938_p096.text:
-7forthcoming fight for the liberation of India from the clutches of the British. Mohd Yunis Quraishi" remarked that it was due to the advent of British rule in India that the Indians were in the worst condition and that they would not take rest until they have thrown off Imperialistic Yoke. In the end he appealed to the audience to give up their mutual differences and Join hands in the interest of the country. Faqir Chand "Vaid explained the aims and objects of Congress and advised the public to muster under its banner.

I. P. S. Daily Diery dated the 3rd.June 1910.
REDSHIRTS PESHAWAR CITY.
It is reported that a secret meeting of the redshirts Gerhibens of Ghellemandi was held on the evening of the 1st.June 1940 and was attend-ed by some 60 persons. In this meeting Khen Moha General Informed those presents that Pandit Jawhar Lez Nehru hed instructed the redshirts Generale and aib-Salara on the evening of the 27th May 1940 in the house of Mohd Yunis that in view of the cloudy atmosphere they should organize end should try to increase their number. In this meeting some of the Hindus and Sikhs had also joined and while addressing to them he exhorted them, to make efforts to enlist Hindus and Sikhs redshirts.
Proceeding,he is reported to have said that in caso they do not join in the K.K.Gar Movement with in a week they should not be responsible for the life and property of the people.

City, came there and ordered them in a threatening tone to stop the announcement, but they carried on their programme. Extract from the Wookly Confidential Diary of the Poshawar, District dated 7.6.40.
II. To celebrate the Bazar-i-Kalan "Martyrs Day" a procession of about 180 porsons, including 80 Redshirts in uniform with two flags and a card board bearing "Shahidan-i-Peshawar Zindabad", started from outside Edwardes Ga te on the afternoon of 31.5.40 under the lead of Hakim Abdul Jalil "Nadvi", Milap Singh "Azad", ✓ Mohd Usman Naswari, Gopal Das Khera, Ram Saran "Nagina" Nagyari, and Chela Ram "Shauq". As usual it marched through the main streets of the City and reached the grave-yard outside Ganj Gate where the gathering showered flowers on the graves and prayed for the souls of the martyrs. On their way back the procession halted near the Memorial in Bazar-i-Kalan. Here Gopal Das Khera gave account of the event in which the Bazar-i-Kalan martyrs lost their lives and appealed to the audience to follow them to save their country. The processi on then marched forward and dispersed near the lasting's memorial.
From 21.30 to 22.45 hours, a meeting, attended by about 400 (150) persons, was held in the Hasting's
~howly
Memorial howk under the presidency of Mohd Usman Naswari. Dilawar Khan of Tehkal opened the proceedings by reciting a Pushto poem on the firings of 23rd April and 31st May. Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi spoke on the firing of 3 st May, in Bazar-i-Kalan reminding people of the sacrifices rendered by the martyrs in the cause of their attaining independence. Abdul Qayum' Barrister, referring to the raids and kidnappings in the Shouthern Districts remarked that a Congress Deputation had waited upon H.E. the "overnor for grant of permission to them to institute inquiries into the Waziristan affairs but were not allowed under orders of the Cent-ral Government. He criticised the

The A.S.P. City who was coming from the Gorkhatri side, noticed the traffic being blocked asked the party not to close the road and this annoyed the redshirts and they are reported to have uttored unparliamentary words.

Here Gopal Das Khera gave account of the event in which the Bazar-i-kalan martyrs lost their lives and appealed to the audience to follow them to save their country. The procession then marched forward and dispersed near the nasting's memorial.
From 21.30 to 22.45 hours, a meeting, attended by about 400 (150) persons, was held in the Hasting's
howk
Memorial howk under the presidency of Mohd Usman Naswari. Dilawar Khan of Tehkal opened the proceedings by reciting a Pushto poem on the firings of 23rd April and 31st May. Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi spoke on the firing of 3 st May, in Bazar-i-Kalan reminding people of the sacrifices rendered by the martyrs in the cause of their attaining independence. Abdul Qayum barrister, referring to the raids and kidnappings in the Shouthern Districts remarked that a Congress Deputation had waited upon H.E. the overnor for grant of permission to them to institute inquiries into the Waziristan affairs but were not allowed under orders of the Cent-ral Government. He criticised the

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1940_p041.text:

British Government for condemning the activities of Hitler and not bringing into mind her own agrossivo policy in Waziristan. Referring to the meeting of the leading people at the residence of H.E. the Governor
at 18.00 hours on 1.6.40, he said, that he also had received an invitation but he replied that he was not prepared to attend such useless meetings. Concluding his
speech he appealed to the public to join, Congress
J
if thet wanted freedom. Khan Mir Hilali and Gopal Das Khera spoke in the same theme. The laller further remarked that if their inquiry Committee had been granted permission to enter the Tribal Territory it would have brought to light to why and under whose instigation zation were these kidnappings and raids committed. Milap Singh "Azad" recited an Urdu poem purporting to mean that he had come into the field to mend the fate of people, awaken them from their slumber and to chew the bones of those who were sucking blood of the poor. The last verse meant that he was mutinous and rebellious and had thus come out to create a rebellion. He then remarked that in exchange of the firing of 31st May on innocent children the British were now facing the same fate
in England where their mothers were weaping for their children. In the end he criticised the luxurious living
by the Capitalists.
