REDSHIRTS PESHAWAR.
From 14. to 16.00. hrs, on the 25th June 1940, a
private meeting of the Tehsil redshirts was held in their office, Mohalla Mochipura, under the presidency of Hassan Gul, General and
was attended by about 12/13 persons.
following resolution was adopted :-
After discussion the
"This meeting of the Peshawar Tehsil Redshirt
Khan C-in-c
Generals appeals Rab Nawab Khan C-in-C., to remove Amin Jen,
Naib Salar, from his post as his attitude towards the K.Ks was not good and as a result of which the K.K. Movement in the rural area had been supressed."
At the same meeting a letter signed by all generals was addressed to Amin Jan, Naib Salar, informing him that they would not carry out any of his orders until an order was passed
by the -in-C., on their resolution.
Hassan Gul General was asked to make a tour of the
Tehsil and organise the K.Ks.
He would also move office bearers
of the redshirts of various villages to adopt resolution against
Amin Jen and send their copies direct to the C-in-C.,
His tour
26/27th June 1940.

(iii)
A joint meeting of the representatives of
the Peshawar Cantt. Congress Committee, Majlis-i-Ahrar Panjabi Ranjit Jatha, Akal Sona and Mahabir Dal,
attended by about 40 persons, was held at the residence of Mohd Ayub in Peshawar Cantt. on 23.6.40.. The present political situation was discussed. It was resolved to form a joint volunteer corps under the name of the "Cantt National Guard" for the defence of the Cantt. people. Gopal Das "Khera" opined that all volunteers of this newly formed Corps should wear Congress badges on their shoulders. The gathering agreed to it but the Mahabir Dal men replied that they would further consider this point in their own meeting.

I. P. S. Daily Diary Dated the 28th June 1940.
6qB
CONGRESS PESHAWAR CITY.
A private meeting of the City Congress Committee, attended by some 10 members was held in the office of the combined office of the City, District and Provincial Congress Committees, in Miss garan bazer, from 20.30 to 22.00 hrs., on 27.6.40, with Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi in the chair.
Hakim Abdul Jalil informed the members that Aphab Abdul Ghafur Khan, in a letter, had demanded from them to hire a separate office for the C.C.C. They then put forward the following
resolution :-
"This meeting of the C.C.C, requests the P.C.C. to punish Isaf Khan, servant, of Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan for his ill treatment towards Khan Mir Halali."
At the same meeting it was proposed to appoint other ✓ members in place of Ram Saran Nagina and Mohd Yunis Qureshi, but it was decided upon to put up the matter in the General Meeting. Hakim Abdul Jalil further informed the members that the
rent of the office which amounts to Rs. 25/- was being paid in equal shares by the P.C.. and D.C.C, while Rs. 12/- were being paid by the C.C.C. for the Choukidar of the office. In addition to that the salary of the sweeper etc., was also paid by the C.C.C. Proceeding the Hakim remarked that they would hire another office, in case they were ordered to do so by the P.C.C., but not under the orders of Arbab Abdul Ghafur.

CONGRESS PESHAWAR CITY.
It is reported that Meulens Khan Mir Halali, Secretary, District Congress Committee, Peshawar, has issued instructions to all subordinate Congress Committees in the district, to abstain from taking part in the war councils, otherwise disciplinary action would be take against the defaulters.
In view of the above circuler, Maulana Khan Mir Helali, and Amin Jen, Haib Saler, ere thinking of forming National Guard in Foshaxar City.
It is reported that at the request of Tappa Khelil Congress Congress Committee (llur ligha's party), Keulans Khan Mir Haleli, has asked the general secretary Provincial Congress Committee, to appoint a member of Teppa Khalil Congress Comittee on the Provincial Congress Committee, in place of Arbab Abdul' Ghafur Khan.
It is learnt that Nur Mohd's party intend
to get Chulen Sarwer, member of the District Board, to be nominated in place of Arbab Abdul Ghefur Khan, on the F.C.C. It is stated that on the visit of the General Secretary P.C.C, in Peshawar, he would nominate the members of the tribunal from the D.C.C., to inquire into the matter as to which of the congress committees of Tappa Khalil was a genuine one

REDSHIRTS PESHAWAR
Officials
On 6.7.10, a private meeting of the Tehsil xeneraly, attended by some 16/17, was held in the Office of the redshirts, under the presidency of Khan Mohd, General. They adopted the following resolutions :-
1. Considering the order of the Neib Saler, regarding the removal of Kechkul Khan, secretery, from his post, as illegal and declaring that they had full confidence in Kachiul Khan and the charges brought against him were baseless.
Further requesting the Solar-i-Azan to intervene in this matter and try to remove the differences.
Resolving to send copies of the above said resolution to the Saler-i-Azen and the Press.

(iv)
A private meeting of the Peshawar Tehsil
Redshirt officials, attended by about thirteen persons, was held at the Balakhana of Hassan Gul, General, outside Kutchery Gate, Peshawar, on the afternoon of 25-11-1940. The Civil Disobedience campaign was discussed. On the suggestion of Dilbar Khan it was resolved that the Hindu and Sikh Redshirts should be asked to step forward after the Congress leaders had offered themselves to arrest and after them would come the turn of the Muslim Redshirts.
(v) A private meeting of the City Congress Committee, attended by about nine persons, was held at the Provincial Congress Committee office in Peshawar City on the afternoon of 26-11-1940. Hakim Abdul Jalil "Nadvi" was in the chair. A resolution was passed acknowledging that the city Congress had every confidence in the decision arrived at in the recent meeting of the Provincial Congress Committee held at Mardan. Gopal Dass Khera produced 140 forms completed by the Cantt: members for the year 1941.

CONGRESS PESHAWAR. On December 11th, a meeting of satyagrahis under Abdul Ghafar Khan we held in the office of the Frovincial Congress Committee. It was decided that the first batch would do satyagraha at about noon on the 14th. The following persons will court arrest at the places mentioned; (1 Dr. Khan Sahib, Peshawar City, (2 cani Attaullah, Landi Yarghajo, (3) Xamotax Abdul Qayu, Bar-at-Law, Charsadda, (4) Arbab Abdul Rehman, Shabqadar, (5) Kardar khan, Yar Hussain, (6 Amir Muhamad Khan, Kardan, (7) Abdul Qalyum Bwathi, Baffa, (8) Pir Shahin Shah, Jengal Khel, (9) Luharicad Jan, Bar-at-Law, Bannu City, (109 Bhanju Ham Gandhi., DAT.Khan. On the 15th at noon, the following will court arrest at the places mentioned; (1) Mian Jaffar Shai, Akora, (2) Allahdad Khan, Rustan, (32arin Khan Katlang, (4) Abdul Aziz, Swabi, (5) Faqira Khan, Mansehra, (6) Akbar All, Kald, (7) Tek Chand Dhingra, D.I.Khan, (8) Muhammad Afzai, Latambar. Jamna lass will court arrest
at Mardan on the same day at 4 P.M. and Hukara Chand at Karak at 3 P.. The satyagrahis have inforced the Deputy Commissioners of their districts of their intentions. They will court arrest by shouting slogans.
On December 11th,
attended by about 350(80)
people, was held in Shahidan Attaullah Khan, Air Moha
Khan of Hoti, and Abdul Ghafar Khan rande speeches on the coming civil disobedience campaign, exhorting people not to give up the struggle, which would be a decisive one, and appealing to them for strict discipline, unity and adherence to the principles of non-violence.

DECRET.
CONGRESS
C.I.D. Daily Diary dated 23-9-1940. A Congress Committee meeting was held in Peshawar ty on the 22nd of september, attended by about 500 persons. The speakers were Chulam Muhammad Khan of Lundkhwar, Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi and Gobind sahal, President of the Bijnor Congress Committee (U.P.). Chulan Mubead Khan and Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi had little new to say. The latter referred to Rom Saran Nagina and said that he was losing weight in Jail. He also, he said, wished to contradict the statements some people had made that India was not in danger. moved so rapidly that it was impossible to prophesy. Then Italy declared war no one thought she would be able to wage it and now they had heard that she had invaded Egypt and had penetrated 100 miles. He then referred to the Air Raid Precautions Committee and said that it was Government's duty to provide air raid for purdah ladies.
Events
to shelters with accommodation Unless Government made these arrangements they preferred to perish in their houses rather than to withdraw to a dangerous place. He concluded by condemning the mentality of certain individuals who were in favour of withdrawal and advised them to take courage and shelter in villages if ever it became necessary to evacuate the city. The last speaker, Gobind Sahal, delivered a long rambling speech in which he confined himself for the most part to a history of European events from the rise of Hitler. His object appeared to be to criticise the British Government for having failed to take preventive action in the first instance and to make occasional complimentary references to the Axis powers. At the end of the last war, he said, the Gemans had not really been defeated but had suspended hostilities because of internal dissensions. During the war Hitler had been a soldier and had sown the seeds of this war in the days of peace. Mr. Charrchill had no right to describe Hitler as a mad dog of urope as at the end of the last war Germany had been ruthlessly oppressed, her natural products had been seized and her country had been
n

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1941_pdf_p022.text:
GSAPDeptt NWFP-4IGP-1,09-11-3-4-(179)
dimembered. Further more restrictions had been placed out on her Amy and Military training and this was the reason why she had risen again. It was the British who had caused the war and Hitler himself was cleverer and more intelligent than any Briti cher. Fifth Columnists did not exist. It was mere talk. The Indians were opposed to Nazism and had no wish to assist Hitler but even so they were under suspicion. On the other hand, as they were out of sympathy with the British they could not help their sympathies tending towards Germany. At the present time if any one dared say that Hitler was strong he was prosecuted under the Defence of India Act. Great Britain had spent a great deal of money and was now in financial difficulties, so much so that she was now appealing to Indians for help. Hitler, he said, had succeeded in gaining the support of his people. This was not so in Palestine and India where although their sympathies had been with the British in the last war it was not so in the present one. The same conditions prevailed in
Australia
Until
and Africa and how therefore could the British win? Indians stood side by side with the British, Hitler would not lose Confidence. He did not believe that there could be a revolution in Germany as even those persons who were at first opposed to him were now united because of British bombs. He concluded with a reference to the prospects of civil dis- obedience and said that he could not say when it would
bean Aval
come as full powers had now been given to Gandhi. The
proceedings of the meeting terminated with a resolution
sympathising with Ram Saran Nagina and political hunger strikers in the Punjab Jails.

C.I.D. DAILY DIARY DATED 4-10-40.
CONGREGA
A meeting, attended by about 1000 persoas, was held 813 in the grain market, peshawar Cantoments, on the 3rd October, on the occasion of the visit of Bhulabhal Desal and Asaf Ali. 90% of the audience were Hindus. Asaf Ali in the course of his speech said that they had been sent to make it clear to the inhabitants of Waziristan that Indians regarded them as their neighbours and fully appreciated the strategic position they occupied in the tribal area. As the gate-keepers of India the interests of the tribesmen would always be considered and if and when India reaped the reward of her sacrifices the wal- fare of the tribesmen would always be before them. The British Government had spent 400 crores of rupees in pursuing its Forward Policy and had sacrificed many lives without solving the problem. With that enormous sun they could have built their own Navy and Air Force but it had been spent other- wise. He condemned the forces of destruction at loose in the world and said that the old map of the world would be rolled up and a new one rolled down showing all countries wd th a change of heart. He concluded by asserting that as India was four-fifths of the whole of the British Empire she had much power and many resources and there was no reason why she should not be free which, it was his belief, she would be one day.
Bhulabhai Desai made his speech in English. He said that the last time he had visited the province was in connexion with the elections to the Assembly and he thought that they could say that their mission had been crowned with lle congratulated both himself and the people of
success.
The Congress
the province for their brilliant achievements. Working Committee, he said, had passed a special resolution and sent them on a mission of good will and he was confident that they would be able to accomplish their task even though their rulers had no faith in them. After making a reference to what he described as the stupendous expenditure of 400 crores of rupees in Waziristan, he criticised Great Britain's alleged destructive policy in dealing with the tribal problem.

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1941_pdf_p025.text:
The past, he said, was pregnant with the sad namories of a continuous war. As the policy of force and destruction of hun Ife had failed,it was their duty to try another method, one of friendship and brotherhood, and it was this monzage of good will that they had been ment to proclaim. They had come to convince their neighbours that they would share everything with them. Alluding once more to the heavy expenditure incurred in connexior with Waziristan, he said that it would have been quite a differ matter if the money had been collected in London instead of in India. The method of compulsory enlistment which had been adopted by Government was quite distinct from their ideas of inspiring confidence. since the Treaty of Versailles the world the had quite altered and today the victor and/vanquished were changing placeE. Hitler had risen because of the desire of Gemany to avenge her humiliation. The war was a war of destruction and nothing less than political suicide. II humanity adopted the principles of Congress and believed in Satyagraha then all men would live together as brothers.
A meeting, attended by about 120 persons, was held in Lakki on the 1st October. Dr. Dev Raj, Muhammad her and Pandit Sobha Ram of Lakki repeated their usual speeches.

C.I.D.Daily Diary dated 5.10.40.
CONGRESS Bhulabhai Desai and Asaf Ali addressed a second meeting which was held in Peshawar City on the 4th October, attended by about 1500 (10) persons. Both speakers mado much the same speeches as in the Peshawar Cantonment meeting, which was held on the 3rd. Bhulabhai Dogai said that the All-India Congress Committoo had decided that although they did not hold the reins of a national & vornmont yet it was imporativo to clarify their policy regarding their foreign relations
* pospone it until India was free.
Congress had decided to
8/3/200
mediately and not live poacofully with their neighbours and Was opposed to the treatment which the British Government metod out to them. Af or announcing that their object in visiting the Frontier was to deliver this mossage to the tribes, he described his exporiances in the Central Assembly where he said that out of a budget of 80 crores of rupees, the British Government wore opposed to allocating anything towards social and educational subjects. In order to justify their policy the British Government had announced that the tribesmen were brutes and had to be kept under control or they would attack Indians. Ho then described his visit to the Mohmand Border that after-noon and said that he personally could see no difference between the tribal area and British Territory except a line of demarcation which had been drawn by the British. They could have asked permission to visit Waziristan from Bombay and they would have en told that they could not do so. What they had expected had taken place and now they had been informed that they could not visit Waziristan. They could well imagine how strong a nation was which was afraid of allowing them to visit a free people! In the last war they had given lakhs of soldiers and crores of rupees but had received nothing in return.
Asaf Ali said that in the course of one year seven thousand bombs had been dropped in Waziristan and Afridi country and they could well imagine the sufferings undergone by the tribesmen. He desired to assure the tribesmen that Indians felt the dropping of these bombs just as much as the tribesmen themselves. Even if they could not visit the tribal aroa yet their voices would surely reach there. Indians, he said, were described as half civilized but were not the leaders of Europe in the same category? Crores of rupees were being spent on the war which could have been utilized to bettor effect in other ways. At the end of the last war Indians soldiers were praised but India herself received nothing.
After making the usual references to unity for the achievement of freedom, he described how regiments of the Indian Army wore organizod in Companies on a communal basis and no one dared remark that they desired to be free like British soldiers.
Bhulabha Desai and Asaf Ali left for lahore, en route, to Delhi by the Frontier Mail this morning.

A public meeting of the Peshawar Cantt Congress Committee, attended by about 300 people, was held at the Fanw Fanwara Chowk,Peshawar Cantt:, on the night of 8-9-1940, under the presidency of Jukam Chand, M.L.A., of Kohat. Before commencement of the regular proceedings Shamby Nath, Dwarka Nath" Baghi", Kundan Ial and Ram Saran of Peshawar Cantt: recited some old poems. The former two raised shouts of "Inqilab Zindabad" and "Crush the Government". L. Hukam Chand M.L.A.,, in his presidential address, informed the audience that a meeting of the Ministers and M.L.As. had been been convened during the day to discuss the resolution passed by the All-India Congress Working Committee with a view to taking some further steps in the near future. Further he said that their this public meeting would probably be the last as soon after there would be a conflict between them and the Government. Gopal Das Khera in his usual types of speech, remarked that the circumstances showed that there would be a sudden conflict with the Govt. They should therefore get themselves ready for offering sacrifices. He further appealed to the congregation to organis organise themselves and fill in the satyagraha form as early Bakhshi Faqir Chand "Vaid" moved a resolution as possible. which was passed, strongly criticising the Declaration re: H.E. the Viceroy's Executive Council; considering it to be totally against India's independence and an attack on the principles of Democracy and Nationalism and demanding a complete independence for India to the entire satisfaction of its people. Speaking on the European War, he referred to the old demand of the Congress and said that as the British were not ready to accept its demands it was now essential for the country to offer sacrifices. Mr. Bhanju Ram "Gandhi" speaking in the same strain, remarked that when the British were fighting for the Independence of England there was no reason why should not the Indians fight for the freedom of their country. At the present juncture it was incumbant on the British to declare that the Indians had the same rights in India as the British As the circumstances had proved that India could in England. not easily attain its object, her people were compelled to follow in the footsteps of other nations to secure freedom for her. Referring to the speech of Mr. Amery, giving a clear challenge with the word "No", he explained that it was not far when he (Mr. Amery) would be compelled to say "Yes". Sadhu Singh of Peshawar City moved a resolution condemning the attitude of the Government in treating Maulvi Abdul Rahim' "Popalzai" as an ordinary prisoner in jail and demanded a special class for him.

(i) After Juma prayers in Mohabat Khan's mosque on 6-9-1940, a public meeting of the Khudai Khidmatgars attended by about 350 persons was convened under the presidency of Ashaq Shah of Deh Bahadur. The president, referring to the resignation of Abdul Ghafar Khan from Congress, remarked that there was no difference between the Congress and the Khudai Khidmatgars because their aims and objects were the same, i.e. to attain independence. Their goal was the same, though they had adopted different routes to reach it. They were quite prepared to join hands with Congress if it launched a civil disobedience campaign. Arbab Abdul Ghafur Chafur Khan made a similar speech. He impressed on the audience that Abdul Ghaffar Khan resigned from the Congress simply because Congress had said that it would fight against the enemy of the British if it attained independence and this was against the non-violent principle of the Khudai Khidmatgan He further made an anti-Recruitment speech remarking that it was a shameful act on the part of a Pathan to run hither and thither for securing a service of Rs.10/-only. In the end he emphasised that it was kupur(infidelity) to help the British at this juncture. Ashaq Shah of Deh Bahadur speaking for the second time, said that the time for war was approaching and they should prepare themselves to take part in the war which would soon be started against the British by acting on the policy of non-violence.

the Islamic Law even a king, he remarked,, had to obey the orders of the Shariat. Referring to the Afghanistan Government, he remarked that the king could not pass any order without first referring the matter to the Jamiat-ul-Ulema. Maulvi Gulbadshah narrated the services of the cause of Islam and

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1941_pdf_p046.text:
THE TRIBUNe Z-9-76
"REVOLT" AGAINST FRONTIER GANDHI
RED SHIRTS' RESOLVE
CONFIDENCE IN A.I.C.C. EXPRESSED
(From Or Own Correspondent) Peshawar, Sept. 2
the
the
the
An important meeting of Red Shirt Officers of Peshawar City, under the charge of Jarnail Khan Mohd., was held on Satur- day, Mr. Ahmed Khan presiding. In an impassioned speech Jarnail Khan Mohd., reviewing the recent developments in the country, es- pecially those regarding the policy of the Congress about violence and non-violence, as embodied in the Delhi and Wardha resolutions, the resignation of Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, and its repercussions on the Frontier Congress polities, said that the Red Shirts of Peshawar City had full confidence in policy and the leadership of A.I.C.C. and would have nothing to do with the Afghan jirga if it was formed independent of the A.I.C.C. The meeting, which was attended by Red Shirt officers from several wards of the city including those from Bhanamari Railway station, Hashtnagri, Ghal- lamandi, Gunj, Chaukyadar and Lahori, passed a resolu- tion pledging unflinching obedience to Jarnail Khan Mohd. and declaring that they would not obey the orders of any body other than Jarnail Khan Mohd and the A.I.C.C. under whose lea- dership they were ready to lay down their lives. They had made sacrifices as Congress members for years, and would continue to do so in future, however great the suffer- ing it might entail. The resolution also says that they would have direct relationship with the Con- gress High Command through their Jarnail. This resolution of the Red Shirt officers, under whose command there are about 500 Khudai Khidmatgars, is being in- terpreted as a standard of revolt against the poliéy of Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan.
8/3/281)
Kartar Singh F.P. S.
7/9/40

STRICTLY
SECRET
G.I.D. Daily Diary dated 12.10.1940.
Khanair
CONGRESS. A public meeting under the auspices of the District Congress Comittee was held in Mohabat Ahan's fosque, Feshener City, after Juna Prayers on the 11th October, attended by about 500(15) persons. The speakers were Ashiq Shah of Deh Bahadur Khanair Hilali, Hakim Abdul Jalil advi and Dr. Then Sahib. They all alluded to the arrest of Arbab Abdul Chefur under the Defence of Indie Rules. Ashiq Shah sold that such an errest was no novelty and he wished to congratulate the Arbab and assura bin that they would all follow in his footsteps. He had been arrested by the British morely for expressing his views in a public speech, for them to live such a life of slavery was nothing but a curse. The British were living at the expense of Indiens and the bullets which were used against then were bought with Indien money. Hilali said that a demand for their legitimate rights nas considered to be an offence and that was why Arbab Abdul Ghafur had been arrested. After congratulating him for being taken into custody he condemned what he called Government's oppressive policy to illustrate which he also quoted the arrest of Abdul Rahim Popalsai! Both Arbab Abdul Ghafur and Abdul Rahim Fopalal had cursed Imperialism and larien and that was their only offence. After Eakin Abdul Jalil had moved a resolution demanding better treatment for Abdul Rakim Popalsai in Jall, Dr. Then Sahib made a speech. He said that Khudal Khidmatgers had taken a pledge navor to rest until they had achieved freedom for their country. Government could use all its powers but they would attain liberty in the end. The British were frightening them by teaching them air raid precautions although they were not afraid themselves. even though their country was being bombed. When he was Premier the Bannu road had been closed but this did not apply to him and such things were merely done to frighten them. He then alluded to the arrest of Arbab Abdul Ghafur and said that he had been taken from the Jell to a bungalow in handcuffs. As long as they were slaves this was the sort of treatment which would be meted out to their leaders. German prisoners of war were treated better than Indians though the former were actually bombing England. Referring again to the hand- cuffing of Arbab Abdul Chafur, he said that whenever an official node a mistake efforts were made to conceal it and this showed how dishonest the Coverment men. The founda- tions of the British Covernment were being shaken and it would surely fell. Congress, hrars and Khaksere were really all the same as restrictions had been imposed on each organiza tion. If they all stood together they would be in a position to show their power.
e

8/3/21 (1)
Peshawar City Meeting.
This meeting was held on the night of 29/30.9.45, at the Hastings' Memorial, Almost all the speakers made election propaganda,culogising the Congress and the sacrifices of the Congressmen which had brought about the present change. They cricitised the Muslim Leeue and the Muelin League Leaders as Capitalists and egents of the Britishere. Abdul Qayum, Barrister was particularly mentioned in this connection. Quit India' was mentioned as the Congress issue for the fighting the elections by most of the speakers,and the saying of the oppossing party that Congress was & Hindu bedy, was discredited. M. Gul Badshah of Toru styled the Britishers as the chief enemy of the Muselmans who had according to him enslaved all the Islamic States. The Muslim Les gue ministry of the Frontier Province was held responsible for the present conditions which prevailed in this Province. He is brother of Chacha Mohd Yunis, a staunch Congress worker with down country connections. Is sonxof son-inlaw of A.G.Khan, well known leader of the Frontier.
He came to notice in the C.D.movement of 1942, when he resigned the post of Head-Master Islamia High School. Contested on Congress ticket for N.W.F.P. Legislative Assembl seat vacated by Pir Bakhsh in 1943, but was defeated by a Muslim League candidate named Ghulam Hussain of Peshawar City He made speeches requesting votes. Once he said that the Congressites were wedded to the creed of non-violence and which they were stictly observing. He requested the Police tc curb all illegal acts of the mischief-mongers.
In January 1944, he along with other Congress leaders of Peshawar viz Dr. Khan Sahib, Ali Gul etc., was invited to attend xth by Malik Akbar Ali Khan, M.L.A. Congressite and 'A' Olass History sheeter, to participate the celebration of Independence day. Again in April 1944, he was invited to attend Bannu District Political Conference held at Lakki
on 10th and 11th April 1944. He attended both the functions. In his speech at Lakki he said that their fight with the Britishers
was on and exhorted his listeners to obey the orders of their leaders and continue the fight without any hitch and hesitation until otherwise ordered.
Again appointed Head Master Islamia High School when Congress Ministry came in power in 1945, but resigned later to contest the NW.F.P. Legislative Assembly election on Congress ticket, to which he was elected and appointed Minister of Education.

The attached poster was made over to me by a contact of mine today the 30th June, 1945, with the information that the like of it were distributed at prayer-time of M.Gandhi in the grounds near the Menor Villa, Summer Hill, Simla, on the 28th June, 1945. He has also come to know that they were said to have been distributed by Ganga Singh of Kasauli and Ram Lubhaya of Gujranwala. The contents of the poster are as below :-
"Keka Bachitar Singh"
Age 16 months.
(i)
"Bibi Harpal Kaur"
Age 9 years.
Children of S.Ganga Singh..
Who were shot dead by British soldier on 31st May, 1930, during civil disobedience movement in Peshawar and their mother Shrimati Tej Kaur severely wounded and too many more carriers of their bier and funeral processionists killed and wounded.
Ref. History of Congress, P.696
(Two photos of Kaka Bachitar Singh and Bibi Harpal Kaur- pasted).
Innocent blood of these children and numerous martyrs of Peshawar and India who gave their lives for freedom demand complete indepen- dence without communalism.
Please waste not their blood.
Ganga Singh, Kasauli".
It would appear that the poster does not bear the imprint
of the press. The subject matter is such that constitutes a prejudi- cial act punishable under section 38(5) DOIRS. Will you kindly register a case under section 38 Doirs for the purposes of investiga tion by the District Security Intelligence Staff.
S.H.0.Boileauganj P.S..

c_red_shirt_pesh_city_1945_p020.text:
37 Statement of Dr.Ganga Singh, G.P.V.C. s/o S.Kishen Singh, caste Kamboh, aged 55 years, actually a resident of village Khem Keran, Distt. Lahore, at present employed as Manager, Military Farms Kasauli.
I was first enlisted as a Veterinary Assistant in the Kashmir State Forces in 1911/1912 and served for about 8 years. I went to East Africa during the last war and was awarded the Indain Meritor- ious Service Medal and the Jengi Inam. After my retirement on Injury Pension from the Kashmir State Forces in 1919 I got employ- ment as a Supervisor in the Military Diary Farm at Kasauli in 1922. After serving at various stations I was posted to Peshawar in 1929 and remained there upto the Middle of 1930. In those days the Congress movement was at its height.on 31.5.30 I was proceeding to Gurdwara of Bhai Biba Singh in a tonga along with my wife and four minor children. Just as our tonga enterod at Kabuli Gate shots were fired at our tonga as a result of which my one son named Bachitter Singh aged 4 years and one daughter Harpal Kaur, aged 9/10 years were killed on the sopt end my wife Mst.Tej Kaur was severely Wounded in the chest. A large crowd collected on the spot and my dead children were taken to the Lady Reading Hospital, Peshwar from where the dead bodies were taken by lekim Abdul Jalil Nadvi, by the order of D.C.Peshwar. The dead bodies were taken into procession and later creminated. An enquiry was held into this shooting affair and the B.O.R. (L/C Cumming of K.0.Y.L.E) responsible for this unfortunate affair was convicted and sentenced to 18 months R.I. by A.D.M.Peshwar (Sir 0.K.Caroe). Dr. Khan Sahib (How Premier, NW.F.P.), R.S.Mehar Chand Khanna and other leading personalities of Peshawar came to sympathise with me in this tragic affair and Dr.Khan Sahib personally attended my wife in his professional advisory capaxcity and treated her for about 2 months. I was also asked by the Govt. to accept monetary compensation for the death of my children but I refused. After this incident I was transferred. from Peshawar and I did not meet any political worker of Peshwar. I also received a letter from the Govt. of India, though the Chief Commissioner, N.W.F.P. as well as C.C. of the Unit expressing regret and sympathy for this tragic affair. Even the accused L/C Cunning, sent me a letter of regret end sympathy through a Padri.. I was satisfied with their expression of sympathy and left the whole affair in God's hand and did not persue it any further.
On the 24.6.45 I happened to be at Kalka in connection with my official duties, when Dr.Khan Sahib arrived from Peshawar and I K saw him at the Kalka Rly Station and just wished him. On 26.6.45 I went to Golon in connection with my official duty and proceeded to Simla to pay my respects to Dr.Khan Sehib, who had been so kind to me during the tragedy rt Feshawar in 1930. I entrained from Salon by the 11 a.m. train and met Ram Lubhaya, a clerk in the First National Bank of India, Kasauli, who was also proceeding to Simla on that date. On arrival at Simla we went together to the Bazar to have our food at the Peshawari Khalsa Hotel, Lower Bazar, Simla, and then went to the Cecil Hotel. Ram Lubhaya remained outside the hotel and I went in to see Dr.Khan Sahib. The Dr. was sitting in the lounge and I met him there. I paid my respects to him and remained with him for only 3/4 minutes. R.B.Mehar Chand Khanna was also sitting there. It was at about 6 p.m. that I visited them and then left for Summer Hill to have a Darshan (I mean only to see) M.Gandhi at the prayers time, as lie was expected to care out at that time. It began to rain at that time and we both returned to the rain Rly.Station, Simla to catch & train back to Dharampur. We left by the 9.55 p.m. train and reach- ed Kessuli the same night. On 28.6.45, an English poster bearing the photographs of the ildren of S. Ganga Singh, who were killed in the Congress agi- on of 1930 at Peshawer were distributed in Simla by some persons. opy of the poster is enclosed for your information.
It is suspected that S. Ganga Singh,Manager, Military Diary Farm, Kesauli Who was posted to Peshawar when this incident took. place, is at the bottom of this show, though he denies any knowledge regarding the printing & distribution of these posters. A copy of his statement recorded during the investigation of the above case is attached herewith for your information. It is clear from his state- ment that during the shooting incident of 1930 at Peshawar Dr.Khan Sahib and other Congress workers of Peshawar sympathised with him and tried to make capital out of this unhappy tragedy. I shall be obliged if you will kindly let me know the past history of Dr.Ganga Singh during his stay in Peshawar in 1930, with particular reference to his association with the Congress workers of Peshawar during this period.
An early reply is requested.

When his two children were shot by accident in Peshawar City,
on 31st May, 1930, some Congress workers sympathised with him in public meetings and thereby he gained some impart momentary importance
in Congress circles. The "Adib", a monthly magazine of Peshawar, reproduced the picture of his dead children, which had appeared in 'Milap' of Lahore, dated 12th June, 1930.
Speaking at the 'Chautha' ceremony of his children, performed in Gurdwara Bhai Biba Singh, Peshawar City, he contradicted the rumour that he had received compensation from the Government for his children. He preferred to sacrifice the rest of his children if

From 19.00 hours to 21.00 hours on 23.9.45,a private meeting of the Peshawar City Congress Committee was held at their office under the presidentship of Hakin Abdul Jalil Nadvi. Amongst 38/40 members Mehta Madan Lal Pleader, Mohd Afzal; Kotangar, Mohd Yunis Qureshi, Ram Saran Nagim,Melik Chand, Paryag Nath,Kundan Lal,Abdul Karim Charkhi, Sheikh Mujahid,Haji Mohd,Abdul Wadud and General Wali Mohd had also attended the meeting. It is said that Abdul Karim Charkhi strongly opposed the to re-admit Mohd Usman Nasvari into the Congress. He said that Mohd Usman was a traitor to the Congress and now, if any body plead his cause, would also be a traitor. A couple of resolutions were also moved. In one of them K.S. Mohd Yunis, Secretary of Peshawar Muncipal Committee, was bitterly criticised for being working dishonestly in preparing the lists of the voters and his immediate transfer was unanimously demanded from the Provincial Government. Mohd Yunis was dubbed a Muslim Leaguer from whom in addition to the Congressmen the Khaksars and the Ahrarism were also expecting no. jxt fair play. In The second resolution, which was not edopted, was moved by Mohd Yunis Qureshi, ples ding to rescind the resolution of the Peshawar City Congress Committee in which Congressmen had been fobind forbidden to participate in the meetings and processions of the 'Anjume n-i-Ghuraba. Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi was the chief of opposer of this resolution.

From 2030 hours to xxxix 2315 hours on the night of 23/24.10.45,a Congress meeting, attended by about 1500/2000 (43) persons,was held at the Hastings' MemorialPeshawar City. Mohd Yunas Qureshi was in the chair. About 15x 50% of the audience were Hindus and Sikhs. The president of the meeting, Mohan Singh Sani, president of the Youth Congress Punjab, Jan Mohd,Medical Practioner of Peshawar City, Amar Kaur andGopal Singh Qaumi of the Punjab, delivered speeches,while Ghulam Khan,Balwant Singh Almast and Adam Khan of Peshawar City,recited political verses. The trend of the speeches was in the nature of the electioneering compaign in course of which the Congress was eulogised at the expense of the Muslim League, the Akali Dal,Hindu Mahasabha and their leadership. All these were described as Communal Body and their efforts were deemed to strengthenxx the foreign domination in this country. They also rediculed the slogans of these communal leaders that Islam,Sikhism and Hinduism were in danger. Master Tara Singh and Ajit Singh,M.L.A.,were the main target. Mohd Yunas Qureshi and Mohan Singh Sani also brought in the impending Court Martiol of some of the I.N.A. and the latter warned the Government that a great upheaval would ensue if all of them were not released,and for which the whole responsibility will txx lie on the Government. Amar Kaur likened the Indian National Congress as a Frontier Mail withx or the 'Toofan Mail',with Mr.Gandhi, the Lango tband Faqir' of Sanbar Mati Ashram" as its engine, and added that it will trampled down everything which came its way. She also thought the secession of Mian Iftikharuddin, of the Punjab, and Abdul Qayum, Barrister, of Peshawar City, from the Congress and joining hands with the Muslim League of no importance and simply based on selfish motives. Almost all the speakers recalled the past services of the Congress and particularly those of the Civil Disobedience Movement of 1942.
NOTE;- Before the termination of the meeting Mohd Yunas
Qureshi announced a Congress meeting for the night of 0.45 21X25 at the Fawara Chauk in Peshawar Sader and another at the Chauk Yadgar Peshawar City for 27.10.45.

Mohd Ayub of Peshawar Sedar was in the chair. Mohd Yunas Qureshi,Mohd Ayub, Jan Mohd,medical pratix practitioner, Arbab Abdur Rahman Khan,M.L.A.,delivered speeches,while Adam Khan xxt Cart-driver, Mohd Hazir and Ram Seran Negins, recited po political verses,cxixix. The speeches and the verses related to the alleged death of Said Akber by the beating of Police and the beating and arrest of A.G.K. on 27.10.42 for which the Police was bitterly criticised, Arbab Abdur Rahman Khan also spoke against the Muslim League and its Pekiston Scheme and criticised some Government servants for making pro-Muslim League propaganda. In this connection a matx prive te meeting of the Congress workers Union was held at the office of the Provincial Congress Committee in the Qissa Khani bazar on 9.11.45 under the president shipaf of Pandit Ragu Nath, Pleader, in which ,in a resolution, the Hon'ble Doctor Khan Sahib was requested to prevail upon Yaya Jan not to withdraw his name. The release of Captain Shah Nawaz,Saigel and Dhalwan was celebrated in Peshawar City by some of the Congress workers and illuminations were made on the night of xt 3/4.1.46. Acheraj Ram Gomendi also distributed some sweets. The photo es of the three officers had been exhibited at the Balcony,a few crackers were exploded and about a dozen Congress men were shouting the typical Congress slogans. Ghulam Mohd alies Gamais house was also illuminated. In Koocha Chakke Gali one Manohar Singh shopkeeper had improvised a decorated gete on which the photoes of the three I.N.A.Officers and of Bose and Gandhi had been exhibited. A Congress flag had also been put on the gate. On a few other cart-boards the wounded children of Ganga Singh had been shown and xx o tes had been asked for Saran Singh,Pleader,. In Pati Dhauna and Bazar Bakhshi Ram Sei illuminations had been made end band was playing which had attracted some 70/80 persons. Taking advantage of the gathering in Pati Dhauna Mehta Madan Lal, Pleader, Mohd Yuuss Qureshi and Sadhu Singh spoke on the I.N.A.and the efforts of the Congrese in releasing the three officers and also made election propagende for the Congress nominee. Her Gobind Singh recited political verses.
NOTE:- It is reported that these illuminations were made by the Hindus and the Sikhs in Peshawar City, but at the most by about 1% of them.
After the termination of the meeting Wali Mohd, Redshirt General,moved back his 49 uniformed Redshirts in a line and after passing through various bazars xxx dismissed them at about 1630 hoursxx inside the Bajori Gete Peshawar City on 4.1.46.

In meeting No.10 Mohd Saber and in meeting No.11 Adam Khan,Cert Manand Mohd Nazir, Tee Agent, recited political verses. Ghulam Mohd alies Gama ia course of his speechheld the Muslim Leaguers aggressors in Matani,Landi and Chamke ni during the polling and in meeting No.11 Hussain Bakhsh Kauser criticised Government servents ax and traders for acting against the interests of the Congress.

Turlabh Singh eulogised the Congress flag and the acitivities of the I.N.A.,while Amar Singh Sher-i-Punjab remarked that the Britishers would leave India at once if these 'todiot' cease working for them..

The details of the proceedings of the above meeting have not so far been recieved.

In this meeting B Balwant Singh Almast and Harbans Singh of Peshawar City, as well as Amar Singh Ajiz of Peshawar City, recited political verses. Tarlochan Singh,in course of his usual speech, praised the Russian Revolution. Turlabh Singh and Amar Singh Sher-i-Raajal Punjab spoke against the Akalis, the former praised the Congress flag and the latter recommended to fk follow the lesson, taught by the personnel of the I.N.A.

A most reliable source reports that Daswandi Mall, Ram Chauk, Peshawar, has suggested the follh ing to Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, President, Intian National form Congress, at Calcutta :-
COMING FAXIN AND ITS REMEDIES. "
Moral duty of every living being is to avert famine ar everyone should feel the responsibility to save the life of his fellowmen. Bearing this in mind, an appeal should be submitted to Government, the leaders of the nation, and to every noble man of India, to take practical measures against this dreadful and unexpectedcalamity :-
1. Govt. should bar the export of the following commodities :-
Foodgrains, rice, pulses, raw and manufactured sugar and their products.
b. Milk and its products.
Fruits (dry and fresh), meat, eggs, fish and it's, products, and all kinds of animals.
2. Butchering of milk-giving animals. should be forbidde by law.
3. The number of visitors to a party or feast must be controlled (say limited to 25 men).
4. Ways of irrigation etc should be improved.
5. Better seeds should be provided to the farmers to increase their yield.
6. Schemes concerning the improvement of the animal breed should be encouraged.
7. Advanced methods should be explained to the people to increase the output of fruit and milk in our country.

Dy.No.252-NGC.
A most reliable source reports that Madan Lal Kakar (or Kabar) of Mohallah Kakran, Dabgari Gate, Peshawar City, has informed Sarat Chandra Bose, M.L.A. Central, 1 Woodburn Park, Elgin Road, P.0. Calcutta, that millions of lambs and kids are slaughtered every year for the sake of their fur- skins which are exported to England and America to be made into ladies fur-coats. They are slaughtered after 3 or 4 days after birth, as afterwards the fur becomes heavy and thick. Their main collecting centres are Delhi and Multan. This is having a disastrous effect on the pregnancy of sheep and goats besides reducing the amount of meat for the nutritions of the people. Madan Lal further informed him that he knew that the present Govt. cares more for the fur coats for ladies of America and Europe than for the good of starving Indians, and that price of meat is so high that a poor man could not buy it. Madan Lal further advised him to bring the matter to the notice of the Govt.

At about 1900 hours on 9.2.46 Arbab Abdur Rahman Khan of Guli Garhi was taken in a procession by about 200/300 persons including 20/25 in red uniforms, from the District Courts towards the City of Peshawar. When the procession was passing near the Bajori Gate one G-hulam Haider, Tea Seller, fired some 4/5 gun shots and one xx other fired a few pistol shots. The procession dispersed near the Provincial Congress office at about 1940 hours. Throughouttax the way the usual anti Jinnah and anti Muslim League slogans were being shouted out.
PESHAWAR CANTONMENT: On the evening of 9.2.46 some 100'cycle- Swars' of the Congress Party, peddled
in the Peshawar Sadar bazars, the Arbab Road and the other roads and then entered the City of Peshawar via the Dabgari Gate. The usual anti Government and pro Congress slogans were being shouted out throughout the way.

PESHAWAR CITY:
After the announcement of the election results on 13.2.46 at the Court of the City Magistrate,Peshawar, some 1000/1500 persons, including Hakim Abdul Jalil Na dvi, Chacha Mohd Yunas, Taj Mohd, Lal Mohd and Wali Mohd, xxx Generals,with 35 uniformed Redshirts, took Yahya Jan in a procession to Peshawar City and moved him through various streets and bazars. It was at about 2115 hours that a part of the pronessionists in the bazar Kalan met with some Muslim League workers,who were coming back to the Muslim League office after the termination of their their meeting and raised voices against them. That party dispersed at about 2130 hours near the Provincial Congress office after Yahya Jan thanking them for their supportxx.. Yahya Jan had been profusely garlanded and about 51 'Goalas' had also been exploded. Throughout the way pro Congress and anti Muslim League slogans were being shouted out. There were about 700/800 persons in this procession. From 2115 hours to 2130 hours on the night of 13.2.46 some 400/500 persons assembled at the Chauk Yadgar,who were addressed by Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi. He told them that Yahya Jan could not come to the Chauk Yadar und so he,on behalf of Yahya Jan and the Peshawar City Congress Committee,was thanking them all for supporting the nominee of the poor organisation. He added that the voters of Peshawar had shown that the great majority of the Musalmans were Ex against the Pakistan. He also informed them that in Peshawar Sadar the Akali Sikhs had injured some Hindu brothers,one of them succumbing to the injuries. On this a voice said that the information was wrong. This attracted a number of Hindus who hurried towards the voice xh which was of one Akali Sikh, but the Police intervened and took him to his house. Later the gathering dispersed.

GENERAL: PESHAWAR:
5/2B (1)
It is reported that at about 1130 hours on
24.2.46 Pera Khan, accompanied by some 25 others from D.I.Khen,errived in a lorry at the residence of the Hon'ble Doctor Khan Sahib and expressed a desire to meet Maulana Abul Kalam Azad, but failed to have an interview with the Congress president. Do ctor Khan Sahib is said to have told them that dick disciplinary action had been taken against them and that they had supported Pera Khan in the past elections so they could not meet Maulana Azad, therefore all of them went back.
It is further reported that Sant Singh, Editor of the Frontier Advocate,and Lakhmir Singh of Peshawar City, and Tota Singh of Peshawar Cantonment,had requested Doctor Khan Sahib on telephone on 24.2.46 for fixing time when they could see Maulana Abul Kalam Azad,and they were replied that the Maulana was sleeping. Again some 20 Akali Sikhs visited the Chief Minister's Bungalow while the private meeting of the Congress Parliamentary Party was in progress. They waited for about half an hour but went back without meeting the President of the All India Congress. Similarly Mehdi Zaman Khan of Khalabat with three others came and went back without meeting the Maulana. (Mehdi Zaman Khan is an Ahrar Leader from Hazara District).

On the night of 1.3.46(2005 hours to 2145 hours), a Congress meeting,attended by about 1500(12) persons, was held at the Chauk Yedger Peshawar City,under the presidentshipax of Ghulam Jilani of Peshawar City. Before the proceedings of the meeting had started Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi explained the aims and objects of the meeting. He said that the meeting was being held as a protest against the conviction of Captain Abdur Rashid and Captain Burhanuddingx fx and the prosecution of the rest of the parsonnel of the I.H.A. and also against the action being taken tx against the R.I.N.Men despite the assurance given to Maulana Azed by thetx C-I C that no one will be victimised. Ra Saran Nagina recited political verses and Aghe Mazaffar Shah, Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi,Pandit Ragu Na th,Pleader,and Chacha Abdul Kerim of Peshawar City and Gopal Dass Khere of Peshawar Cantt; delivered speeches. it was tho BxIxEx C-In-C who should be Court Martialled, because he was the person,who had sat tight over their demands. Chacha Abdul Karim supported the resolution. Hakim Abdul Jalil Nadvi spoke once again before the termination of the meeting,announcing, the coming of Maulana Abul Kalam Azed to Peshawar on 4.3.46 with a view to form the Congress Ministry and also to construct a programme. He also informed his audience that a meeting of the Congress workers from throughout the province will be held at the Serderyab Markaz on 3.3.46 and a public meeting in Peshawar City on 5.3.46 in which Maulana Azad will also participa te.

and Hian Jafar Shah,M.L.As., and Mohd Ibrahim Khan, candida te elect to the Frontier Legislative Assembly, in the meetings at Mugdarzai,Badizai and Regi, were of the usual type, relating to the electioneering struggle. They were telling the people how the votes had been won by the shedding blood by the Redshirts and how it was now kkaRE their duty to cast them for the Redshirt-cum-Congress candidate. They spoke of the Muslim League as a party organised by the Britishers with a view to strengthen their rule for all times in this country. The cry of Pakistan,raised by these people, was but a faxafarce, and was being raised only to put the Pathans on a wrong way. Arbab Abdur Rehman Khan once said that the Britishers were Badmashes and would be quitting the country sooner or later. Ghulam Mohayuddin and Mohd Nazir recited their usual political verses in the above meetings.

A meeting of the villagers of Abdara, Naudeh Payan and Sufaid Dheri under the preseidentship of Abdul Qayyum, Secretary, Congress Committee was held in village Pawaka on 20.8.41. The object of the meeting was to stress on the Govt: that either the Bara Water should be given to them or the land Revenue be reduced.. On the same day a meeting of the entire Tappa Khalil and Mohand under the presidency of subedar Abdul Akber khan was held in village Pushtakhara. Tappa Mohmand C.C.,took place at Shahindan near Ahmed khel,P.S. Badaber on the afternoon of 13.4.42 with Abrahim Khan of Bhagrah in the chair. It was attended by about 500 (100) persons. Pushto political poems by Sheikh Fazal Rahim and sort speeches by Ashiq o Shah of dehbahader and other were made in connection with the constructive programme.Q.Attaullah,Pleader Mardan, was the only prominent speaker who made a lengthy speech explaining how the congress Ministery resigned.He atributed the failure to the selfishness of the members.Refering to the present war he advised the audience to become true K.Ks,and serve the nation and the country in time of need. Mr.Churchill who was not willing to give any further reforms to the Indians was compelled to do some favour to Indie being obliged by the state of affairs in Burma. He came to know that all the mordern armaments was nothing'

All Indians with a brother with a common object of freedom. Let all of us united and fight for the common cause. There should be no difference between the congress and the Muslim League.

J.P.S. Daily Diary. Dated 15.7.42.
Peshawar
prominent Redshirts of Peshower Last light ebo-ut 50 City are reported to h ve met into a private meeting in the 9/32) Congress office under the presidentship of Lal Mohd a 8/3/2 B(10) Redshirt General. The question of volunteer for the forth
W
comming Redshirt Camp was the subject matter under discussi It was decided that about 25 delegates will be sent which will be selected by Lal Mohd. The President informed the present that only those should volunteer who will promtly obey the order of A.G.K in accordance with the new scheme There was every likely hood that such volunteers might be ordered to go to T.T. for propaganda. It is further reported that Haji Mohd Naim Katchkole,Majahad Khan and Gopi Chand have volunteered themselves for the Camp.
It is reported that some hot words were exchange between Ashaq Shah of Deh Bahadar and Delber Khan a Redshirt General in a private meeting held at Phandu on 12.7.42. The former was charged for propagating against Dr.Khan in the Illaga in connection with his daughter 6s marrage. It
is said that he will convene a public meeting in the
Mosque of Mohabat Khan on the afternoon of 17.7.42 in which action of Dr. Khan will be criticised..

On 23.9.42, A.G.K. accompanied by Arbab Abdur Rehman Khan of G.Ghari proceeded to village Pawakai, where he addressed about 50 persons including 10 in red-uniform in the villege Mosque, after Peshin Prayers in his usual mann to unite and assist the Congress in its struggle for freeing India from the foreign rule. Arbab Abdur Rehman
Khen of G.Ghari also spoke in the same tune. The couple
then wont to village Tahkel and met Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khange ex-parlimentary secretary. They requested him to
eccompany them to village Sparsang, but he did not acceded
to their request. Ultimately they along with one other left in Tonge for village Spersang, where they reached before Khuften time'. Here again A.G,K. and Arbab Abdur- Rehman Khan of G.Ghari mede a repetition of what they had said in Pawakai before an audience about 60 (15) persons, at an open place outside the villege.
After the dispersal of meeting the party proceeded towerds village Kochian, where they stayed the night with Amin Jen, & Redshirt General of the village.
After offering the 'Pashin Prayers' in the Mosque A.G.K., returned to Peshawar, and getting in a Lorry, near
the Hashtnagri gete, proceed towards Nowshera and Marden.

A.G.K. accompanied by Babu Fazal Illahi reached village Pekha Ghulam,at about 13 hra,on 24.9.42, in a Tonge. About 200(40) persons were present in the
for about an hourhe village Mosque. A.G.K., spoke to them, and dilated upon his old theme that a flood
e

the Britishers should quit India, so that the Indians might not it He, next exhorted them to

they were helpless. He exhorted them to associate with ther (Policement, with a view to bring them to their own views. After offering the 'Pashin Prayers' in the Mosque A.G.K., returned to Peshawar, and getting in a Lorry, near the Hashtnagri gate, proceeded towards Nowshera and Marden.

C. I. D., I. P. S. Daily Diary 22.12.42.
MARDAN DISTRICT:
As aresult of a secret meeting, attended by about
20 Redshirt Workers including their Salar, Munir Khan, held on 23.1242, at a place called, Kajwan Mehra, two Redshirt volunteers, namely Rahim Shah s/o Alam Khan and Wasaf Shah s/o Isa Khan of village Tand Kohi, police station Swabi, in plain clothes, who were carrying their uniform and were proceeding towards Kohati Rest House for pieke ting, were arrested u/s 40 F.C.R. at Swabi, on 24.12.42. at
about 11.00. hours.
Again at about 16.00 hours on the same day the foll- -owing Redshirts volunteers turned up at the Kohati Rest House for picketing. They stealthyly entered the garden in front of the Rest House, having concealed their uniform under their" chaddars" of white colour. They shouted Anti-Government slogans and the police arrested all of them u/s 40 E.C.R. without any trouble.
1. Ali Gul s/o 'Maddat Khan of Kalu Khan P.S.Kalu Khan
2. Mir Dad 3/0 Dalil of Adina
3. Meskin s/o Murad Khan of Kalu Khan P.S.Kalu Khan.
4. Said Khastan s/o Ali Akbar
5. Swab Khan s/o Said Alam of
6. Abdur Rahim s/o Ibrahim
7. zarab Shah s/o Mahdi Shah
8. Qahar Khan s/o Jalal Khan
"1
9. Gul Said s/o Sharif Ullah of Takhta Band
For the second time again a party of 12 Redshirt
Volunteers in plain clothes turned up for picketing at the Rest House at about 16.30hours. This time they were on the Pakka Road in front of the Rest House, where the police arrested them u/s 40 F.C.R. The volunteers shouted the Congress slogans but offered no resistance hen they were being arrested. This batch was in in possession
two "Addresses" in handwriting for He E. the Governor. Copies are ached.

110In these addresses they wanted to show to the Governor that the Redshirts Movement had not died in that part of the province as he had been told by the pets of the Britishers in their addresses presented to him in his tour of the district in November last, and as had been alleged by the leader of the cheats, Brad Shaw, and the head of the devil, Mr. Wood.
N.B.
H.E. the Governor was away from the Rest House on that day (24.12.42 ) for Shekart towards Totali, Swat State, from 09.30 hours to 15.30 hours.
On 25.12.42, H.E. the Governor, had been toured Rana Dheri,. Some Redshirt Volunteers staged a demonstration and shouted Anti-Government slogans before the Governor, when he was on his way between Rana Dheri and Zaida. No arrests were made at that time.
Today again, when the Governor was away, 10 Redshirt Volunteers turned up at the Gohati Rest House and Shouted Anti-Govt. slogans. All of them were arrested by the police.

Mohd Azim asked all those present to make efforts for enlisting volunteers and collecting subscriptions for their expenses in Peshawar City.Shér Mohd is stated to have said that the general public were of opinion that Dr.Khan Sahib Arbab Abdur Rehman Khan, Abdul Qayum Barrister, and Qeim Shah pleader, were pro-Government people and that was why they had not been arrested so far. The policy of the Government in not arresting them was to create a suspicion into the public that they were pro- Government persons.

On 5.2.43 Hussain Shah, accompanied by 10 Redshirts in uniform, came to the Miangan Mosque in village Bahadur Killi,and said his Jumma Prayers. After the prayers he got up and requested the congregation of about 20 persons to stay and hear him. It is reported that all of them replied that they had had better experience of the Redshirts' Agitation of 1930 and that they had no faith in the present Congress Movement. Inspite of Hussain Shah's repeated appeals they left the Mosque and went to their houses, followed by Hussain Shah and his Redshirt Followers, who
dispersed broken hearted.
of Sheikh Killi, On 4.2.43 Sarfaraz and Amir/had come to
Peshawar to join the Redshirt compaign of picke ting on courts. Said Rekhman and Mohd Sherin followed them on the same day but they failed to meet them. However, they sent a message
to the commander of the Redshirts Camp that Amir and Serfaraz should not be deputed on the picketing duties or they would stop them in the way. Said Rekhman, however, succeeded
to take back his son Amir, to his house on 5.2.43,and Sarfaraz refused to go back with Mohd Sherin.x

villages of Takhtabad, Chagher Meti, Gerrenge 1, Shanda1,73 Garhi Nisatta and Charpariza, were so done during the Propaganda tour by Doctor Khan Sahib accompanied by Arbab Abdur Rehman,M.L.A., Ibrahim Khan of Jaggra, Sheikh Mohd H**** Jabin of Sheikh Killi,Abdur Rehim of Charperiza and Amin Khan of Gulabad, on 6.2.43 and 7.2.43. Dr.Khan Sahib delivered his speech in the Hujra, where he happened to go by calling up men from their houses. Every place he delivered his speech of the type usual to him and appealing for assistance, to continue the present struggle for Independence in the shape of picketing on courts.He also
told them not to pay Land Revenue and taxes to the Government and assured them that their properties would be restored to them by the National Government if they were attached and confiscated by the present usurper Government.
He also
them to be brave and to unite and thus would a surely evict the foreigners from their soil,
Arbab Abdur Rehman Khan spoke only at in
two meetings at Takhtabad and Chagher Mati, where he supported Dr.Khan Sahib and requested his listeners to accord every help to the dependence of those volunteers,who were away from their homes either in Jails or in the Peshawar City
Redshirts Camp.

and discussed the movement with Arbab Abdul Rehman.M.L.A., After a long discussion it was decided that picketers should should be sent not be sent daily to courts but on and off or once a week. me Ibrahim s/o Motabar Khan of Masho Khel with two Redshirts in uniform is reported to be touring in the Badaber 1llaqa in an endeavour to induce people to join the Redshirt movement but the response is not cheerful.

on 17.3.40, Mohd Azam, s/o Sarfraz, Secretary,
Congress Committee, Tehkal Bala, gave a dinner to Spin Badshah About nine other Redshirts joined the dinner. After dinner, Mohd Azam asked them to go in a Jirga to Sher Mohd s/o Fateh Khan of village Pawaka in order to request him to withdraw his resignation because in his absence it would be impossible to do Congress propaganda in this
party of the country.
Spin Badshah said that he had made persistent efforts in this case but had failed. His argument was that he could not continue in the movement owing to private affairs. Eventually,
it was decided that should they fail to persuade Sher Mohd
to withdraw his resignation, Mohd Azam should take charge of the Congress Committee, Tappa Khalil.

persons of villege Mashoo Khel and the villages in the environment,have asked one Mohd Ibrahim s/o Mohta bar, who is a
prominent Congress Worker in that locality, to dissociate
himself with the Congress Movement, which was a Hindu Body and detrimental to the Muslim Interest, or he would be boycotted by all the residents of all these villages. It is stated thet Mohd Ibrahim went to Chamkani on 22.3.43 and acquainted Musa Khan of the situation, who pacified him and asked him to go on with his work and do not pay any attention to the sayings of those persons which were simply a bluf.
It is reported that Mohd Azam Khan of Tehkal Bela has been appointed Secretary of the Tappa Khalil Congress Committee ixxthaxplaza vice Sher Mohd who has resigned, on 20.3.43. It is stated that Mohd Azam has been ixtrustad instructed to spread Anti Pakistan Propaganda in his illaqa.

c_red_shirt_pesh_dist_1941_42_p075.text:
Brief note on Abdul Majid, son of Samundar Khan, Khalil of Pawacka, S. Burj Hari Singh, Peshawar District.
Red-Shirt General of Tappa Khalil. His brother Abdul Qaiyum is Secretary of the Congress Committee.
The
He was convicted and sentenced to two years' R.I. under section 17 (2) C.L.A.Act, in connexion with the Civil Disobedience Movement. He was released on 30.11.1933, after he had been served with an order under section 5(1)(d) of the N.W.F.P. P.T.Act, 1932, by the City Magistrate, Peshawar. He is a keen orgin organiser of the Red-shirts. He also collects subscriptions for the Congress Fund. In August, 1940 he was elected a member of the Sub-Committee formed in illaqa Daudzai. object was to get the SATYAGARHA forms filled up. In January, 1943 he went to Tirah in the company of Ashaq Shah of Deh Bahadur. It was reported that he was spreading Congress propaganda there. He returned from Tirah and visited the Red-shirt Camp in Peshawar City on 9.2.43. It was reported. that he told Sher Ali Khan of Parn that the Afridis were ready to assist the Congressites in their anti-Government activities. He was further reported to have told him that the Afridis had prevented Government from carrying on Defence Works in the Khyber Agency.
Ilumain 29/3/43.

Abdul Majid of faoks.
Reference your Mem. No.1197-CC, dated the 27th
March, 1943.
Abdul Majid first came to notice when he was convicted in the civil disobedience movement in 1933. In August 1940 he was elected a member of a Congress Sub Committee formed in the Daudzai area, whose function was to obtain ch satyagrah forms filled up. In January, 1943, he went to Tirah with Ashaq Shah where he was reported to be spreading Congress propaganda- He visited the Redshirt Camp in Peshawar City on the 9th of February. In my opinion he is a Congress work er of some
importance and he should be arrested.
Asstt. to the Inspector-General of Police, C.I.Department, N.W. F. Province

1.P.S. Daily Diary dated 9.4.1943. 123.
On 8.4.43(1515 hours to 1700 hours) a Congress meeting, attended by about 250/275 persons including 36 in red uniform,25 children and 10 women, was held at a place called Shahidan police station Badaber, under the Presidency of S.Qeim Shah,Pleader,. Dr.Khan Sahib, Ashiq Shah of Badabar Killi, Musa Khan of Chamkani, Sher Ali of Prang,Mohd Khan
of Ahmad Khel, and Yusaf Gul of Nissata, were prominent amongst the gathering. Ashiq Shah of Bahadar Killi, Dr.Khan Sahib, and Qaim Shah, pleader, delivered speeches,while Sher Dil of Bahadar Killi, Abdullah of Chamka ni, and Gulab of Pishtara Khara Payan, recited verses of the political nature.
Ashiq Sheh of Bahadar Killi repeated his previous speech made in a Congress meeting held on 29.3.43 at Nimak Mandi,Peshawar City. In his speech Ashiq Shah incited his listeners to avenge the wrong done to their leader(A.G.K.) and also repremanded the Pathans for their slackness while the females of the down country were fighting side by side with their menfolk for achieving the independence of the country, He further discribed the hardship thereby crowding the Jails. which he had undergone during his five months stay in the Tirah but assured the audience that he had conveyed A.G.K's message of non-violence to the Tirahwals in a satisfactory manner and had brought them to his own view with the promise that they were one with their Pathan brothers living in the British Territory. He also mentioned that the Government had fixed Rs.2000/- for his life in the tribal territory but thank God, he said, that he returned from the hills quite safe.Concluding he observed that he was not afraid of being arrested and challenged the Sub-Inspector of Police, who was present in
22/4/43

c_red_shirt_pesh_dist_1941_42_p078.text:
-2-
(125
the meeting, to arrest him, announcing that he would fight non-violently against the Government untill independence was achieved.
Dr.Khan Sahib delivered his stereotyped speech
in which he emphasised the no rent compaign and exhorting the Military and Police Men and others to leave their services.
wear red clothes and join the National Army. He further exhorted the Police Men present in the meeting to take possession of the Police Stations and informed him latter thatxthayxkadx on telephone that they had done so. In conclusion he again extended a warning to the traitors of the country that they would be punished with death during the regime of the National Government.
DrxkhaxxSahib S.Qaim Shah, pleader, severely
criticised those,who were sending their children to the Battle Front for a meagre sum of Rs.15/- and thus were committing treachery with their country men. He appealed the residents of Tappa Mohmands to wear red uniform and. come into the Battle Arena for liberating the country from the foreign domination. The next of his speech was in the above strain.
N.B.
It is reported that the speeches had had no effect on the audience, and the t they considered the speakers as the real traitors, who and particularly Ashiq Shah, who had recently mortgaged 60 Kanals of Land from the Congress Fund. After the termination of the meeting the
Redshirt Volunteers including Ashiq Shah, returned to the
Peshawar City Redshirts Camp, where they arrived at 1830 hours

of Shahi Payan Mokarab Khan of Sarband held a private

also
this time. Saher Khan/spoke in the above strain. Mokarab Khan replied to them in suitable words. He said that the residents of Sarband were national in true sense and were prepared to make every sacrifices, but they were tired of the present day Congress Leaders, who were selfish and working for their personal interests.. In this connection he mentioned Ali Gul Khan, who had earned thousands of rupees At last it was decided by selling sugar etc to the masses. to tour the illaqa of Tappa Mohmand and Khalil for the spread of the Congress Propaganda.X

thrive
would never rixa, because the Muslim Leaguers were hired
people and the general public have no confidence in any one them. Musa Khan exhorted them to enlist more and more Redshirts and prepare them for making sacrifices for the cause of the country and the Nation..

enacted again, and therefore, they should all join the Congress with a view to oust the Britishers from this country.

I.P.S. DAILY DIARY DATED 25.6.1943.
1: On 23.6.43 (1315 hours to 1400 hours) a Congress meeting, attended by 27 persons including 15 in red uniform, was held at an 'Arhat' in village Sheri Kera police station Mathani,under the Presidency of Inzar Gul of the same village.
Fazal Rahim of Sheikhan and Ashiq Shah of Bahadur Killí delivered their usual speeches.
N.B.
This meeting had been announced by Ashiq Shah and others in all the villages of Sheri Kera Illaga,but only 12 persons had responsed. After the termination of the meeting Ashiq Shah was told that they were not prepared to go to the Jails, ixxthexexoritx xxx leaving behind their dependence uncared for in these critical days.
Ashiq Shah and his party then proceeded to V.Pasanni station Mattani.
police
On 23.6.43 (1700 hours to 1815 hours) a Congress meeting, attended by about 150 persons including 15 in red uniform,was held in village Pasanni, police station Mattani. Maulvi Abdul Halim of the same village presided.
Fazal Rahim of Sheikhan and Shiq Shah of Bahadur Killi delivered speeches, while Gulah of Pushta Khara sang songs in Pushto,which were afxtha political in aux nature.
The speakers spoke in their usual ways.Fazal Rahim made an emphasis on the fact that inspite of the wholesale arrests the Congress Leaders in this province and particularly in the Peshawar District, were not arrested; because the Government and its agents,Mirza Iskandar D.C.Peshawar and K.B.Quli Khan,wanted to create a misunderstanding in the minds of the Redshirts against the unarrested leaders. While Ashiq Shah was speaking one Hajam Khan of Pasanni xd put some question to him which he could not satisfactorily replied. One of the question was that the Congressites should come out with guns and rifles to oppose the foreigners to settle the matter once for all, and they would join them, otherwise they would not succeed. Ashiq Shah replied that the Congressites had no arms and so they could not fight the battle in that way. Hajam Khan then said that they should please do not put them in trouble when they were not prepared to fight with arms. Ashiq Shah replied that the Britishers were not so much afraid of guns and rifles as they were terrified from the Congress movement. Before Hajam Khan could say anything Ashiq Shah declared the meeting as ended.
N.B.
It is stated that Hajam Khan had been asked by his villagers to interfer with Ashiq Shah in the meeting.
Ashiq Shah with his party then proceeded towards village Adizai police station Mattani.,

Abdur Rauf Khan of Kathozai and Arbeb Adam Khan of Kankoli, President and Secretary, respectively, of the Congress Committee, Qasim Jan of Shabgader, Haji Amanullah of Sarikh, Abdul Qadus of Khobi,Faqir Lohd Khan of Khatki,Fazal Illahi of Mian Isa and Shiekh Jabin of Sheikh Killi,wore prominent anonget haxgx those,ho were present. C. I. D. Report dated 29. 9. 1932.
On the 28th September 1932, a secret meeting of the Red Shirts was held at about 1-30 p.m. under the grape arches, behind the serai of Jaggan Nath situated outside Hashtnagri Gate. About 10 Redshirts are stated to have attended the meeting. In the meeting it was decided that Kundal and Isa Khan of Kurvi assisted by Mausam of Serai Jaggan Nath should collect Red Shirts Volunteers from the rural area for picketing in the City.
FIDENTIAL. No. 4829 342-S.B., dated Peshawar, the 29 September 1932.
Copy of above forwarded to the Senior Superintendent
of Police, Peshawar, for information.
(Mushtaq).

for Assistant to the Inspector-General of Police, C. I. Department, N.- W. F. Province.

Karim, son of Mohammad Saryar, of Mohalla Bazdaran, "A" Division, and Ghulam Sarwar, alias Bacho, son of Abdul Majid, of Mohalla Kamach Pazan, "A" Division, were arrested during the disturbance of 1930 for the alleged murder of the Despatch Rider in the City. As sufficient proof was not forth coming, they were let off and were bound over under section 110 Criminal Procedure Code by the City Magistrate, Peshawar on 14th January 1931, to furnish security for Rs.5000/- for one year. Both of them are Bad Characters on Register No.X and are hooligans of the City.

Information has been received that Shad
Mohammad of Gandi Wehra and Tila Mohammad 'lieutnent' of the Red Shirts of Gunj, have been appointed dictators of the Red Shirts of the City. Waris, Secretary of the Red Shirts of Yakatut, and Deputy, 'lieutnent' of the Red Shirts of Gunj will make a tour in the Pubbi illaqa in a day or two to make propaganda asking the people not to pay land revenue etc. Similarly Mohammad Afzal, Secretary, of the Red Shirts of Lahori Gate and Sultan 'lieutenent' of the Red Shirts of Gunj will make tour in the Charsadda illaqa for this very purpose. The Red Shirts are proposing to send a man on 16.1.1932
by 5 p.m. train to Lahore, Amritsar, Delhi xx etc., to make propaganda there.

27 265
I made extensive enquiries in Nimak Mandi today
in connection with the lathi charge carried out on 13/1/32.
The result of which is as under:-
On the evening of 12/1/32 the City Red Shirts distributed hand bills in the surrounding villages inviting the villagers to the city for the purposes of demonstration saying that some leaders had come over to Peshawar from down country to enquire in to the repressive measures adopted by the Government. The villagers were led to believe that they would not be interfered with by the MobGovernment while the leaders were in the city. They were A also made to understand verbally that Abdul Ghaffar Khan had released. The villagers began to pour into the city in do baches and collected in Nimak Mandi. They were accompanied by about 20 women. They carried their red clothes in 00 bundles over their backs and one of them had also a Putflagstaff. the incident Wr-Pugh
A flag was hoisted in the centre of Nimak Mandi. The village women were joined by the females of Cartman and they took their seats on the roof of the Serai of Dhero Mal.
for
On the receipt of information, the Local Police
and additional Police arrived on the scene and did their best in persuading the mob numbering about 600/700 to disperse but they refused to move and insisted on remaining there. There after the mob was dispersed by lathi charge., simultaneously the women sitting on the top of Dhero's Serai began to throw brickbats on the police, resulting in injuries to one constable and minor injuries to several others. A party of police got up the roof of the Serai, some entering it by the front and some by the rear side. They began to strike the women who flew being panic stricken.Some of them fell in the garden of L. Sarab Dial on the back of the Serai from a height of about 9 feet. There are numerous wild rumours afloat in that vicinity regarding this lathi charge. It is alleged by some that the women were hurled down to the garden but no eye witness
C
Nas

c_red_shirts_1_pesh_city_1930_pdf_p027.text:
-229267
could be found to confirm it. I was told that one woman of Spinawari had died in consequence of the lathi charge. A special man was sent to Spinawari to verify this. He visited Abdara, Swati, Nauthe, Pushti Khara, Spinawari Charkhakhel and Sarbandand and came to the conclusion that no woman had died as a result of the lathi charge. It transpired that the mother of one Sher Ali of Charkhakhel was wounded on the arm and head. Further more a woman of Spinawari who owns no relation and hails from Afghanistan whose legs and one arms are broken was injured in the lathi charge. Ashrafai wife of Mohd Shah cartman of Serai Mufti Latif and Manai wife of Ayub cartman of Kucha Shah Kabul Pir and Gul Saida wife of Aman Khan a sawman received minor injuries and they are doing their own business. Talla Mana wife of Marselin alias Marsal a red shirt of the same street also received injury on the head and is stated to be confined in the bed. During the course of the incident Mr: Pugh was grappled with by a red shirt and received a scretch on his cheek. Tila Mohd a lieutenant of the red shirts of Ganj who holds a contract for the Tonga stand situated out side the GanjGate along with his servant Sultana are responsible for arranging all this demonstration by distributing hand bills to the villagers. The feelings of the public in connection with the molestation of the ladies were very high at the time being but have sub-sided now.
r
was
The rif raffs of the city also took very active part in throwing the brickbats when it was once commenced. by the women folk.
Sd, Dhani Ram. 17.1.32.
Another version which I obtained from a
literate man shows that when the Police went up to the roof to clear the place, the injured Constable who was bleeding also went up. The crowd of women on the roof was given a lathi charge, the injured F.C. taking the lead and a prominent part. Some women got pushed down the roof an fell about 9 feet. Amongst those injured the following more are mentioned:
