Special Dairy,
D/- 21-1-32.
It is strongly rumoured in the city that a fine of several lakhs of rupees has been imposed by the authori- ties on the residents of the City and that measures to realise it by force are about to be taken. It is believ- ed that some Hindu residents of the city have already left for down country fearing reprisals at the hands of the authorities. It is also believed that people, as a precaution against the above measures, are hiding away their valuables, cash, etc.
2. In a secret meeting of the Red-shirts held in a serai outside the Hashtagari gate on 19-132, one Shah Nazar, a "Gariban" of shadi Pir mohalla, Peshawar City, was elected President of the "Garibans (Red-shirts) in
place of Jhirin who is in jail.
3. An Urdu cyclostyled Loster headed Farangi and dated 18-1-32, was found placarded at various places i the City on 20-1-32. The poster describes the alleged high-handed actions perpetrated by the Police and the Military on the residents of Fakha village on pretext of realising land revenue, etc. It also says that the deputation of the Jamiat-ul-Ulema which was to visit this Province to enquire into the grievances of the people has been refused permission to enter N...F.. 4. Copies of an Urdu poster (written in red pencil) were found placarded at various places in the city this morning. The poster is to the effect that unless the Chief Commissioner and other responsible officers ab- stain from further high-handed és actions such as loot ing villages, they will be killed off one by one. The poster is signed at the bottom with two crossed dagger with the words "Young Society' written round them.

Peshawar Day". It is hoped that similar" Hartals" will be observed in other cities.
Pahlwan Ayub, Mohd Yaqub alias Yaqubi and Imo old Congress volunteers and ex convicts in the movement of 1930 were noticed( beside others) exhorting the public last evening to observe Hartal this morning.

Mir Aslam, s/o Anar Khan of Gulab Khana,
returned from Utmanzai on 3rd February with a message that 4 men from every red shirt centre of the City should start picketing foreign cloth shops. They should wear black clothes or, at least,a black band on one of their arms. Accordingly, on 4.2.32, at about mid-day, 4 men namely Habibullah, President; Said Wali, Picket Commander; Isa and Landai "Captains", Nimak Mandi Centre, arrived in the Lara Bazazan. They had black bands on their arms. They had just started picketing and shouting 'Inqilab Zinda Bad' when a message reached them from Chirvako ban that a Secretar of the red shirts has arrived from Utmanzai asking them not to start picketing and not wear black clothe It was decided that, after holding a secret conference, the red shirts of the urban and rural areas should in y the early morning of Id day appear before the shops of foreign cloth dealers and, while the shops are stil closed, start picketing, ignoring 'lathi charges'.

Enquiries made go to show that the woman referred to in the report is one Mst: Shahzad Gai, widow of Sohbat Khan of Spinawari, who with 3 other women of her village had come to City on the day of occurrence to attend the meeting arranged by the redshirts when she received injury. This woman does not now admit to have visited the City on that day to attend the meeting but had come to make some
purchases. She states that in confusion when everybody ran while the crowd was being dispersed, she fell on the ground and was trampled down and her leg was broken. She does not want to be examined by a doctor ans still confined to her bed at her house. (It cannot be positively stated if her leg is really broken or not). Of course Mst: Shahzad Gai and her villagers persist in their allega- tion that her leg is broken.
The reason why she was alleged by her co-villagers to be the resident of Afghanistan is obvious i.e. that they were afraid of the imposition of a fine on their village as a punishment for defying the law.
Further more this woman does not like to be examined by a doctor as she is evidently affraid of being prosecuted for attending the unlawful meeting.
Sd/- Dhanni Ram.

Inquiries have been made by the C.1.D. in this
case and the statements of police officers concerned have been taken. The facts appear to be that on the
12th January a large crowd collected in Nimak Mandi to hold a meeting. The crowd consisted partly of people from the Peshawar City and partly of villagers from outside. When the Police arrived, the crowd at the meeting consisted of about 4 or 500 people who were very excited and shouting "Inqilab Zinda Bad". A number of women had come with their relations to watch the meeting and there were about 40 or 50 women in Burgas on the roof of the shops immediately a djoining the place where the meeting was being held. The meeting itself was an unlawful assembly and was dis- persed by the police. While the meeting was being dispersed, the women on the roof commenced throwing bricks at the police, in the course of which several of the police, including Mr. Pugh, A.S. Police, were injured. The crowd on the roof consisted of both men and women, and as the crowd was throwing bricks Mr.Pugh sent a small party on to the roof to clear the crowd there. This small police party in spite of the bricks thrown at them got on to the roof and commenced to disperse the crowd with their lathis. The crowd rushed away and as there were only two ladders, numbers of them jumped from the roof to the ground, and in the rush several were apparently trodden on and injured. injuries at the time И as on the other side of the shops away from the Serai, and when I personally visited the Serai about an hour later, there was no sign of any injured person. Sub- sequently, there were rumours in the City that somen women had been killed, and I ordered inquiries. So fur as can be ascertained, the following women

Said Haider Shah served 3 months sentence u/s
341/114 I.P.C. and was due for release on 20.1.1932. On that date D.S.P. Qasim Ali Shah of the C.I.D. phoned up "E" Division and asked for particular of the part played by Said Haider Shah in the Red Shirts movement, whether he was a dangerous Red Shirt etc. He was told that he was a man likely to cause trouble if released.

Sir,
The petitioner submits as follows :-
1. That I submitted an application to your honour on 13.2.32 for the release on Maufi of my brother Sayed Haider Shah, son of Sayed Qalandar Shah, of Bhana Mari, which was sent to Captain Abdul Rahim Khan City Magistrate Peshawar.
2. That before conviction of my brother, he had resigned his "Sadarat" soon after his becoming aware of the fact that Congress has no connection with Khilafat and therefore he removed his congress office, full detail of this fact is well known to Sayed Hakim Shah, son of Sayed Haider Shah of Mohalla Sheikh Bahauddin Peshawar City.
That my brother Sayed Haider Shah, son of Sayed Qalander Shah of Bhana Mari was sentenced to 3 months imprisonment under section on 21st October 1931,
His date of release was 20-1-32, but he is still under- going imprisonment.
Honoured Sir. I respectfully beg to st-ate that inspite of expirey of his imprisonment Sayed Haider Sha has not been released. If there is anything against him which is the cause of restriction. I am quite prepared to satisfy your hencur in every way. It is probable that some of his enemy caused him further imprisonment.
It is therefore humbly request that under the circumstances my brother Sayed Haider Shah who is beggin for pardon, be released.

I have the honour to state that the following three Red Shirts of Bhanamari, Peshawar, were sentenced to 3 months rigorous imprisonment under Sections 341/143/114 Indian Penal Code for holding up a Police lorry in Bhanamari :-
1. Faizulla s/o Pir Mohammad,
2. Juma Khan s/o Yar Mohamad, 3. Haider Shah s/c Kalandar Shah.
Nos. 1 & 2 were Red Shirt 'Captains' and No. 3 was the 'Sadar' of the Bhanamari Jirga.

On release from jail they were detained in the jail under orders of the Deputy Commissioner, Peshawar, copy of order attached. The period of detention expires on e. 19th March. If they are detained further they will have 3 to be sent to the Haripur Detention Camp. The best plan will be to serve them with an order under Section 4(d) Emergency Powers Ordinance and restrict them to Bhanamari and also to abstain from political activities.

Rashid, son of Abdul Hakim, of Mohalla Gandi Wehra, Yakatoot who was a prominent worker with Shad of Gandi Wehra and made himself scarce when the latter was arrested remained absent from Peshawar City for about a month and a half, has re-appeared in Peshawar City about a week ago. He appears to believe that no further action will be taken now and has secretly started work on the old lines by quietly meeting sympathisers and co-workers. He has been talking of picketing and continuing the agitation till their leaders especially Abdul Ghaffar Khan and Gandhi were released. He tried to hold a small secret meeting in his Mohalla on the night between 28th and 29th March but the womenfolk protested on account of the misery and suffering already caused by such
activities.

will join the Juma Prayers in mosques in Peshawar City, on Friday the 1st April. After prayers they will entreat the public not to participate in voting as the persons staniing for election do no represent the nation, and that their true representatives are in jails.

They should be reserved for Propaganda Work only, as they are considered to be very trustworthy. By being arrested none would be left for Propaganda work.
Said Akbar, Asghar, Amirai and Ghulam Ashaq are stated to have left Akbarpura with the intention of holding a big meeting of the Red-shirts somewhere in the Pabbi Illaqua.

from his village. He would carry on propaganda in the

On 19.8.32 the following persons assembled in the 'Ziarat' of Pir Abdulla Shah situated in the limits of
Shahi Bagh on the City Railway Road Side between 11.30 & 12 noon & held a secret meeting.
1. Said Alam Khan, President of Hed Shirts, Badaber;
2. Akram Khan, "General" of Red Shirts, Takhtabad;
3. Mansur Khan Red Shirt of Daudzei.
4. Amin, "General" of Red Shirtal Spearkh;
5. Musa Khan, Naib President, of Kachhi Mohalla;
6. Mohd Sadiq of Mirpura;
7. Akhtar Mohd, "Captain" of Red Shirts Yakatut;
8. Khan Mohd, ex-"General" of Red Shirts and two others. In the meeting it was decided that the hand written posters should be distributed in the City on the 20th and 21st August 1932, Consequently 4 men from the Badaber illaqa will come to the city on the 20th and 21st August with the object of distributing the posters.
The Posters is an appeal for the Frontier People urging them to boycott the foreign cloth & to use the country made cloth. It has been stated in the poster that a large number of our young men are undergoing all sorts of troubles in the Jails for the sake of their country.
Inspector City and Sub-Inspector Badaber have been informed on the phone regarding the distribution of the posters in the City.

Special C.I.D. report regarding political arrests in
Peshewar on 26. 8. 1932.
In view of attempts at revival of activities noticed during the last few days in Peshawar City the following arrests are being mede under the Special Powers Ordinance, Section 3, in consultation with the Deputy Commissioner and the Senior Superintendent of Police.
1. Mehta KIRPA RAM, aged 45 years. He is under trial (on bai
Arprins!
in a case under Section 17 (1) C.L.A.A. relating to the despatch of a report by Abdul Malik, red shirt president, to Lahore. It was intercepted in the dak. Recommendation was made for action against this man under the Special Powers Ordinance on 29th June, before the Lavabove case arose, but he was not taken into custody under the S.P.O. because he was am'ested in the above case. Now that he is on bail in the reported case, it is necessary to place him in custody under the S.P.C.
2.Mian Mohd late driver of Dr.Khan Sahib, aged 22 years,
3. Imo alias Mohd Shafiq, aged 22 years.
4. Abdul Aziz Halwai, aged 26 years.
He has been reported from time to time as working for the red shirts as messenger etc. He was suspected of dissemination of posters too. Now his name has been found on a list of persons receiving aid from Congress. He is on ball on a point of identity. Judicial decision of this point may be made later in Court, but as he is receiving aid from the Congress and working for them. his arrest is in the public interest.
He has been an active worker for the Congress leaders in the City, Now his name too has been found on a list of persons receiving aid from the Congress. He is an bail in a case which is under trial but it is necessary to detain him in custody under S.P.0.
He is a member of the Naujawan Bharat Sabha and is also mixed up with the Congress workers. He is a friend and helper of Kirpa

C.I.D. report, dated the 27.8.32.
159 137
On 26.8.32 at about 1 p.m. the following Red Shirts held a
secret meeting in the mosque 'Idgah'sAT
Rustam; Sadar; of Surozai Payan.
2.Dilbar Sadarjof Surozai Bala.
3. Halim Shah, Secretary of Badaber (absconder)
4. Masur Khannof Dagai (appointed in place of Said Alam)
5. Amin Jan of Kochian, General Secretary.
6. Alaf Jan of Shah Alam Babuzai (absconder)
7. Hazrat Gul of Badaber (absconder).
8. and more.
In the meeting it was decided that the Red Shirts should encounter arrest by going to the city with a view to distributing posters.
Consequently the old posters entitled "An Appeal to the Frontier People to boycott foreign cloth' which have already appeared in the district will be distributed in the city with effect from the 27th August.
In accordance with the arrangement No.3 and 6 stayed the last night in Mian Gujar to arrange for the volunteers from Mian Gujar Takhtabad, Kochian and Dagai to distribute these posters in the city. In other words Halim Shah and Alaf Jan would pass the night of 27/28 at Takhtabad, 28/29 at Kochian and 29/30 at Dagab respectively to arrange for volunteers for the referred purpose. 32 The informer says that Badshah Gul of Kangra brought these posters from Utmanzai and handedover them to Amin Jan of Kochian and the latter delivered them to Alaf Jan for distribution. Alaf Jan in company with Halim Shah is arranging for the volunteers ti distribute these posters in the city between the 27th and 30th August 132. These posters are reported to be about 400 in number. Note. Inspector City and .Is Daudzai and Mathra have been warned to be on their alert.

URGENT
Information has just been received that 14 men from village Parang (P.S. Charsadda) will artive in the City by this evening. If they come up, half of them would go after dark to the place of the " Martyrs of 1930" in Kissa Khani Bazar,near Dhaki Nal Bandi and there do some mischief. Next morning the other half will, get into the 12.40 down train either at Cantonments or at the City Railway Station. They would stop the train by pulling up the alarm chain somewhere enroute to Attock and distribute anti-Government posters.

c_red_shirts_2_pesh_city_1930_p010.text:
and their families. He cursed such a disgraceful service and those who had bombarded the holy places like Mecca and Medina for paltry sums of money. He said that the Tahsils and the Thanas were gambling dens and the Jirgah members and Hon'y Magistrates were sucking the blood of the nation by taking bribes. (Shouts of toady bachcha Hai Hai). He concluded by advocating the use of Khaddar. Maulvi Shakir-Ullah proposed resolutions of hatred against the Sarda Act and the firing on 23-4-30, and of the boycott of foreign cloth and use of Khaddar.
L Abdul Karim Khan, son of Khair Muhammad Khan, Lambarda: of Jati Payan rose to announce his resignation from his lambardarship. He was congratulated on his resignation. A member of the Congress Committee then rose, and giving an account of the situation in the city, remarked that the authorities were practicing great tyrannies there. A house in Dhakk-1 Dalgaran was put on fire and when people collected there, the city was blockaded. - Four perons were wounded and many members received slight injuries. His "Concience" said that the house was put on fire by the Government, after which their leaders in- cluding, Pir Bakhsh, pleader, were put under arrest. The language used by the speaker was inflammatory.
Khadim Muhammad Akbar Khan again rose and said that they (the Khudai Khidmatgars) were against violence and dis not wish to fight with the Government. Their object was to boycott its "shops" after which it will remove itself away
100/from their country. He further remarked that the tyrant Government had arrested their leaders as they proached people to be good and to give up gambling, adultry, dacoity, theft, use of intoxicants, etc. These t. things, however, were a source of income to the Governme Abdul Karim Khan, Lumbardar, Abdul Ahad Khan and - 1 Muhammad Akram Khan of Tukht abad and their relatives took active part in the organistaion of this meeting.

c_red_shirts_2_pesh_city_1930_p011.text:
(3)
✓ Muhammad Ali Lambaradar of Nahaqqi, who was offered - presidentship of the above meeting, did not attend.
Muhammad Umar Khan of Shahalam, Khalil-ur-Rahman of Khatki and the Khans of Babozai did not allow their volun- teers to attend the meeting.
Rahmat-Ullah Khan of Jati Bala, who is a friend of the
Police, also kept himself aloof.
About 800 of the volunteers who attended were from the Batgram and Charsadda ilaqas. About 400 from Mathra, etc.
were
In a meeting held in Mussozai on 1-5-30 the City people were blamed of aggression against the Government.
It was also resolved in this meeting to request Govern- ment not to exempt them from paying revenue for the land which they did not cultivate. If any revenue is asked for suc? lans they should refuse to pay it.
The meeting was attended by about 100 persons from
Jurozai, Mussozai, Bahadur Killi, etc.
On 3-5-30, a meeting was held at Shahidan, Ahmad Khel Jhangi, Sadar Sub: Division. The audience numbered about 100. The promonent persons present were Ajab Khan, Izzat Khan, ah Muhammad Afzal Khan and Nur Muhammad, Lambardars of Ahmad Khel and Khalid Khan, lambardar of Kaggawala, casim, lambardar of Landi Yarghajo, Akbar Khan, Karim-Ullah, Farid Khan and Said ✓ Chulam, lambardars of Hazarkhani. Aslam Khan of Idgah, Akram Khan of Kaggawala, Ayub Khan of Bahadur Killi, Ashiq Shah of Bahadur Killi, Sanobar of Kaggawala and Zahid Shah of Garhi
Saidan delivered speeches protesting against the distribution"
レ
of the Bara water to the Cantonment etc. It was resolved not
A
to,pay revenue of the lands which were not cultivated./
Zesolu
tion of disbedience of the Sarda Act was also passed. It was ᄉ
also proposed that dead bodies of the persone murdered shoul/ic
not be sent to the mortuary, but the doctor should be phoned

c_red_shirts_2_pesh_city_1930_p012.text:
to come and inspect the corpse on the spot. Women doctors should be appointed to examine the corpses of the females.
None of the City people or the Hindus and Sikhs attended this meeting.
The chief organisers were Akram Khan, Ayub Khan and Ashiq Shah of Bahadur K1111, Sanobar of Kaggawala and Aslam Khan o Idgah.
On 2-5-30 Muhammad Amin and Yakub, Imam of the mosque of Mian Umar Sahib, Chamkani, got it proclaimed by beat of drum in the village that a Hindustani Maulvi would deliver a sermo in the said mosque after the Khuftan prayers. This attracted about 150 persons to the mosque, but many of them, finding ⚫ that the story of the arrival of a Hindustani Maulvi was in- vented to attarat atfarct audience, returned to their homes.
Muhammad Amin addressed the public with an account of the firing in the City on 23-4-30, and said that the Govern- ment had shot down a large number of innocent people. It was proud of its machine guns and aeroplanes. The only way to answer the tyrannies of the Government was to pray to Heavens for its destructions, to boycott British made cloth, and to wear Khaddar. The public was also advised to get their cases decided by Punchayats.
Master Muhammad Ayaz said that a "Hartal" had been ob- served throughout the country to sympathise with the "Martyrs It was their weakness that the British had come to rule over them from 8000 miles. He exhorted them to prepare themselves to oppose the Government
a
A resolution of hatred against Khan Bahadur Sarfaraz Khan of Chamkani, who was Government man, was also passed.
ELLIG
7- MAY 1930

Special Diary, D/-8-8-30.
Hajs
The following reports have been receved from Sadar Sub: Division, Peshawar:-
1. No meeting in contravention of the Seditious meet- ings Act has been held in the jurisdiction of Daud- zai police station.
2. Muhammad Amin, Lanbardar, and Sher Zaman of Pushto Sag, Mahbub, son of Abdul Wahab and Cabot Chulam Sarwar of Khazana, Ghulam Muhammad, son of Muhammad All of Shahalam, Qudrat Ullah, son of Hidayat Ullah of Jati Bala have been enlisted as Khudai Khidmat- gars.
3. The Commandant of the Khudai Khidmatgars of Nahaqqi has received a letter from Rab Nawaz Khan of Utaan- zai informing the latter that no volunteer would, in future, join any meeting which is held outside their ilaqas, without special permission of the Committee. They can take part in the meetings held in their own 1laqas with the permission of the village president. All their visits to the meetings would be reported - to Utmanzai. They have further been instructed not to recognise any orders other than those received from and scaled by the authorities at Utmanzai.
4. The police has succeeded in inducing Muhammed Ali Khan, President of the Khudai khidmatgars of Mahaqqi, to sever his connection with the Khudai Khidmatgars. 5. Non of the villagers of the Mathra p.s. has enlisted as Khudai Khidmatgar.
6. It is rumoured that the Haji and his followers are 66 staying at Chalo Ghund at a distance of about 2 miles from British territory. Gul Amin and Ismail who were sent to him are also detained there. His son, Badshah Gul is collecting lashkars.
7. It is also rumoured that Mahmud Akhunzada has written Nawab Muhammad Zaman Khan saying that as the Shias have offered themselves their services to Goverment without his co approval, the Shia-Sunni agreement recently arrived at should be considered as cancelled. Rumours are afloat in the Sadar Sub: Division that the soldiers of the Kurrum Militia are ill-treating the Sunnis of Peshawar City.

The following reports have been received from the Sadar
Sub: Division, Peshawar:-
1. Mispah-ud-Din, son of Mir Aftab, Ajab, Ahmad Jan and Abdul Karim of Daudzai, went to Shabdar in the tonga of one Ahmad Jan. There object is said to have been to see Badshah Gul, son of the Haji of Turangaai. They are all volunteers. They returned on 9-5-30.
2. Abdul Haq, Muhammad Akram and Abdul Karim of Tukht- abad, whose arms licenses were cancelled, deposited their arms in the Daudzai p.s. They came to the - police station along with 40 volunteers clad in red. A relative of Abdul Karim who is a clerk in the D.C's office had informed him that his license had been cancelled, before the police were informed.

It is now reported that Babu Zakariya of Chuha Gujar, who was shown in D.8.P., Sadr: Sub: Division's diary dated 13-5-30, to have been taking part in anti- Government activities, came to the D.S.P., 3adr on 14-5-30 and stated that the complaints made against him were due to the intrigues of K.B. Sarfaraz Khan of Chamkani, and that he had no intention of taking part in any seditious movement.

MACAdential.
Copy of B.8.Polles, adr: Sub; Division's 6 dated 26-5-30.
Today I visited Dalazak, Gulusai, Muhammadzai Budni, Sabi and Yasinnbad. I held a jirgah with the headmen and volunteers and made them understand that present movement is a political and not a religious I gave them the instance of Budho, & lindu of Charsets she is a Captain of the volunteers. I told them that the movement been a religious one the Muslims would v have agreed to the appointment of a Hindu Captain. Thy admitted my argument to be reasonable and promised to have no connection with the movement in future.
Misbah-ud-Din, son of Mir Aftab, Aurang, son of Hawas, of Nahaqqi, and 10 other volunteers of the same village went to Utmanzai to join a meeting. Fifty volunt eers headed by Sadat, Qudrat-Ullah, Hastam of Jati Bala and Tukhtabad also went to attend the meeting held at Charsadda in connection with the arrest of Khadim Mund: Akbar of Utmanzai.
3. A meeting will be held at Takhtabad on 28-5-30 in connection with the rrest of Abdul Ahad Khan. Posters were distributed to announce the meeting.
6. Maulvi Abdul Rauf of Gulosai is absent from his house. It is said that he has gone either to Rustum, or to Akora to see the Haji Sahih of Akora. The Police con- cerned has been asked to locate his whereabouts.
Sd: Zahir Gul Khan 1.8.P., Jadr.

Gopy of confidentiel diery debet 2.5.30, of the addr
Bub Division Teater.
Today meeting was held at Talchtsbed Al. ladtan Khan son of June Khan was in the chair. The congregation amounted about 2,000 men the meeting was opened with recitation from the Holy Quran, and a Pushtu poem by Mohammed Akbar of Char- andde who in yet in his teens. The Mohd Yusef of Ambs thor, P.8.Batgram with the permission of Abdula Mian of Mazars -- stood and took with him thres tons of Abdul Ahad to the stags. He told the people that due to the Government these three children are today orphans.Abdul Ahed Khan for serving commi nity is in Swaraj Handar for a year. He is not dead but his arrest harmade our success a bit nearer. Afterwards he add- rossed the audience in the following terms.
When our Khane and Khan Sahiba go to see an English officer they usually pay Rs. 1/- or 2/- to the orderly, so that he may beg the permied on ofsahib on their behalf to see him; but the reply comes to it because Sahib at that time happens to play with his dog. When he gets tired and gets fre from his dog, then an interview is granted. Khan Sahibs and Khan Bahadure instead of telling him about the troubles of public begin to pray his dog in order to humour him. The Khans are worse than those dogs which the English officers love so mich, because dogs do not eat one another's flesh while there Khens prey upon us.
for
God hasДbidden the flesh of that bird which thresh upon the flesh ofits fellow birds, so the Khans and Khan Sahibe who prey upon us should be totally boycotted.
Abdul Rauf student ofthe 8th. class of Utmanzai school lecured next. He exorted the people to be brave and he said the the dothes of the volunteers were red with the blood of the hation and the Government has added to our vos by arrest- ing our leaders and making us orphans. The Government should know that no power on earth on prevent us from our movement. He 61860scias called upon the Khans to participate in the

c_red_shirts_2_pesh_city_1930_p021.text:
33201
the movement. lie said that today he went to a person who is present in the meeting. I told him to join the movement, but he declined. The reason which he gave was that the people of his illaqua ere weak and if he should join the movement, than it would be better for him to migrate himself from this place to Charsadda. The lecturer said, it was a mere pretext in order to avoid them. If the men whom I am referring ea08e80 conde- sends to join us in the movement, we are sure that a large number ofvolunteers will join the movement from this illaqua. The man to whom the lecturer was referring is Mohd Ali Khan Headman of Nahaki.
Mian Abdulla of Mazara said that red clothes were an act of religious duty and quoted page 228 of Shani Kitab, with respect to the truth ofhisassortion. He red a message from Abdul Ghaffar Khan declaring that he had show sympathy for the helpers ofthe movement, specially to Utmanzai Durgai, Kohat, - Peshwar, Banmu etc., Moreover he said that Abdul Gaffar Khan earnestly sympathises with the persons of the above mentioned villages andtoms, who suffered.
Ghulam Muhyuddin, a drumer ofzarinabad who is a captain of the "REDS" read a poem in Pushtu. The purport of which was that the tyrant Government put us in Jail simply to prevent us from legitimus aspirations. The son of Mohammad Albar Khadim rose and said that the Government has arrested his father and other leaders simply to prevent us from being united. They -- like us to live like barbarians, killing and looting one another The Reds in the Jalsa mbered about 1200 of whom 200 were of Daudzas and the rest ware ofCharsadda and Batgran. The leading members of the Red were of P.S.Daudzas and are the following persons.
Mohamad Amin son of Sher Dál of Pushti Sag, Ahmad Jan who has recently applogised. Hisbahuddin son of Hir Aftab of Nahaki Kaohkaul and Gul Zada of Nowra, Mohd Akram and Hastan of Takhtabad, Qudrat Ulla son of Hidayat Ulla, Saidat son of
r

c_red_shirts_2_pesh_city_1930_p022.text:
35 202
of Koohien. A few volunteers of Chagri Mati and Alizai 2.5. thra had als come to the meeting. Fifteen tonts were pitched there. These were brought by Mohammad Akram of Takhtabad from Peshawar. The flags in the meeting numbered 17. The volunteers coming from lashtnaggar approached the Zemindars working in -- their fields and persuaded them to join the meeting.Their weapon of persuasion is that they declared the movement a religious one in- They made demonstrations in the neighbourhood, of K.B.Mohamad Akram
e Than's Hujra and showered vollies of abuses on him but his Hujra was quite empty at that time. The volunteers are re sorting to a measure which isuite distressing to the people who eams for the present stand aloof from the movement. In spite of the amoyance caused by the volunteers no one complaints because they know the general predudice ofthe people who wor- ship the movement as a religious one.

Today I went to Babuzat. 3.1. del Khan of P.S. Daudzei
was with me. The office bearers of he volunteers of Takhatabads
Jeti Bala and Payan and Lombarders were summoned. We had a talk with them. K.S. Abdul Albar in Mohd Amig Then of babuzai and Rahmat Ullah Khan of Jati hal pad e in our aim and wa succeeded to persuade the office benrere to stop the movement. They gave us the following written undertaking.
As
Translation of undertaking of Tekhatabad people.
the movement was going all round, so we also held the meetings at Takhatabada. We joined the movement with the idea that in the case if reforms are given, we should not bo left of. We have no idea to rebellainst the Govt. nor we try to spread hatred among the people against the Govt. We want to let you know that at least we are desirous to be given equal rights with the rest of India. We for the present give up the movement for no fear of army but we desire that good understandings should prevail between us and the Govt.
If any mischief-monger shall try to renew the agitation in Jati Bala, Payan and Taithetabada Awal, we undertake to inform the officials of it, and by so doing we will fulfil our plodge. We give this in writing and we will stick to it undertaking is signed by the following parsons.
The

Lohammadi who was killed in the fight between Afridis and the Government found an ammunition box. The box was snatched
from him by ayun, Ahmad Gul and Duran of Suleman Thel, but 1ster on the box was handed over to Said Ahmad son of Yunis of Suleman Khel. Babu Zakaria of Chuha Gujar, a Sub-Assitant Surgeon, who was dismissed during the Great War for disloyalty, is reported to be taking an active part in the present agitation.
From police station Badaber comes the report that a tribal territory Mohmand has been telling the villagers that a "War" had commenced at Shabkadr and that they should take part in it. The man absconded before he could be apprehended.
The Mathra police report that Feroze Khan, brother of Musa Khan, headman of village Panam Dheri, and five others were seen going towards Shabkadr. Feroze Khan, who appeared to be excited, said he would go with his sepoys to Shabkadr.
Feroze Khan was required to attend the Sessions Court on 16 May. H. aigned the summons but refused to sign the bond.

A meeting was held at Takhatabad-Awwal, Police Station Daudani on the 4th instant. The audience were estimated to be about 3000 in number including about 1000 "Khudal Khidmatgers and 30 Hindus and Sikhs who had come from the surrounding villages.
The following were prominent in the meeting:-
1. Mohd Akbar, Khadim of the Azad School Uttmanzai. 2. Maulvi Shakarrullah of Nowshera Kallan.
3. Maulvi Abdul Wahid of Meerzai, P.S. Batagram. 4. Kari Allah of Akbarpura.

That "Jirgas" should be established in all villages in order to settle disputes between the parties and these Jirgas will obviate the need for Police Stations. These Jirgas should work under the direction of the Uttmanzai party in order to meet the desired object of Abdul Ghaffar Khan.

He would be known by his white face and black heart (Liberally his sign was that he had a white face and a black heart). He had made them helpless and had taken away every thing from them.

He exhorted the audience to non-cooperate with the Government servants and its supporters, 1.6. Khan Bahadurs, Honorary Magistrates and Jagirdars, etc. A British Soldier was paid Rs.80/- p.m. besides free ration and clothings, but the Indian Soldier was paid only Rs.10/-, which was quite insufficient to meet his expenses and require- ments. That was the reason that Indian soldiers were compelled to accept bribes and to suck the blood of their brethren. He cursed those who were still serving under these conditions. He regretted that Muqaddas although the Muslims fired upon "Baitul ages (Jerusalem), the Government had no regard for their services. He thought that the Tehsil and Thanas of Charsadda were merely gambling dens because he knew that about Rs.500/- were daily taken as bribes by the Police and Civil employees from their brethren. The Honrary Magistrates served honorarily merely to take bribes from the public, because they receive no regula light pay. Curses be upon them, as they suck the blood of their own brethren. Upon this shouts of "Toady Bacha Hai Hai" were raised. If the Government had any reger for them it would have not allowed brothels and liquor shops. The Liquor shops were a source of income to the Government hence it had opened these shops. He advocated the use of Khaddar.

One Muslim (probably Yusaf Ali) member of the Peshawar Congress Committee asked the people to hear about the oppressions perpetrated by the Government on the public at Peshawar to-day (4th May 1930). He said that Fire had broken out at Dhaki Dalgaran which he considered to be the work of some Government men. The City was being guarded by the British Soldiers. 4 members of the Bharat Naujawan Sabha had severely been wounded and 27 had received simple hurts at the hands of British Soldiers. Abdul Rab and Pir Bakhsh, Vakils and Acharj Ram had been arrested and nothing was known as regard the others.
Upon this Mohamad Akbar of Uttmanzai said that as their creed was that of Non-violence, they would not oppose the Government. Their success lay in the policy of non-cooperation and Non-violence.
In the end he said that this meeting was
convened by the Congress but he had delivered his lecture on the lines of the Anjuman-1-Afaghina on his own responsibility.
The meeting dispersed at about 5 p.m.

19.8.44.
From 1550 hours to 1650 hours ex after the Jumma Prayers on 18.8.4,a meeting, attended by about 300/350 Musalmans,was held under the auspices of the Afghan Jirga, in the Mhabat Khan Mosque,Peshawar City. Qabri Fida Hussain of Akbarpura, police station Pabbi,re presided.
The president of the meeting and Haji Mohd Amin of Haji Abad delivered speeches. The former said that it was due to the ir bad habits and a curse of God that the Britishers were ruling over them, and also it was due to the same that the present Ministers were occupying the Ministerial chairs. Hex Haji Mohd Amin, in the course of his religious speech said that if he had powersx he would have kept the females of Hindus and the Britishers behind the 'pardah'. He further warned the Government that it would be greatly harmful if the Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan of Tehkal Bala was arrested. He further said that the Government had done- nothing so far in connection with their demand to forbid the Non- Muslim shopkeepers to deal in the religious books of the Musalmans but if still no action was taken the members of the Afghan Jirga would picket such shops and would bear every hardships in doing so. Moreover the re-establishment of the brothel in Peshawar City would never be tolerated. Ix
The president of the meeting then moved three resolutions demanding from the Government to make arrangements for Haj, to EXEMPLXXt exempt or reduce the land revenue of the people of Akbarpura and other villages in that vicinity, thexaxaps where the crops had been damaged by the flood,and also that one Sarwar, poet, who had dishonoured Haji Mohd Amin,may come to his senses. In the end Prayers were also offered for the successful result of the interview between Mr.Gandhi and Mr.Jinnah.

ARCIAN TIRGA.
C.I.D. REPORT.
Arbab Abdul Ghafur. President of the Afghan Jirra. WEP, has, according to a reliable source, supplied the following information to the Editor of the "Daily Shahbaz" Lahore:-
"In 1939, Government had kindly passed the "Quran Sharif Act" that no non-Muslim should sell the holy books, himself, or through any agent. Time of one year was given to those non-Muslims who had stock of such books in order to clear their shons. On 20th November, 1942, when "Mujahid Sarhad" Haji Mohd Amin Sahib came to this province after ten years, with his approval, it was decided that besides the holy books other religious Kooks should also be stopped to be sold by non-Muslims. So the accompanied by the present Premier (S. Aurangzeb Khan) waited on the Deputy Commissioner to approach His Excellency.
Now the Muslim
Ministry has been formed and during such a long time the Premier has been so many times requested to decide the matter, but unfortunately, the Premier (who is a Muslim only to show) has not touched the question even. His all promises have proved false. At last on 13th October the Afghan Jirga has started non-violent picketing on the shops after consultation with the people of T.T.
They again ask the Muslim Ministry (though it is worse than a Hindu organisation) to help them in respecting their religious books, otherwise they would be responsible for any trouble created in this religious matter. ( Dy.No.299/NGC).

AFGHAN JIRGA.
From 1245 hours to 1530 hours a meeting under the suspices of the Afghan Jirge, attended by bout 400 Muselmans, wes held in the Me he bet Khan'e mosque, under the presidentship of Mian Abdullah Shah of Haxare Mahzare.
Moulans Fide Hussain of Akbarpura recited Quranic verses, while Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkel Bela, Mian Abdullah Shah of Kalzare,end Haji Mohd Amin of Heji Abed delivered speeches.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan stated how there were two aims were very dear to Haji Mohd Amin since he came down from the T.T., viz, the closure of the Peshawar City brothel,and the stoppage of the selex of Islamic religious books by the non-Muslim booksellers, how the first was accomplished successfully within a short time, and now the second was still lingering on owing to the apathy of the present ministry to which the matter had been represented for so many time. Inspite of the promise this unfortunate chief minister paid no attention to this work in the service of Islam. He further said that they had approached the non-Muslim book-dealers, but they also id no attention.
Mien Abdullah Shah of Mahzara supported Arbab Abdul Ghaffoor Khan, but added that onoa again the prostitutes had taken abodo in the old brothal, but were forcibly maad turned out, resulting in a criminal suit against Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan,and others of the Afghan Jirga who were ask subsequently acquitted by the trying Magistrate. He asked all those present to inform their co-villagers when they return to their houses that the Afghan Jirga membors had determined to stop the non-Muslim book-sellers * from selling Islamic books, and will succeed by the grace of God as they had succeeded in the removal of the prostitutes..
Haji Mohd Amin supported Mian Abdullah Shah and Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan,and said that when the Hon-Muslims had no belief in Islam how could it be hoped that they would respect the Islamic books.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan got up again,and moved a resolution, which was unanimously adopted, that this meeting of the Musa Imans demand from the nominal Islamic Ministry to stop the sale of Islamis books by the non-Muslim book-sellers, and also demand from the non-Muslim book-sellers to stop this buisnesbx in the

camscanner_15_01_2026_1410_p108.text:
Islomio books when the Muselmans were not trading in theirs. Speaking on the resolution the Arbab mentioned how one retired school-master, analy, Chiragh Din had started buisness in the sale of Guru Granth Sahib, but for a long time no Sikh purchased any copy from him,and at last he had to sell all his belongings in Amritsar to the Sikh Committee at low prices,and at thus closed his buisness. By mentioning
is fact the Arbab wanted to impress the Musalmans not to make th such purchases from the non-Muslim book-sellers,and thus compell them to stop the sale of their own. Concluding he said that now they wouli picket the shop of the book-saller,and the picketters would stop the Muselmans from making purchases.
si
After the termination of the meeting Haji Mohd Amin ied lead the procession of about 500 Muselmans from the Kahabat Khan's mosque upto the shop of Kharek Singh, book-seller in the Kissa Khani bazar, but the shop-keeper had closed his shop before their arrival.. However Faji Mohd Amin,Mian Abdullah Shah,eri Fida Hussein,Arbab Abdil Chafoor Khan, one other Kuliah sat down in front of the shop,and began to read the holy quran. After some while Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan repeated his speech which he bed ade in the Mohabat Khan's mosque, this time mentioning the Chief Minister as 'Begharat'. He also said that the shop of Abul Khalig book-seller would also be picketed if he endeavoured to sell them his books at higher prices. He was followed by & Haji Mohd Amin who said that this picketting would continue untill the non-Muslim stopped their business in the Islamic books,and would not care if they were sent to jail in doing BC by the goverment. Accordingly four volunteers of the Afghan Jirga were posted on picketting the shop under the supervision of Sunab Gul and the rest going away from this the spot. The volunteers were also gerlanded.

THE "ALJAIAT" (PESHAVAR) OF 25th OCT., 1944.
10812
吆
PICKETING OF NON-MUSLIM BOOK SHOPS
2-18
The paper has published a atatement purporting to have bee supplied to it by the Secretary, Afghan Jirga, NWP. It inter Tia mentions that at a meeting held in the Mahabat Khan Mosque on 13.X.33, under the auspices of the Central Afghan Jirga, Arbab Abdul Ghaffur Khan said that the Government had promised eyapathetically to consider their demand to prohibit non-Muslimi from doing any business in Islamic books. But since the coming into power of the present Ministry, this demand had not been considered and several deputations had seen the Chief Minister, but without any result. After a lapse of 2 years, they felt compelled to picket non-Musiin ehoos of book-sellers. Minn Abdulla Sheh, condemning the Frontier Ministry, said that it had harmed the Muslims in every way, religiously, econimically and educationally, and that it had therefore no right to exist. The following resolution was unanimously passed :-
"This grand meeting of the NIFP Muslins resolves that non-Muslim book-sellers should not trade in Islamic books. Despite protests for a long period of 2 years, the Frontier Government and particularly the so-called Islamic Ministry have paid no attention to this demand and nor have the non- Muslim book-sellers respected our religious sentiments. We have therefore been compelled to picket their shops which is conti- nuing. The non-Muslim book-sellers have closed their shops and the Khudai Khidmat gars of the Afghan Jirga, wearing red uniform are standing in front of Hindu shops. Strong resentment and hatred of the Ministry is being expressed in the city.
No.10263-64PPA 26.X.44. Copy to AIG CID, NWFP., and D.C. Peshawar

AFGHAN JIRGA.
Zein Khan Nazim Afghan Jirga, Peshawar, has it is understood, informed the sartor of the Milap' Lahore, of the following resolution which was passed at a meeting held in the Mohabat Khan mosque, Peshawar City, on 13th October, 1944:-
Pes
This gathering of the Muslims of the Frontier Provice decides that non-Muslims should not be permitted the trade of Islamic books. Since the Government has not paid any heed to our request, so we have started picketing at the shops of non-Muslims who sell Muslim books. Muslim ministry is responsible for all this trouble"

Haji Mohd Amin and Arbab Abdul Ghafur, visited K.S.Shah Nava in Utmanzai on 17th April. The former with some Maulris toured 1.S. Banda Mohib, Banda Nabi and Nowshera Kalan on 18th April, where Mohd Anin explained the Afghan Jirga and its achievements, including the removal of prostitutes from Poshawar City 400 people attended his Nowshera Kalan meeting and were requested to join Afghan Jirga proceedings in Peshawar on 23rd April.
PTO
A meeting in four sessions at intervals from 11.00 to 20.00 hours was held at Nimak Nandi, Peshawar City on 23rd. April. 40 volunteers, in Red-shirt uniform except for the distinction of star and crescent emblem on their chests, attended the meeting at which other public attendance averaged 200 per session.
The principals of the meeting with the 40 volunteers, wont in progession after prayers from Mohabat Khan Mosque to the Martyre memorial in Bazar Cissa Khani where Haji Mohd Amin throwing flowers on the memorial said that these men had given their lives for their country. He offered prayers for them and thanked God for the removal of the brothel from Peshawar City.
Various poets at the Nimak Mandi meeting including Khan Mir Hilali recited peoms to the effect that the Pathavas are a separate separate nation, stretching from Attock touetta and shoul strictly follow the Holy Quran.
poem by Mian Abdullah Shah of Mazra related that the Afgans under the Afghan Jirga flag do not want Japanese, Russian, Hindu or Bitish rule. Some of their leaders have sold themselves to Hindus and the British and pay God destroy them and others who were trying to destroy Afghans. If they went a rule of religion, he continued, they should join the Afghan Jirga and if they do not like that, then join Congress.
Mohd Ramzan, Vakil of Dera Ismail Khan, in a speech rejoiced that Pathans had gathered under one flag, Aakir Religious law was the foundation of National happiness and not hunan laws like non-violence or charkha. He maintained that Hindus and Muslims cannot unite.

camscanner_15_01_2026_1410_p134.text:
AFGHAN JIRGA:
1PS 10.19. Date 1/1/2 From morning till 1330 hours on 10.11.44, Zabte Khan alies Bazurg Mullah,and Purdil of village Shekh No.6 police station Khenmahi, picketted the Books Seller shop of Kherek Singh in the Qissa Kha ni bezer Peshawer City. They then went to the Mohabat Khan Mosque for Jumma Prayers,and on ooring be ck took up their chairs from befor the shop and went to their office in the old City Brothel. It was latter learnt that Kharak Singh had compromised matters with Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khen and that it was the end of the picketting compaign.
After the Jumma Prayers from 1500 hours to 1600 hours,a meeting of theAfghan Jirga,attended by about 250 Muselmans, was held in the Mohabat Khan Mosque,under the presidentship of Mian Abdullah Sheh of Mazara. Arbab Abdul Gha fur Khan,Mian Abdullah Shah of Mazara and Haji Mohd Amin of Haji Abad, delivered speeches. Arbab Abdul Ghafur Khan condemned the foreign education to the detriment of the religious one when the Muslim youths were catering after service and heeding not the leastxx for their religious affairs. He then mentioned the achievement of the Afghan Jirga in the removal of about 5x0x 350 prostitutes from their dens in Peshawar City. followed by its success in the stopping of the Islamic religious books by a Non-Muslim dealer,Kharak Singh. In this connection he made short references regarding their approach to the present Ministry which did nothing. He then read out the written undertaking by Kharak Singh trat in future he would not publish ncr sale Islamic religious books and that he would make a bargain with Abdur Rahim and Abdul Hakim,Books seller, in the Qisa Khan bazar. It was a very good news for the Musalmans, he added,and appealed to the congregation to convey this news to their co-villagers as well. Mian Abdullah Shah of Mazara also spoke axx in the above tune. Further he mentioned the banning of the 14th Chapter of the Satyarath Parkash by the Sind Government and said that the Government of the N.W.F.P. should also follow suit in this province. Accordingly he moved a resolution on the above lines and also congratulated the Sind Government on their action. The resolution was unanimously adopted. Haji Mohd Amin spoke in the end. He exhorted the Muselmans to unite and follow the injunctions of the Holy Quran.
T.TO.

!!! Daily Diary D 7.7. 43 (1 Haji Mohd Amin of Haji Abad arrived in Lund Khower, police station Katlang, on 5.7.43 and held Maulud Sharif'in Haji Afzal Khan's Mosque in village Shah Dhand. Some tax fifty percons participated. The Haji,efter delivering & religious speach, acquainted his listeners regarding the success achieved by the members of the Afghan Jinge in the closing down of the brothel in Peshawar City. He advised then not to be extravagant in their marriages and not to sell their daughters and sisters, because it was un-Islamic. He further said that it behoved the Gonment to forthwith prohibit the Non-Muslin Book-Sellers from dealing in the religious books of the Musalmans. Haji Mohd Amin also demanded from the Government the establishment of the department of Cazis for settling coses under the Shariat Bill and also the removal of the prostitutes and the closing down of the brothels throughout the province.

On 9.7.43 (1450 hours to 1600 hours)a meeting of the Afghan Jirga, attended by about 1500 persons,
was held after the Jun Prayers in the Mohabat Khen Mosque. Haji Axxnxufxadz Mohd Amin of
Haji ******XXX Abad raidedxxxxx presided.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkel Bala,
Qahri Fida Hussain and Haji Mohd Amin delivered speeches.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khen delivered a lengthy speech in which he stated how he had out his relations with the Congress over the marriage of the daughter of Dr.Khan Sahib with a Sikh gentleman against the principles of Islam and how he had started a-ner the old Afghan Jirge. He also referred to the meeting of the Afghan Jirge in the Mohabat Khan Mosque in which they had moved the resolutions of the removal of brothels, the establishment of the system of Qazis, the prohibition of the religious books of the Musalmans to be sold by the Non-Muslim,end to which Mohd Aurangzeb Khan, the Chief Minister, who was then ax simply a pleader, had extended all his assistance. He further said that Aurangzeb Khan was now the Chief Minister of the Province and he hoped that the above mentioned demands would soon be granted. The fourth demand was to organise the Wakf property of the Mohabat Khan. Mosque and the Shrine of Mian Umar Sahib of Chamkani,with a view to spend the income on Islamic education, while the fifth demand was to start Pushto language as the medium of instruction in the schools. He said that the Government should accept the above mentioned demands or the members of the Afghan Jirge would be prepared to sacrifice their all to achieving that end.
Qahri Fida Hussain expressed hope that the present Ministry would grant these demands.
Haji Mohd Amin supported Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan and added that the Ministry should also stop the dencing of girls from the Punjab and also the dancing of the boys.
A meeting attended by about 300 porsons was held under the suspices of the Afghan Jirga at the Hastings' emorial,Peshawar City, on night of 9/10.7.43 from 2245 hours to 0015 hours. Mian Abdullah Shah of Mahzare presided.

Afghan Jirga.-At a meeting of the Afghan Jirga which was held in a mosque in Peshawar City on the 8th of October, demands were made for the abolition of prostitution in cities in this Province, the establishment of a system of Qazis, Pushto as a medium of educa- tion, the prohibition of the sale of Islamic books by non-Muslims and 'Auqaf' property to be under the management of Ulemas. Khan Mir Hilali (paragraph 73) referred to law and order in Charsadda and blamed the Police and Govern- ment for the present state of lawlessness. Fida Hussain (paragraph Nil) requested Govern- ment to remove the ban on the export of 'gur' from this Province, whilst Mian Abdullah Shah (paragraph 174) discussed the scarcity of small coin and the difficulties they were experiencing over the shortage of wheat and sugar.

EXPLOITING MUSLIMS
IN NAME OF ISLAM
FRONTIER MINISTRY
SPEECHES AT AFGHAN JIRCA
Jama! N.W.F.P.
PESHAWAR, April 23.-Speaking at a public meeting of the Afghan J.rga, held in Peshawar City, Khan Khan ex-President of the Provincial Muslim League, strongly criticised the present Musim League M.nistry
for
also
if
the
Condemning the present control system he said that the present M n.s- try was exploiting the ignorant Mus- lims in the name of the Muslim League and Islam. Khan Samin Jan Khan, Education Minister, who spoke held out great hopes the Afghan nat.on it stuck to the programme of Afghan Jirga. Reviewing the past history of the Afghan nation he said: "The country from Tibbet to Arabian Sea belongs to Afghans, the history of which reveals the true na- tional life of the Pathans, and it has been gradually spoiled by the defeats at the hands of S.khs and the Bri- tish.
the
Concluding he remarked that the time was not far off when all the po- litical organizations would either merge in the Afghan Jirga or follow the programme of the Afghan Jirga for its nom.nal separate existence.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor, ex-Parlia- mentary Secretary in a sho:t s, eech blamed the Ministry for nepot sm and prayed that sooner the M.n stry was finished the better it would be for the poor people. The people of the Frontier', he sa d, "are not Indians but belong to that great Afghan tion of about 'three crores who scattered between the borders Persia and the river Indus."'
naare
of
foCondemning the policy of all reigners who had come to the N.W.F.P. but would always invest their money outside the Province, he stated: "We have been always misled by other Provinces in the hope that they will put us on the right path. It was our mistake and now it is our duty to organize this huge Afghan na- tion under a new order of unity and brotherhood."
A resolution was passed urging the Government to prohibit prostitution in the N.-W.F.P. by a definite legisla- tion, and to prohibit non-Muslims from selling Islamic literature. It also demanded the appointment of specia! Qazis to administer the Islamic Law in the courts of the Frontier Pro- vince.-United Press.

The attempts to destroy the greatness and glory of the Islamic Caliphate are being thwarted. Fight these attempts in a fair manner and completely from the Messenger of Allah, and Allah is the Enlightener, and even though there are many disbelievers, the Light of Truth will not be understood by the soul! What should be done?
This question has been decided by the representatives of the Muslims of India at the conference in Dubai. And Mahatma Gandhi and other Hindu leaders have promised to support and endorse this decision. Statement Details

خلافت اسلامیہ کی عظمت و قار کوتباہ کرنے کی کوششین بوری بین بجائز طریق پر ان کوششوں کا مقابلہ کرو اور كامل عن رسوله والله متونوره ولوكثرة الكفرون ، نورحق سمجھ نہ سکے گا نفس اعدا سے ! کیا کرنا چاہیے؟

کیا گیا مسلمانوں کے توای وایت کالا اور اب کیا کرتے ہوکرم سے سماج ایمان سلامت ہو تو خدا بھی تمہارا حامی و ناصر ہے!

Jauurosues
Mahatma Gandhi's order
Maulana Abdul Bari's fatwa
and
the decision of the Joint Conference of Hindus and Muslims
is that no Indian should celebrate the reconciliation that will be celebrated on December 13, 14, 15, and 16, 1916.
There is no reconciliation anywhere? Then why should we celebrate the reconciliation?
Is there reconciliation in Asia?
Persia, Egypt, Turkey—these are living proof of reconciliation. All Muslims are saddened by the Khilafat issue. Hindus are also saddened by the Muslims' grief. Where can we see no end in sight, and mourning is observed in the neighborhood?
How can India celebrate reconciliation?
Celebrating reconciliation at this time is a false statement and a papad for Hindus and Muslims.
Dr. M.S. Ansari, Sadar; Lala Shankarlal, Secretary, Home Rule League; Tajuddin, Editor; Taj Jabal Pur; Professor Indra, Editor; Vijay, Secretaries, Opposition Happiness Committee, Delhi

Mahatma Gandhi's order, Maulana Abdul Bari's fatwa
and
the decision of the Joint Conference of Hindus and Muslims
is that no Indian should celebrate the victory of Mulh, which will be celebrated on December 13, 14, 15, and 16, 1616.
There is no peace anywhere!
Then why should we celebrate the victory of Mulh? Is there peace in Asia?
Persia, Egypt, Turkey—these are living proof of peace. All Muslims are saddened by the issue of the Caliphate. Hindus also feel sad at the sorrow of Muslims. Reconciliation is nowhere in sight, and mourning is observed in the neighborhood.
How can India celebrate the joy of reconciliation?
Celebrating the joy of reconciliation is a lie and a sin for Hindus and Muslims at this time.

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p100.text:
NOTE ON RED BHIRT OR RAZAKAAN ORGANIZATION,
There appears to be an iden that the above
organization is in reality a kind of Salvation Army started by Abdul Ghaffar Khan for the religious, moral and social uplift of the Pathan nation. It is true that this is the outward cloak under which the activities of the Society are disguised and colour has been given to this theory by the name "Khuda-i-Khidmatgaran" and by the insistence upon non-violence as the creed of the true volunteer.
It is
also admitted that a large number of recruits to the movement are simple rustics, who believe that the movement is in the main religious and that the Government are attempting to prevent the Pathan from working out his own salvation.
There is, however, ample evidence to show
that the organizers of the movement have had from the start
very different intentions as to the ultimate objects of the Association and it is in my opinion most dangerous to
assume that the real leaders have any intention of making any permanent peace with Government. Their aim is to overwheln and eradicate the present Government altogether within the next few weeks and to establish an entirely independent Pathan State possibly under the suzerainty of
the Amir of Afghanistan. If they fail in that,
they will only make terms on such conditions as will
enable them to renew the struggle at the earliest possible moment. Any negotiations, therefore, with those leaders
can only result in giving them further time and opportunities for pushing forward their revolutionary plans. In order to prove the above assertions I propose to marshal as briefly as possible the main facts regarding the genesis and development of the movement and it will be

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p101.text:
convenient to divide them into three stages :-
(a). before January, 1930,
(b). from January, 1930, to 23rd: April, 1930, when Abdul Ghaffar Khan and his lieutenants were arrested,
(c). from that date up to the present time.
Abdul Ghaffar Khan, the founder of the movement, has for the last 10 or 15 years been mainly distinguished for his bitter hostility to Government. During the Rowlatt Bill Agitation in 1919 and the Afghan War his village Utmanzai was the only rural focus of political agitation and he was the leader of the whole trouble. He I was arrested and imprisoned for three years for sedition and immediately after his release he founded the so-called "Azad" or "Free School" at Utmanzai in opposition to the Government School. The "Azad" School has since been frequently reported as a centre for the dissemination of communistic and seditious doctrines among the youth of Utmanzai and the surrounding villages, but it has not been possible to obtain any sufficient proof to justify Government in closing down what purported to be a harmless experiment in indigenous education. The pupils after completing their course were not qualified for Government or professional employment and being thoroughly imbued with the founder's seditious sentiments are ideal instruments for disseminating his subversive propaganda. Abdul Ghaffar also founded a paper known as the "Pukhtun", which never lost an opportunity of bringing the Local Administration and their policy into disrepute. This stag of preparation lasted for several years and it was not until the political atmosphere in India favoured a definite advance that a forward move was made. In 1929
Spermbes 1979
e

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p102.text:
Thare not ble to but it fe on recad J.B.
Am
about the same time that the "Naujawan Bharat Sabha" came into being he founded the "Frontier Youth League", A copy of his manifesto with regard to this League is
attached and it leaves no doubt as to the definitely During the
seditious aims of the organization.
disturbances in Afghanistan, which led to the downfall of Amanullah Khan, he continually attacked the Government for alleged complicity with the Amir's tribal enemies and posed as the champion of the Afghan nation against the perfidious British, who were seeking to destroy Islam. About that time the Deputy Commissioner, who was in close touch with Doctor Khan Sahib, brother of Abdul Ghaffar, in connection with the abortive Afghan Medical Mission, suggested to Abdul Ghaffar through his brother that a personal interview might dispel misunderstandings, if it was really the case that his activities were merely pro- Pathan and not anti-Government. The suggestion was declined on the ground that Abdul Ghaffar could not visit, a Government official without losing the confidence of his followers. The above facts are, I think, sufficient,
to show that Abdul Ghaffar has for many years been the declared enemy of Government, and it is reasonable to assume that in his later activities he has undergone no real change of heart.
The second stage commenced from the Congress 1929 Meeting at Lahore at the end of December, which decided to work for complete independence. The meeting was attended by Abdul Ghaffar Khan and several of his most prominent advisers and there can be no reasonable doubt, that a complete campaign was there worked out for starting an agrarian revolt in the Charsadda and Mardan Sub-Divisions of this District, which would synchronise
t
a

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p104.text:
with the congress compaign of Civil Disobedience in India. The ground had already been prepared and owing to the increase in revenue at the recent Settlement, which was followed by a succession of bad harvests, the atmosphere was favourable for anti-Government propaganda. The method adopted was regular touring by Abdul Ghaffar himself throughout these Sub-Divisions and the holding of public and private meetings in the villages, at which the people were urged to form village committees for the settlement of disputes and the presentation of demands to Government with regard to their taxation and other grievances. At the same time the younger men, including in most cases the village bad-characters, were persuaded to become volunteers and to wear red shirts, their nominal duties being to assist the village councils in carrying out their decrees. The organization war difficult to take exception to at this stage, although it was obviously modelled on the Bolshevik Soviet system and was known to be in strict accordance with instructions received from the Bolsheviks themselves. Throughout this period Abdul Ghaffar remained in very close touch with the Congress and Khilafat leaders in Peshawar City. He toured the Province with them on at least two occasions and made seditious speeches both at Bannu and
less
Tank, copies attached, where he was much careful as to what he said than in his own District. No decisions as to action for forwarding the campaign of Civil Disobedience in Peshawar City were taken without his advice. The period eclosed with a very large gathering at Utmanzai noninally to advertise the "Azad" School. To it were invited all the most seditious agitators not only from Peshawar City and District but also from Kohat and Bannu.
Government
officers of gazetted rank who were sent to attend the
meeting in an official capacity were treated with very

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p105.text:
V
scant courtesy and were made to sit on the ground among
There
the rank and file while the platform was occupied by person distinguished only for their virulence against Government. Five hundred Gandhi cape were ordered from Amritsar and distributed among the guests. The general tone of the reported speeches was comparatively mild but this modera- tion was amply compensated by the revolutionary poems which were recited. There was one poem calling upon the young men to come out and fight the war of independence, which has become the favourite song of the red-shirts. were also theatrical performances showing the alleged methods adopted by Government servants in wringing revenue from the destitute zamindar. Both these and the poems were obviously designed to inflame an ignorant agricultural population against the Government and were far better calculated to do so than the delivery of political speeches 4. The last period opened with the arrest of Abdul Ghaffar Khan and three others on the 23rd: April. A very large crowd collected at Charsadda Tahsil and an outbreak of open violence was only prevented by the intervention of Abdul Ghaffar himself, who impressed upon his followers the necessity for observing the doctrine of non-violence, which was the Congress creed. The arrival of a Cavalry Regiment to escort the prisoners to Risalpur also steadied the situation. The places of the arrested persons were, however, immediately taken by other persons obviously designated beforehand and an intensive campaign was started for the enlistment of more volunteers not only in Charsadda and Mardan but also in parts of the Nowshera Tehsil. The results of the campaign are that practically no revenue or other Government dues have been paid in to the Treasury since the 1st: of May. The Police have been

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p106.text:
L
unable to function and all crimir cases have been concealed and compronteed or punished by the Village Committees with the aid of the Volunteers. A11 respectable persons have been terrorized either by violence or by the threat of religious and social boycott into joining the movement or refraining from opposition to it. The most minister development was the arrival of the Haji of Turanezai, who is connected with Abdul Ghaffar by marriage, with a small "lashkar" on the borders of the District, where he has been in constant communication with the "red-shirts" and has been supplied by them with food and information. This is proved by the fact that many "rod-shirts" have been seen by aeroplanes on the other side of the Administrative border. Meeting have constantly been held in open defiance of the Seditious Meetings Act not only in Charsadda and Marden but in Nowshera and the northern part of the Peshawar Tehsil. Continual damage to Government property has been done by cutting telegraphs and telephone wires and even trees have been felled across the road to prevent movement of Government forces. Meanwhile Government roads North of the Kabul River have been infested with large processions of "red-shirts" many armed with sticks and axes and carrying standards. British officers passing these processions have invariably been insulted and threatened and even stoned. A typical report from the Commandant, Frontier Constabulary, 11. W. F. P., is attached for information. Until the middle of May pre-occupations in Peshawar City and on the Mohmand and Afridi borders made it impossible to spare the forces necessary to deal with the situation in Charsadda. Some Cevelry were then sent in support of Civil Police and two villages were rounded

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p107.text:
t
up and some arrests made. On both occasione anti- Government, demonstrations were made by Volunteers, which resulted in a clash with the Police but no loss of life occurred. Attempte were then made to effect a settlemen with the leaders of the movement by removing mutual misunderstandings and co-operating in restoring normal relations between the Government and the people. The lenders professed no hostility to Government and were asked to prove the fact by stopping the volunteers from holding meetings or processions, from picketting Govern- ment buildings and from shouting revolutionary cries.
a
They pressed for the withdray of troops and for the release of their leaders from Jail. The latter request was uncompromisingly refused but troops were withdrawn with the result that an even more intensive campaign of meetings, processions and general spread of disorder was at once started. This culminated on the 25th: of May in the brutal murder of a European Police officer by a mob le of red-chirts near Mardan. The village mainly responsib for this was rounded up by Civil forces with troops in support two days later and several arrests made. The troops were fired on by the surrounding villages and were compelled to fire back causing several casualties. Since then excited meetings have been held at which a large proportion of the persons present have been armed with fire-arms or other lethal weapons. A threat was made to burn down a Government Canal Bungalow and it was freely stated that large trans-border armies were on thei way with arms to join the villagers in attacking neighbouring cantonments.
At a meeting held on the
29th: on the banks of the Lower Swat Canal near the
r
г
boundary between the Mardan and Charsadda Tahsils several

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p108.text:
other The Livo
shots were fired at a reconnoitring seroplane. These events make it sufficiently clear that the movement hag now passed the non-violent stage. It is impossible to believe that any person, himself a Pathan, could anticipat that violence would not result from a mass movement of this kind. The obvious conclusion is that the founder and his assistants deliberately intended in consultation and co-operation with the Congress to produce by non- violent and religious incitement of the ignorant rustic state of affairs, in which Government must either passivel submit to the usurpation of all their functions or use force to suppress the movement. In either case violent insurrection assisted by the invasion of trans-border tribes was the inevitable result. It may be noted that the whole policy is still being directed from Utmanzai and Charsadda, which are in almost daily communication with their leaders in the Gujrat Jail. Messages of encouragement and sympathy are being received from Abdul Chaffar Khan and only a few days ago the whole Charsadda Sub-Division was flooded with a proclamation from Abdul
points I have Chaffar advocating ovation resignation of not been able all village officers, beatinineon's to track, though

In conclusion I may state the main points
which it is sought to prove in this note -
(a). That the movement aims at political revolution
and not at religious or social reformation.
(b). That it is a branch of Congress
activities and is directed and probably financed by the Congress leaders.

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p109.text:
(c). That its method and organization bear a
striking resemblance to those adopted by the
Bolsheviks and advocated by them for use elsewhere.
(d). That any attempts at negotiation with the will
leaders merely result in more intensive propaganda and disorder.
(e). That whatever policy is adopted the movement is bound to end in violence, which can be met only with force.
(1). That steady and consistent pressure with the whole of our available forces will probably reduce materially the amount of violence and bloodshed, which will have to be used in suppressing the
movement.
30.5.30.
Munhälle
DC Peshawar

khudai_khidmatgar_1946_p112.text:
Abdul Ghaffer Khan of Utmanzai arrived at Jannu on
the 27th March 1930. At a Congress meeting held on the oth March 1930 at Chauk Kalan under the presidency of Jhelum Hag Nawaz, Ablul Ghaffar Khan made a speech in Pushtu before an audience nubering about 3000 men. Abau chaffer Knan spoke na under:.
S
People often ask me to what party I belong. In my opinion there are no parties in India, and, even if there are any parties, I belong to the one which intends to free the country from the clutches of the tyrant English people, who have not only ruined India but almost the whole Islamic world, who are responsible for the destruction of Afghanistan, and whose hand are still red with the blood of innocent Afghan martyrs. If an one opposes my party, then let him suggest another one, which may point out to me some way whereby I can release not only Islam but the people of the whole world from the clutches of the cruel-hearted English nation. Oh! Pathan brothers! what has happened to you? Your brethren and neighbours, the Wazirs, who live only 10 miles away, have shed their blood in guarding their bare hills from foreign interference, yet you can do nothing for your fertile country. You should learn a lesson. There is no better 'Jihad', from the point of view of Islam, than to free your own country from slavery and a foreign yoke" The speech was concluded by an appeal to the audience to take a practical share in the present movement and to free the country from the tyrant rulers.

The following is the message received from Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan for publication:-
In the presence of God I assert that knowing the poverty and helplessness of in thans I came in the field, and thought it my duty to try for the betterment of is then community. I thought it necessary to serve my nation to please God and His Prophet (may peace be upon him). In my opinion it is the principle of lelam to regard all human beings as brothers.
Brethren the first thing I did for you was to create love among Afghan people so that they may serve one another at the time of affliction. It was not my intention to put any body to trouble, or to be instrumental in creating disturbance. If other nations of the world have got a right.. to live with ease and comfort, why then we Afghans are deprived of it. And if we are given the above privilege?I don't think it will harm anybody. But sorry we have been blamed for nothing nd sent to jail and it was for your sake only that we have accepted all this from the core of our hearts. We would not hesitate to sacrifice our lives and wealth for the service of the nation, but the news that you left serving your nation will grieve us much. You ought to know that you are doing God's work and refrain from doing such work which will put you to shame and disgrace in the presence of God. Now is the time for you to show your worth. Do not let your enemy laught at you, be industrious and create a spirit of self sacrifice. Do not fear anybody but God. Resignation from your posts of Lambardaris and Chaukidaris will show your sympathy towards Afghans and will be a source of pleasure to us.
At the end we appeal to the Frontier people and
specially the Afghan community to use 'khaddar'

The Khudai Khidmatgars' Association mentioned in the intercepted letter refers to the Redshirt Organisation in this Province. This was started by Abdul Ghaffar Khan of Utmanzai in 1929-30. It is closely allied to the Congress organisation, but retains its separate character and distinctive red uniform and is predominently Muslim organisation. The name Khudad Khidmatgars or servants of God was originally given to it by the organisers inorder to give it a religious tinge.
Agstt. to the Inspector-General of Police, C.I.D., N.W.F.Province.
