Peshawar District,
The
athi Khel Day' was celebrated by the red-shirts and congress volunteers in Peshawar City on 24-8-31. About 500-600 Red-shirts collected in the radgar Chowk at about 5 p.m. they formed a procession at about 6 p.m. and marched through the city bazare under the leadership of Abdul Aziz, 'General' of the city Red-shirts. About 4 or 5 women also joined them. On their reaching near the congress office, 6 about 25-30 volunteers of the city Congress Committee also joined them. The combined procession then marched towards the Qissa Khani Bazar. Ilahi Bakhsh, Secretary of the cong- ress Volunteer Corps, Peshawar, proclaimed en-route that the Tyrant Government had, on 24-8-30, fired at the people who were holding a meeting at Hathi Khel, Bannu and that Some killed of them were and wounded, and the others were arrested and sent to jail. This procession has been taken out in the memory of those Martyrs'.
The processionists raised the cries of Long Live Revo- lution", "Long Live Abdul Ghaffar Khan", "Islam Burri" "Hindustan
Sarhad Azad", "The faces of the informers be blackened", etc.
As the procession passed through the Jahangirpura Bazar some of the mob pointing to S.I. Kartar Singh of the Special Branch remarked:-"There goes the dog of the Government May God ruin these 'toadies'"
The procession after parading the Jahangirpura, old Egerton Hospital, misgaran, Bazazan, Chowk Yadgar, Karimpura, Machhi Hatta and Chirwakoban Bazars stopped before the dis- pensary of ur: Khan Sahib to salute him, and then dispersed. the different parties of the red-shirts and congress volun- teere left for their respective localities.

The processionists, who numbered about 250-300 were led by their 'Commander Abdul Aziz and other red-shirt officers like Shirin, Khan Muhammad, valel Khan, Sahib Gul, etc. They were carrying a National Flag' About 15-16 women also joined the procession, Sharif Khan a 'Gariban' proclaimed en-route that the Red-shirts of Peshawar City congratulated Arbab Muhammad Asiam, editor of the 'Angar', Peshawar, and Sant Ram, manager of the Lakhst ed mi Press, Peshawar, on their arrests, and look/with hatred the action taken by the Government against them.

1931.
Special Diary,
D/- 4-9-31.
Peshawar District. 37
The New National Flag' hoisting ceremony was performed in Peshawar City on 30-9-31.
A pole with the National Flag' wrapped to one of its ends was fixed on the platform of the Hasting's Memorial on the night between 29/30 and 2 Red-shirts were posted to guard it during the night.
The next morning about 400-500 volunteers (Red-shirt) from the City and the villages, about 20-25 Congress volun- teers, about 15-20 volunteers of the vir Bharat Sabha, Peshawar Cantt., and about 10-15 Muslim women collected in the Yadgar Chowk. There were also about 700-800 sightseers. They Red-shirts played their band, and raiz-Ullah Khan - 'Salar' of Utmanzai unfirled the National Flag' at a bout
8 a.m. The following prominent red-shirts were also present,
1. raiz-Ullah Khan, 'Salar of Utmanzai,
2. Abdul Aziz Khan of Landi.
3. Abdul Aziz 'G.I.C.' of the City Red-shirts.
4. Taj Muhammad Khan of Tahkal.
5. Abdul Ahad Khan of Tukhtabad.
E
.6
Ten crackers were exploded on the arrival of Faiz-Ullah
on
Khan khan at Yadgar and the hoisting of the National Flag. None of the prominent Congress leaders was present.
After this one of the Red-shirts read out a Pushto poem on the National Flag'.
Faiz-Ullah Khan delivered a speech in rushto which concluded with a prayer for the glory of the National Flag'. cries were then raised of 'Long Live Revolution', Islam Burri', 'Allah-o-Akbar', 'Long Live Abdul Ghaffar Khan', 'Long Live Abdul Ghafur khan', 'Ruin to the inform- ers', etc. were raised during the progress of the proceed- ings. The flag was then taken away, and a procession of some 200-300 Red-shirts went round the City Bazars raising
the usual revolutionary cries.

In the mosque of sohabat Khan, after the Jumu prayers on 4-3-31, one Janadar, a tabuli of Jahangirpura Bazar, Peshawar Clay (who gooms to be of unsound mind) preyed for the success of ex-Aman-Ullah Khan and the ruin of his enemies. ic also prayed for the success of Malik Zamin Khan and the Afridis and the ruin of the informers. He als asked people not to pay the revenue, and prayed for the ruin of the British Government as it was practicing oppres sion.
In the mosque of Qasim Ali Khan, a Sayyid of Gadi Khan: Peshawar city, said that the municipal committee had demolished a mosque outside the Lahori gate.
Note.- No mosque was demolished outside the Lohori gate. About 5 or 6 months ago the municipal Committee levelled to the ground two platforms which had been constructed temporarily for say- ing prayers.
2. The Khilafat Committee, Peshawar, took out a proces- sion at about 5-30 p.m. on 4-9-31. There were about 30-35 volunteers of the Khilafat Committee under the command of Sheikh Nur Muhamad. They sang khilaft sorge and 50% raised the cries of 'Allsh-o-Akbar', 'Long Live Revolution' The procession passed twice through the city brothel and terminated at the Khilafat office at about 7-30 p.m.

Police Department.
Confidential.
1931.
Special Diary, D/-5-7-31.
Peshawar District.
The local Khilafatists held a meeting in the Shahi Bagh on 3-7-31, at about 6 p.m. The object was to protest against the behaviour of the Principal of the Moghalpura College, Lahore, towards his Muslim pupils, and to enlist volunteers for picketting the said college. 20 volunteers were enlisted for the purpose, but on receipt of a telegram from Lahore that the Government had acceded to the demands of the Muslims, their despatch to Lahore was post- poned.
As the above meeting was in progress some mischevious persons began to molest the police reporter by poking him intthe back with their sticks. Their intention was to divert his attention from the speeches and snatch away his note- book. As the meeting dispersed he was mobbed by a crowd of ruffians who slapped on his back and shouted abuse on him. On this a party of the Khilafat volunteers came up and made aircle around the reporter and other S.B. men. They were thecorted to the Grand Trunk Road. The mob follow- ed them. They took another route and succeeded in entering City in the Hashtnagari gate. The situation had me dangerous but thanks to the timely help of the Khilafatists that an untoward incident did not happen. It is believed that the ruffians were sent by the Congress Committee to spoil the Khilafat meeting.
1. D..G., C.I.D.
2. D.C., Peshawar.
Lohmand Si
Confidential Clerk,

Taj Muhammad Khan of Tahkal proposed the name of Chan Shah of Moti mohalla for the chair, who took the chair and the actual proceedings of the meetings started. Abdul Azáz Khan of Landi put forward the following resolution:-
"This meeting of the Red-shirts of Peshawar expresses its indigination on Government's action in ordering the Mohmand and Ningrahari residents of Peshawar City, either to furnish security or to quit the British Territory. This is an oppressive act of the Government as none of these people have committed any offence and the gathering wants to let the Government know that if it continues its oppressive policy, they would be obliged to look after them- selves
Taj Muhammad then got up to support the above resolu- tion and delivered a speech in Pushto saying that their T.T. brethren who were residing in Peshawar City, had been ordered under Section 36-F. C.R., either to give security or to quit the British territory. The Government should know that these people were the residents of British Territory as they were living here from about 20 to 40 years. If the Government wanted to turn them out under Section 36 F.C.R., then they (the Red-shirts) would announce that Sec:36-F.C.R. was applicable on the Govt. also and that it should quit their country. These Moh- mands etc., were living in a place which was very near their country, and were connected by relations with the residents of the British Territory of all communities. Section 36-1.C.R. was more applicable to the British who were the residents of a country thousands of miles away. They had no right to live here. Their brethren (the Mohmands and the Ningraharis) had committed no offence to deserve punishment under the said Regulation. Their only offence was that they were Khudai Khidmatgars

congratulated. They (the Afridis), it was further said, had taken revenge of the 'Martyrs'. May the British be ruined. The 'Kafirs' had been conquered in the Khajuri plain. Heaven was invoked to put the Britsh into the Hell so that the Afridis might live their (in Khajuri).
Shouts of Amin' were raised by the auduence in in- tervals during the recitation of this poem.
Наше
www.an, a Red-shirt residence etc., not known) read out a Pushto poem in which the King "cunning - and vile" British was asked to go away to London, as the Frontier was theirs (the Pathans). He read out another poem in which it was prayed that the house of the in- formers be ruined and they might get the Kingdom again with Abdul Ghaffar Khan as their King.
S
During the recitation of this poem, the Red-shirts and the audience abused the informers using extremely indecent and obscene language.
Pir Bakhsh, pleader, General Secretary of the Provincial Congress Committee, Peshawar, delivered a speech saying that the Government had adopted an indis- ing
creet policy of send- to the prison, national workers like Maulvi Abdul Rahim, Abdul Ghafur Atish and Chazi Abdul Ghafur of Tehkal. They had started to work for the Nation the day on which the Government had started oppressing the people. They (the people) entertained grievances against a Government who claimed to be the champion of civilisation. After the Deb agreement they had been strictly following the terms of the truce and would continue doing so until they received different orders from their Hd. Qrs. The Government was violating the terms of the truce by molesting them in different ways, and was provoking people into breaking the terms of the truce. The people should rise on one vioce. Their Prophet used to respect his promises. He (Pir Bakhsh)
and Abdul

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p064.text:
(3)
and Abdul Rab Mahtar had seen Maulvi Abdul Rahim wear- ing fetters like dacolte. Abdul Ghafur 'Atish'
ofne
and Rahim Bakhsh 'Ghaznavi were being treated similarly. He would ask the agente of the Government to explain how far they had violated the terms of the truce, and would show to this tyrant Government that the people ofthe Frontier were sticking to their promise and would remain so as long as their leaders wished. In case of opposition they would stretch their chests before the shots of the Government and bow their heads to the lathis. During the had been last struggle the women disgraced but they continued working with patience. The men should continue working
with firmness. Let the Government know that it can
oppress them in any way it likes and that they would face it with patience and stick to their principle of Non- violence. The Government could face Guns etc., could not face Patience. They would be successful if they acted upon these principles. He then put forward the
following resolution:-
but 1
"This meeting of the public of Peshawar protests again the indiscreet and despotic policy the Government has adopted and under which it has arrested Maulvi Abdul Rahim, Rahim Bakhsh 'Ghaznavi', Abdul Ghafur 'Atish', Muhammad Ramzan Khan, pleader of Dera Ismail Khan and other national workers of the Frontier Pro- vince. In the opinion of the public this act of the Frontier Government is a clear violation of the Gandhi-Irwin truce and the officials of the Frontier Government will be responsible for the consequences HL of their actions."
Addressing the Police reporters he said that they should take to their masters his message that the who fell Jrill en
said a
es ved
responsiblity (of disturbances ?) X on the ers as they were not acting on the terms of th If the Government wanted people to remain peacefu should stop oppression and come to an understanding the. The Government might use its power but they would stick to the terms of the truce.
Ali Gul Khan, vice President, Provincial Congress Committee, Peshawar, delivered a speech in Pushto in

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p065.text:
(4)
in support of what Pir Bakhsh had said. Further he @dvoce! advocated the use of country made cloth and took from the audience a promise that they would wear Khaddal
clothes only. He remarked that the British who had come from a distant country were enjoying their wealth while they themselves were dying of hunger. He asked the audi- ence to love their country and in this connection he re- lated the story of a Japanese woman who had committed had sucide as her son/failed to join the war against Russia as he was reluctant to leave his old mother behind. He concluded his speech saying that they could free their country by using country made cloth only.
The resolution put forward by Fir Bakhsh was then passed unanimously.
One Baz Muhammad of Charsadda, read out a Pushto poem in which it was wished that the tyrant should leave their country, and the Pathans should wear red clothes and attain freedon for their country. They did not fear from the Guns etc. and would sacrifice themselves for their country.
He read out another poem saying that the young Red-shirts had come forward for the liberation of their country. All should wear Red clothes and boycott foreign made cloth. The informer had spoiled his life in both the worlds for a cup of tea from the British. On this the usual cries were raised and some of the people shouted that the fathers, and the children of the inform ers should die. The audience raised shouts of Amin'.
Gul Rahman, a Red-shirt boy of Peshawar City read out a Pushto poem in praise of Abdul Ghaffar Khan and asking the informers to take down what he said to take it to their fathers (the British).
A youngman read out a Pushto poem in which it was said that shirin was their Commander, and they were ready to sacrifice their lives for Islem. They should
E

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p066.text:
(5)
liberate themselves from the Englishmen who were enemies of theirs and their religion. They were also the enemies of Abdul Ghaffar Khan who was thei king. The bodies" should be ruined. They (the British) should leave their country and go away.
Another young man (name not known), read out a Pushto poem saying that the Government should go away as they did not like it. Abdul Ghaffar Khan would be their king and the English would be ruined.
At this shouts were raised that the informers should be destroyed, their children may die, their wives be disgraced and molested, etc., Other extremely obscene remarks were also made against the informers. The audience shouted 'Amin - Amin' at intervals.
Taj Muhammad of Tahkal then got up and delivered a speech in Pushto saying that the only way to succes was that of Unity. The British, who had come here from a country thousands of miles away, were ruling over - them through their unity. They were practicing oppres- sion over the Indians as they latter were not united. They would never be successful as long as they were disunited. God had made his creation in different communities for distinction and not for disunion. They could never liberate themselves from their enemies as long as they were disunited. They should promote unity amongst themselves and free themselves from the thief, and tyrant Government. They could not be successful as long as these mischief makers were present here. EXXX Pointing to the National Flag, which had been fixed near the stage, he remarked that some people said that the flag belonged to the Hindus, but, he observed, it were the Muslims only who were present when it was hoisted and that no Hindu was present their. Some peopl crticised Mahatana Gandhi as he was a Hindu but would they drink wine if Mahatama Gandhi prohibited them to

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p067.text:
(6)
จ
do so. Some Muslims raised an objection that the Hindus of Tahkal raised shouts of Islam Burri!
The usual cries were raised with the addition of "Lal Badshah Zinda Bad". Some of the audience shouted that the informers should be disgraced and their faces blackened. On this all said 'Amin'.
The speaker continuing his speech explained the three colours in the National Flag and said that they could know no peace without attaining their rights. They were abiding by the terms of the truce, but the Government was not. The Afridis and the Mohmands were ordered to leave the British territory. This was the reward for their having shed their blood for the British in the great war. The licenses for a gun and a psitol of one of their men had be cancelled as he was a Khudai Khid- matgar. They demanded their rights according to the promises which had been made to them, but were, instead, ordered to leave the country. It was a general rule that r the Badmashes shed the blood of others and similarly the Government shed their blood. They had worn coloured clothes and still the Government pretends to be deaf. They should keep working with patience. The G Government had no regard for the blood they had shed for it. They should not fear from Government but should always re- member that they were bound by an agreement. All of them should unite and turn out their enemy out of their country. Pointing to the Charkha on the flag he re- marked that it would hurled the British away at a had been distance. The condition of the Indians w far better before the advent of the British.
Abdul Karim, 'Captain' of the Red-shirts of Moti mohalla, an ex-Hd: Constable of Bolice kept shouting, and
throughout the meeting, KLIK the audience followed him. The meeting ended at about 10-45 p.m.

Bakhshi, a relative of Pt: Jowahir Lal, Nehru, arrived in the city from the cantts., at about 9-15 a.m. on 15-9-31, and went to the dispensary of Dr: Khan Sahib in the Chirwakoban 10% Bazar. The Red-shirts of Dabgari Bazar etc., about 100 in number, also came there to greet him.
Ali Gul Khan, and Hakim Abdul Jalil visited Abdul Ghaffar Khan in Dr: Khan Sahib's dispensary, and held talks with him and his companion R.K. Bakhshi.

He left for nawalpindi by 10-30 p.m. train the same night.

1. The picketting of the brothel by the Khilafat volunteer continues. Some villagers of Sufeid Dheri and Tahkal are helping the Khilafat volunteers in picketting.

It is said that the City Red-shirts have given notice to the President of the Piece Goods Sellers Association, Peshawar City, that they should come to an understanding with the Red-shirts, as the Cloth Merchants of Mardan had done, otherwise the Red-shirts would picket their shops. The President of the said Association is said to have handed this notice over to the Congress Committee who has referred the matter to the All India Congress Committee 5- and has, for the present, withdrawn its pickets from the foreign cloth shops. The cloth merchants have agreed to act upon the decision of the All India Congress Committee in this matter. The shops of Lakhmi chand Sain Dass of Rem Chowk and those in Katra Khalsa Bros:, who have not yet given their approval of this proposal, are still being picketted by the Congress volunteers.

Bawa Siri Chand Patti, for having insulted their Guru, In- dar Singh.

Emerson said he (Abdul Ghaffar Khan) wished to overawe the authorities, and that he did not know how powerful the covernment was. The speaker said that he was not a diplomat and that he was a straightforward rathan. His object was to demand his rights and not to bring pressure on the authorities ! Mr. Emerson then told him to keep quiet till the Round Table Conference was over to which he replied that the Government too should change its policy and stop molesting the Khudai Khidmatgars for the present. A struggle was going on between the people and the Government, and the time had passed that Xng he (r. Derson) would be lying sick in his bungalow and the people standing outside for hours. 1. Abdul Ghaffar Khan of utmanzai, arrived here from awal- pindi, by the Frontier Mail on the night of 18-9-31, and put up in the bungalow of Dr: Khan Sahib in the Peshawar Cantonment. He paid a visit to the city at 9 a.m. on 19-9-31 and then went back to the cantonments.
on 18-9-31, at about 6 p.m. 3 Red-shirts of Peshawar City were noticed proclaiming the city Bazars, that the cloth-merchants of Peshawar City should come to an under- standing with the Red-shirts as had been done by the cloth- merchants of Mardan, otherwise Red-shirt picketting would be started on their shops.

Iwo Khilafat volunteers who were on duty in the brothel were arrested under section 341-I.F.C. on 18-9-31, on a com- plaint made by one Basheshar Nath, a cloth-merchant of Peshawar city.
The names, etc., of the volunteers are:-
1. Mubarak Shah of Paoka village, B.H.Singh police stn. 2. Umar Bakhsh of Chirwakoban, 'C' Dvn., Peshawar city.

The bales of the foreign cloth were removed from the godown to the Serai of Kirpa кam, outside the Kutchery gate at about 2 p.m. by the owners on which the pickets were withdrawn. The picketting of the octroi post inside the Kutchery gate continues.
5. The Red-shirts of Ghalla mandi held a parade at about 6 p.m. on 18-9-31, under their 'Captain' Lalai. They were 30 in number and wore Red trousers and white shirts while parading. The parade ended at 6-30 p.m.

They also declare that although they have with the Conquess, no objection to the Youth League's working side by side when necessary, they disapprove Abdul Ghaffar Khan's decision of affliating it with that body. in conclusion they hint that if Abdul Ghaffar Khan insisted upon carry- ing out his wishes, he, and not they, will have to resign from the League and join the congress.

The Red Shirts (Hand Cart Drivers) started picketing over the shops of the foreign cloth dealers in Lara Bazazan, Kam Chowk, Ander her, Kariengura, Serais situated outside the Katchery Gate & also in the country liquor shops in the city from 5. 30 a. m. on 20. 9. 31.
Picketing over the cloth dealers lasted till 7. 30 p. m. while on the liquor shops it remained till 9.30 p. m. The important points of the day are :-
Ram Singh wine contractor was detained by the Red shirts & was not allowed to enter his shop situated at Dhaki Dal Jaran. People collected there. When the Red Shirts realized their mistake, they allowed him to enter the shop.
Ram Singh driver after loading his lorry NO: F. P. 6794 with the foreign cloth startedp his lorry to leave for Kabul from the Serai of Pir Rattan Nath situated

alias "Ghazi Ghulam Muhammad of, Ludhiana, made a speach saying that he was originally a Sikh named am Singh and had embraced Islam about 5 years ago. He had disowned his Sikh wife and a son and had re-married in Mansehra through Maulvi Muhammad Ishaq who also instructed him in religion. He had converted 25 others to Islam and had a perfect knowledge of the qoran and the 4 Vedas. About 22 years ago he adopted the Arya faith with a view to put him self in touch with the Vedic teachings. He criticised the "Nayog" system of the Arya, and also the way of worship by the Sanatanists. He also informed the congregation that in a certain village in the Ludhiana District the wikhs did not the Muslims to say 'Azan'. He had had a
fight with the Sikhs of that village in which he was 每

He expressed a wish of making a speech in a place where all the Hindus with their women were present to hear the defects of their religion. He concluded his speach by reading a poem on Islam.
It is said that Ishar Singh, Brij Lal, Niamat Rai, Gokal Chand, Ram he ja and Basant Singh, cloth merchants of Karimpura Bazar, Peshawar, went to Dr: Khan Sahib (who is controlling the picketting operations in the City) on 25-9-31 and arrived at an agreement with him similar to that which had been arrived agreed upon by the retailors.
The picketting on the shops of the cloth merchants of karimpura Bazar was withdrawn at about 1 p.m. on 25-9-31.
The picketting by the red-shirts of the shops of the whole-sale dealers and the liquor shops continues.
It is said that Fundit Jawahir Lal Nehru has wired to Abdul Ghaffar Khan asking him to attend the Political Conference to be held at hang, with Ali Gul Khan, Dr: C.C. Ghosh, Hakim Abdul Jalil, Qasim Jan, and Abdul Rab "Nashtar".
Mian Ahmad Shah, Bar-at-Law, arrived from Kohat on 25-9-31 and left for Charsadda the same evening.

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p088.text:
Situation in Peshawar City.
Since the extension of Red-Shirt movement from rural areas to Peshawar city in June last peaceful situation in the City has been disturbed. Four separate offices of Red- Shirts exist in Peshawar City.
1. In Mohalla Chiryakoban, where Dr.Khan Sahib resides. These Red-Shirts are all Hashtnagri labourerswho are employed for cleaning rice etc.,
2. In Illega Gadi Khana. These Red-Shirts are Ningrari labour employed on cart driving.
3. In Nimak Mandi. Shinwari and other labourers. Employed
on miscellaneous labour.
4. In Moti Mohalla. Mohmand labourers and local men. There number is close upon 1000 and they have ranks divided among themselves. They move about in military and quasi-military formation, dressed in red, and provided with belts and badges. An immédiate result achieved by these Red Shirt Associations has been that a carriage rate for a load from point to point was mised to 4 annas from 6 pies. Then with their campaign of picketting cloth shops they have been able to raise varying amounts of money from dealers. Several cases of interference on their part in the peaceful avocations of the people have been reported but owing to fear of reprisals on their part it has been seldom possible successfully to prosecute then. During the last two months there have been no less than 20 processions taken out by
ers
these men on one pretext or an other. The number of procession- ists is usually very large. Some times it spreads over
thousands, being augmented by Red-Shirt followers from suburbs and from Hashtnagr. During the course of a procession general traffic and the usual business is practically stopped during the time it lasts. Shop-keepers have realy to close their shops through fear. Agitation is kept up by meetings and speeches at intervals.
Rahim Bakhsh Ghaznavi a Congress Agitator is under-

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p089.text:
under-trial for delivering two such speeches in which his addresses were fant found to be distinctly seditious.
A few days ago there was a very large procession in City followed by a meeting in which speakers loosely indulged in their usual clap-trap, over the conviction of a dumy editor of the Red-shirt paper 'Angar'. Again there
was a crowded procession and noisy meeting over the conviction of a number of Red-Shirts who obstructed a Revenue Official on duty collecting revenue.
Besides the difficulties created by the activities
of the Red-Shirts the Khilafatists have started a campaign of picketting on the brothel. They get their picketters from suburben villeges. A party of over 50 men has lately been provided by Mian Jaffar Shah from his out-lying villages, in Charsadda and Nowshera. Two ar to three days ago a number of prostitutes ran in 'A' Division Police Station for refuge. Cases have actually been reported of wrongful restraint in this picketting but it has become difficult to administer justice as people are afraid to give evidence, Abdul Ghaffar Khan saw the Ander Shehr Bazaar closed with his own eyes when he was addressing a meeting in Mohabat Khan Mosque. There was such a large accumulation of men outside the walls of the City on the day of conviction of Red-Shirt Office bearers of Tehkal that the City Gates had to be kept closed while they had enforced compulsory Hartal in the City. Cessation of business is quite usual to occur when Abdul Ghaffar Khan called Badshah Khan or any of his leading followers amongst whom are Generals and Commanders walk in procession through the City. As people are afraid to give true expression of their fears it is difficult to describe the actual situation and the present or only a few instances of any occasions on which business and peaceful condition in the City has been disturbed.

red_shirt_6_1931_pesh_dist_p092.text:
Translation
LONG LIVE REVOLUTION.
Has come again"
16th: November 1931.
Has come again".
"Shoe to the Gandhi-Irwin Pact.
Since the Gandhi-Irwin Pact has been arrived at, the
Frontier Government has rendered its weapons of repression and violence sharper and more glistening. During the days of the Civil Disobedience one who acted upon it was sent to the jail, but, after the agreement, every one who wel-comed the political leaders on their release from the jails, began to prick in the eyes of the Government, so that, after the (said) agreement, it has thrust into the jail about 300 young men, who have been given more labour than the moral convicts; not only so much, but many were punished with flogging. In the present time of indigence the Government is making people a target (of its high-handedness) like Obeid-Ullah Khan. If the present attitude of Government continued for some days more, then there is no doubt, that Civil Disobedien will be started in the whole of the Province. No warn Govt. to put a stop to the series of repressions and violence, otherwise the self-respecting people of the Frontier will bring it to distress, and will bring about ita ruin!
The Frontier and the 'Gol Mez or chul Mez (Round Table or Filth Table Con)
(ference)
*Comments of different sorts are appearing in the news. papers on the 'Gel Mez or Ghul Mez' Conference. The self- respecting people of the Frontier have decided to secure what Pt. Jowahir Lal had announced in the Lahore Congress, 1. the 'Complete Independence'. If the Government offered (them) half a grain less than the Complete Independence, the 27 lakhs of the residents of the Frontier will re-start the campaign of Civil Disobedience*
*age (Ghazab), M.A., Frontier*.

Confidential weekly Diary of the Senior Supdt: of Police, Peshawar, for the week ending 10th October 1931.
The Special Magistrate, Peshawar City informed me yesterday that crowds of several hundreds, including large number of red shirts, demonstrated outside his court, when he was trying petty cases of street encroachments in which red shirts were concerned. This is serious if correct.
Sa/- R.E.H.Griffith.
D.C.Peshawar.
23-10-31.
General appreciation.
There has been little change in the situation during the week. In the Peshawar City there has been a tendency to lionise the picketers on the brothel and liquor shops who have been proceeded against for obstructions, etc. Meetings have been held and processions arranged in their honour. This largely neutralises the effect of the small punishment it is possible under the existing law to inflict There has been a recrudescence of
picketing on certain whole-sale dealers, who are allaged to have broken their agree ments with the red shirt organisation. The nature of such agreements is now being kept secret from the Police.
no no: 3806/28302. X1. 1931

After the Juma prayer held in the mosque of Mohabat Khap Maulvi Muhammad Yusaf of Bhanamari, delivered & speech dur- ing which he informed the congregation, which numbered abou 600-700 persons, that the attitude of the authorities in
e
regard to the picketing in the brothel had changed and that1. the Deputy Commissioner was thinking of promulgating a new Picketing Ordinance, under which all the picketers of the brothel would be arrested. He then asked the congregation whether they would help in the removal of the curse of the brothel from the City or not. The congregation replied they were quite ready to do this. He then informed them that a meeting of the Jamiat-ul-Ulema-i-Sarhad would be held XX/ on 3-12-31, in which the question of sending Jathas to Kashmir, where their religion was being interferred with, would be discussed. The gathering expressed their approval of the proposal by saying "Amin Amin".

would d this movement, and that he he would g
as they would require..

CIVIL BECRETARIAT
NORTH WEST FRONT IS PROVINCE
NOTIFICATIO
Dated Peshawar, the 15th September, 1948.
No. 37044.
In exercise of the powers conferred under section (1) of the North West Frontier Province Public Safety Ordinance No. VII of 1948, the GOVERNOR N.W.F.P. being satisfied that the organization known as the Red-shirt Organization or 'ZALMAI PAKHTOON has as its aim or object the incitement of people to co mit prejudicial acts,
declares this organization, by whatever name it has been or may be from time to time known or called, as unlauful with immediate effect.
In exercise of the powers conferred under No. 37045 section 10(1) of the North West Frontier Province Public Safety Ordinance No. VII of 1948 the GOVERNOR, N.W.F.P., is pleased to order the forfeiture to Government of the property known as the ASHRAM at SARDARYAB together with the lands attached to it and belonging to the Red-shirt Organization declared to be an unlawful organization under section 5(1) of the Ordinance. Immediate action will be taken under sub-section (5) of Section 10 to seize and take possession of this property.
By order of the Governor N.W.F.P.

tribal_research_cell_1968_p017.text:
A NOTE OR IN ACTIVITIO OF SHINWLY FORMED *KUDAI KHIDMATOAR SPLITTER GROUP OF THE RATIO AL AWAMI PARTY
******
The formation of Khudai Khidmatgar Splinter Group of the N.A.P. was announced in a meeting held at Peshawar on 12.1.1968 by Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkel Bala, Peshawar. The main object of the revival of the Movement is to strengthen friendly relations with Afghanistan, undo One-Unit, restore old provinces, release of political prisoners, repeal of black laws and termination of the state of emergency.
The Khudai Khidmatgar workers are of the view that the N.A.P. organization has failed to achieve the above objectives and therefore it was essential to raise another party to strive for the betterment of Pathans. Since the announcement of the revival of this organization five meetings have been held by the sponsors of the new Group. Proceedings of these meetings are briefly as follows :-
1. On 12.1.68 a meeting was held at the Old Municipal Hall, Peshawar City under the presidentship of Hakim
Muhammad Aslam Sanjari.
Addressing the meeting, Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of Tehkal Bala, threw light on the Knudai Khidmatgar Movement Saying that the Movement was originally started by Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan and Haji Sahib of Turang zai at the time when there was no other political organization in the illaqua. He said that it was because of that stuggle the Britishers were ousted from the country. He added that Khudai Khidmatgar Movement is being revived mainly wing old N.W.F.P. and strengthen with the object of restoring old friendly relations with Afghanistan. He remarked that eturn to the mother land
Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan will return
as soon as the Pathans were restored their rights.
He
disclosed that they would organize the Movement on the old
footings and would spread it even to the
y tribal are
areas.

tribal_research_cell_1968_p023.text:
The Khudai Khidmatgar Shura organised a meeting
(55/60-1.3.68) in Masjid Akhtar Munix in village Regi, Diatt: Peshawar with Jan Mohammad of Regi in the chair.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan and Hakin Mohammad Aslan
Sanjari were the principal speakers at the meeting who advocated the cause of the Movement remarking that they took the intiative of reviving the Movement to combat communism which is
flourishing in the country. They spot lighted the disadvantages of One Unit in the usual way and declared it as detrimental to the interest of Pushtoon.
Resolutions urging the authorities not to deprive Khalil Mohmands of the right of irrig tion from Bara water or otherwise exempt them from the Abiyana of Warsak Canal and pay due attention to the water logging and salanity menace, were adopted at the meeting. Through a separate resolution the Govt. was urged not to acquire the land at Malka Dher for the extension of Peshawar University and if at all it is to be acquired, due compensation he paid to the owners.
Addressing a meeting (200/22.3.1968) at Jobli Park, Namak Mandi, Peshawar City, with Hakim Mohammad Aslam Sanjari in the chair, Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan recounted the services rendered by them for the cause of the country and regretted that the Independence did not solve their difficulties. + According to him, corruption and black marketting is due to the fact that the people at the helm of affairs did not participate in the struggle for Independence. Assailing One Unit he remark that 80 lacs of Pathans are being trampled under the feet of 32 crore Punjabis. He thought that after the promulgation of Martial Law, the Pathans would be given their due rights but he regretted that instead 1500 innocent persons Ex were sent to Jail and their property confiscated. He stated that in case of disintegration of the Province, Abdul Ghaffar Khan will also return to his home land.

tribal_research_cell_1968_p026.text:
NOTE ON THE ACTIVITIES OF KHUDAI KHIDMATGAR SPLINTOR GROUP OF TH H.A.P. IN PESHAWAR DIVISION.
The decision to revive the Khudai Knidmatgar Movement was woken at a messing meld at the old Municipal Hall Pesha ar on the forenoon of January 12, 1968. Tue meeting was presiced over by Hakim Munammad Aslam 3 ajari of Peshawar City and attended uy Aroab Abdul Ghafoor Khan of ehkal Bala and other like-minded people .Later in the day a public meeting (1000/1200) was held at chowk Yadgar Peshawar City with the said Hakim Muhammad Aslam Sanjari in the Chair. The meeting was addressed by Purail Khan of Kohat, Ashiq Hussain Kakezai of Peshawar City, Mr.Sanjari and Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan. Addressing he meeting the last named gave a urier history of the Khudai Khidmatgar Movement and recounted the services rendered by it to the people and the country. Referring o their ureak with ue N.A.P. he gave the reason for it as the fact that NAP people were inclined towards communism and were busy disseminating communist doctrines particularly of the Communist China. He also dwelt úpon the purposes for which the Khudai Khidmatgar Movement was veing revived and said that the Movement was intended to foster unity among Pathans, undo One Unit and establish friendly relations with Afghanistan. Resolutions demanding disintegration of one Unit, release of political prisoners, repeal of Oppresive Laws, termination of the state of emergency and the holding of elections based on adult franchise which nad been passed at the earlier party meeting were also adopted at the Chowk Yadgar meeting.
Two days later Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Kuan entertained the leading workers of the Movement to a lunch at his residence in Ahalil Abad. The occasion was availed of uo discuss ways and means to popularise the Movement.
The Workers of the Movement held two more public meetings one at Lahore village in Merdan District on 25th Jan. 1968 and the other at the Jobli Park Namak Mandi Peshawar City on 22nd March 1958. At both these meetings the speakers dwelt upon the justification for the revival of the Movement, the failure of the NAP to serve the cause of the Pathans and the disadvantages of the formation or one Unit from the stand point of the interests of residents of former WFP.
Since the revival of the Movement the Khudai Khidmatgar Shuara have met uwice au Regi a village in Peshawar Distt: At these meetings they determined that the undoing of One Unit was to be their main oujective. At one of the so meetings two resolutions, one urging the authorities not to deprive Khalil Mohmands, of the right of irrigation from Bara River and the other urging the authorities not to acquire the land at Malka Dher for the expansion of Peshawar University were adopted.
Some of the reports regarding their activities go to show that the Khudai Khidmatgars are trying to wean away some of the workers of the NAP from their party.
Commissioner,
Peshawar Div.Peshawar

The activities of the newly formed "Khudai Khidmatgar" plinter Group of the N.A.P. from 1 to inception till 27.1.68 were reviewed in the note of Mr. Said-ud-Din 3,0. dated 10.2.68 paras 15 to 32. D.I.G (S.B.) has now prepared a no te covering the activities of the Splinter Group up to 223.68. A resume of its activities for the months of February and March(up to 22nd) is given below:-
A meeting of Khudai Khidmatgar Shura WE 8 held on 22.2.68 at the hujra of Akbar Khan of village Regi (Peshawar). Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan Khalil of Khalilabad explained the aims and objects of the movement. He main tained that the main object of the movement was to disintegrate One Unit which was detrimental to the intrests of Push toon. He pointed out that the Punjabis and Bengalis were not treating the Push toon fairly and therefore it was in their interest to sever relation, with the N.A.P. and strengthen the movement. He charged the N.A.P. for pro-Communist views. It was decided to hold ano ther meeting on 1.3.68.
The Khudai Khidma tgar Shura orgajnised a meeting (55/60-1.3.68) in Masjid Akhtar Munir in village Regi, Distt: Peshawar with Jan Mohammad of Regi in the Chair.
Arbab Abdul Ghafoor Khan and Hakim Mohammad Aslam Sanjari were the principal speakers at the meeting who advocated the cause of the Movement remarking that they took the intiative of reviving the Movement
to combat communism which is flourishing in the country. They spot-lighted the disadvantages of One Unit in the usual way and declared it as detrimen tal to the interest of Push toon.
